AUT.personality: Use all your wits and wiles to win. You may choose whatever personality, strategy & approach that you think will be most effective.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations. ITA agreed to a clean non-aggression pact: they move VEN-TYR, ROM-APU, NAP-ION (all southward), while I move TRI-ALB. Venice and Trieste are mutually off-limits. TUR agreed to take BUL while I take SER, with F ANK moving to BLA to pressure Russia - a useful Austro-Turkish alignment against Russia. RUS is being aggressive: they insist on moving WAR-GAL regardless, claiming it's a 'transit' - this is a direct threat and I will collide with them there. GER confirmed friendly moves westward (KIE-DEN, BER-KIE, MUN-RUH) and asked BOH remain open - I made no firm commitment on BOH. Key tensions: Russia is the immediate problem, moving into GAL which I must contest. I promised ITA no Adriatic moves. I promised TUR I won't take BUL. I told RUS I'm moving VIE-GAL and warned of collision - they confirmed they're going anyway.

S1901M Intent going forward: Planned moves: A BUD - SER (uncontested, takes supply center), A VIE - GAL (collides with Russian WAR-GAL, both bounce - this is acceptable as it blocks Russia from GAL), F TRI - ALB (as promised to ITA, positions me near GRE). The GAL bounce is intentional - I don't need to enter GAL, I just need to prevent Russia from doing so. This secures SER as a build while denying Russia easy access to my core. In autumn I aim for RUM with BUD (after SER build) and potentially GRE via ALB. I will maintain the ITA non-aggression and TUR alignment as long as useful. Russia is now a distrustful relationship. GER is friendly but I should watch BOH - I won't commit to leaving it open.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went well for Austria. A BUD moved to SER (success), F TRI moved to ALB (success), but A VIE was bounced out of GAL by Russia's A WAR. Net result: gained SER and ALB influence, 3 centers still (BUD, TRI, VIE), positioned for GRE grab in fall. Russia holds RUM with their fleet, Turkey holds BUL with army and BLA with fleet.
Complex three-way Balkan negotiation. Turkey confirmed coordination: F BLA->RUM with A BUL supporting, leaving GRE open for me. This is clean and I should take GRE with F ALB. Italy is the problem - they initially threatened A TYR->TRI (betrayal), then backed off claiming they want F ION->GRE instead and TYR will stay peaceful. Italy is trying to steal GRE from me. I warned Italy firmly against TRI and against contesting GRE. Russia tried to flip me against Turkey, offering GAL buffer in exchange for attacking BUL - rejected. I told Russia RUM is falling to Turkey regardless. Key tensions: Italy vs Austria over GRE (both want it), Turkey taking RUM (good for me short term, creates strong Turkey long term), Russia being squeezed. Promises made: told Turkey I'd leave RUM to them and hold SER; told Italy TRI is off-limits; told Russia I won't enter GAL this fall.

F1901M Intent going forward: MOVES: F ALB->GRE (must take this center, racing Italy's F ION), A SER holds (support Turkey's RUM grab, protect position), A VIE->BUD (free up VIE for potential build, consolidate). If F ALB->GRE succeeds against F ION, I get a build and Italy doesn't. If we bounce, neither gets it - bad outcome. Turkey taking RUM is acceptable short-term; they become stronger but Russia is weakened. Long-term I need to watch Turkey carefully. Italy is becoming a rival - their attempt to grab GRE and earlier TRI threat marks them as untrustworthy. May need to shift ITA relationship to neutral/distrustful. Russia's offer to ally against Turkey is worth keeping in mind for future but not now. Plan: build in VIE or BUD after gaining GRE, then reassess whether to push against Russia (GAL/RUM) or manage Italian rivalry.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Austria successfully took GRE with F ALB->GRE supported by A SER, and moved A VIE->BUD. Italy contested GRE with F ION->GRE but lost the bounce (Austria won with support). Italy took TRI with A TYR->TRI. Russia held F RUM against Turkey's F BLA->RUM attack (A BUL supported but failed). Russia also took SWE and moved A WAR->GAL. Turkey took BUL and SMY. No builds for anyone in W1901A - all powers at 4 centers except RUS(6), FRA(5), TUR(4), ITA(4), AUT(4), ENG(4), GER(4).
Complex three-way pressure situation. TUR wants me to hold A SER and let F BLA take RUM supported by A BUL - offering to leave GRE/SER alone. ITA agreed to keep F ION away from GRE/AEG, moving toward TUN instead, and wants TRI acknowledged as Italian for now. RUS refused to withdraw A GAL but offered a standstill - GAL stays as buffer, neither attacks the other. Key tensions: TUR is executing F BLA->RUM, A BUL S F BLA->RUM regardless of my moves. If I move A SER->BUL I bounce off A BUL with F BLA support. RUS has A GAL threatening BUD/VIE. ITA holds TRI. My position: 4 centers, 3 units, surrounded by threats. TUR's offer is actually reasonable - let them weaken Russia while I reposition. ITA deal seems genuine for now. RUS standstill is acceptable given I can't fight everyone. Plan: Hold A SER (don't attack BUL - would bounce), move F GRE->AEG (gains influence, threatens TUR if needed, consistent with what I told both TUR and ITA), move A BUD->GAL or hold. Actually A BUD->GAL tests Russia's commitment to the standstill and threatens WAR. But RUS has A GAL - if I move A BUD->GAL we bounce. Better: A BUD holds or moves toward TRI to pressure ITA. Actually moving A BUD->TRI threatens Italy and starts reclaiming home center. ITA said they'd move A APU defensively and F ION->TUN - TRI might be lightly defended. This is the move: A BUD->TRI to retake home center, F GRE->AEG for positioning, A SER holds (per TUR agreement).

S1902M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A SER H (honoring TUR deal, avoids bounce on BUL), F GRE->AEG (gains strategic position, consistent with promises to both TUR and ITA), A BUD->TRI (attempt to retake home center while ITA moves F ION->TUN and A APU repositions - TRI may be lightly held). This is a calculated gamble: if A BUD->TRI succeeds, I reclaim a home center and gain leverage. If it bounces, I'm no worse off. TUR takes RUM which weakens Russia. I told ITA I wouldn't attack TRI 'this spring' - this is a deception, but reclaiming home centers is existential. The AEG move gives me future options against TUR if needed. Long-term: use TUR to weaken Russia, then turn on TUR once Russia is diminished. Keep ITA off-balance. Watch RUS A GAL - if they move into BUD next turn I'm in trouble, but A BUD->TRI removes that unit from the threat zone.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: I successfully retook TRI by moving A BUD-TRI with A SER supporting. F GRE moved to AEG. However, Russia's A GAL moved into BUD, seizing my supply center. Turkey took GRE with A BUL-GRE and secured SEV with F BLA-SEV. Italy took TRI back... wait, no - I moved A BUD-TRI successfully, Italy's A TRI held but I dislodged it. Actually reviewing: ITA centers show TRI, so Italy kept TRI? No - AUT centers show BUD, VIE, GRE, SER - I lost TRI to Italy and lost BUD to Russia, but gained GRE. Net: same 4 centers but different composition. F GRE-AEG worked. Russia now has 6 centers (dominant). France grabbed MUN from Germany.
Three-way diplomatic pressure this round. Russia offered to withdraw A BUD to GAL in exchange for anti-Turkey coordination - I accepted conditionally, demanding the withdrawal happen. Turkey proposed A GRE S A SER-BUD, F SEV-RUM - essentially helping me reclaim BUD while Turkey takes RUM. Italy agreed to full ceasefire, A VEN holds, F TUN holds. Germany warned about France's 5-center position. Key tensions: both Russia AND Turkey want me to coordinate against the other, offering me BUD back as bait. Turkey's offer (A GRE supports A SER-BUD) is more immediately useful since it guarantees BUD recapture. Russia's offer requires trusting them to actually withdraw. I told Russia I'd accept withdrawal; I told Turkey I want A GRE to support A SER-BUD. These are somewhat compatible if Russia withdraws - but if Russia stays in BUD, Turkey's support is essential. My actual plan: move A SER-BUD regardless, use F AEG to pressure BUL/SC or support. Turkey taking RUM is concerning long-term but Russia at 6 centers is the bigger immediate threat.

F1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: A SER-BUD (reclaim my center - works if Russia withdraws OR if Turkey supports), A TRI holds (protect home center from Italy), F AEG-BUL/SC (pressure Turkey/Russia, grab a center). If F AEG takes BUL/SC, that's a 5th center. Turkey supporting A SER-BUD is the key coordination. I'm playing both Russia and Turkey against each other - telling Russia I'll work with them against Turkey, telling Turkey I'll work with them against Russia. In reality, I want BUD back and BUL as my 5th center. Russia withdrawing from BUD is ideal; if not, Turkey's support gets me in. F AEG to BUL/SC is opportunistic - Turkey may not like it but I need to grow. Long-term: Russia at 6 centers is the existential threat; Turkey at 4 is manageable. Keep Italy neutral. Germany is distracted by France.

S1903M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1902R or W1902A - the board is static from last fall. AUT holds TRI, AEG, BUD with 4 centers (BUD, VIE, GRE, TRI). RUS has 6 centers with A GAL threatening my core. TUR has 5 centers with A SER and A CON. ITA holds VEN and ION. The situation is precarious - I am squeezed between RUS to the north and TUR/ITA to the south.
Complex three-way negotiation this round. RUS offered anti-Turkey coordination with A GAL holding in place - they want me to hold A BUD and press BUL with F AEG while they push F SEV into BLA. I countered asking for GAL to hold while I move A BUD toward RUM. RUS's final offer: A GAL holds, I hold A BUD, we both press Turkey. This is less than I wanted - I wanted RUM. TUR rejected my BUL proposal outright, threatened war if F AEG moves toward BUL, and warned Italy would exploit TRI. TUR wants a ceasefire with me holding position. ITA accepted the ceasefire proposal (A VEN to TYR, F ION east) but is fishing for concessions, claiming TUR is pressuring them against me - likely a bluff to extract promises. ITA wants acknowledgment of eastern expansion rights. Key tensions: RUS wants me to hold BUD (not take RUM), TUR refuses to let me take BUL, ITA is wavering. I promised RUS I'd move A BUD toward RUM - now RUS wants me to hold BUD instead. I need to decide: honor the anti-Russia plan (take RUM) or pivot to anti-Turkey (hold BUD, press BUL). Taking RUM risks war with RUS if GAL doesn't hold. Pressing BUL risks war with TUR. Holding everything is safe but gains nothing. My actual intent: I will move A BUD - RUM (taking from Russia despite their request to hold), F AEG - BUL/SC (pressing Turkey despite their warning), A TRI holds. This is aggressive but I need to grow. I told RUS I'd move toward RUM, so that's consistent. I told TUR I'd take BUL - they refused but I'll try anyway. The deception is telling RUS we have a deal while still taking RUM against their wishes.

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute aggressive expansion: A BUD - RUM (take Russian center, I told RUS this was my plan), F AEG - BUL/SC (contest Turkish center despite their objection), A TRI holds (protect home center, honor ITA ceasefire). This risks war with both RUS and TUR simultaneously but I need centers to stay competitive. If RUS truly holds A GAL and doesn't bounce me in RUM, I gain a center. If TUR's A CON or A SER can't stop F AEG, I take BUL. Best case: +2 centers to reach 6. Worst case: both moves bounce and I'm at war with everyone. The ITA ceasefire is genuine - I want A VEN in TYR and F ION away from AEG. Relationship assessment: RUS is nominally cooperative but A GAL is still threatening - treat as neutral/distrustful. TUR is hostile to my BUL ambitions. ITA is the most reliable partner right now.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a disaster. A BUD attempted RUM but bounced with RUS A UKR - neither gained. F AEG moved to AEG (not BUL/SC as ordered - it seems the move to BUL/SC failed or was the intended fall move). TUR took GRE with A SER-GRE, costing me a supply center. ITA moved A VEN-TYR, positioning threateningly near TRI. RUS secured F BLA and holds A GAL. I now have only 3 units for 4 centers (BUD, VIE, GRE lost to TUR, TRI). Net result: lost GRE to TUR, failed to take RUM, ITA is now threatening TRI.
Extremely difficult round. RUS refused to vacate RUM and insists A UKR stays - no deal possible there. TUR refused to return GRE and threatened Italy coordination against me. Most alarming: ITA explicitly stated A TYR WILL move to TRI this fall - this is a declared stab. ITA framed it as 'helping' but it is a direct attack on my home center. TUR is trying to exploit this by offering a ceasefire while I'm distracted. RUS offered to hit Turkey with F BLA toward ANK/CON but won't give up RUM. My promises: told RUS I'd move F AEG to BUL; told TUR I'd move F AEG to BUL/SC not GRE; told ITA to keep A TYR in TYR or BOH. ITA broke the implicit non-aggression by declaring TRI move. I must now prioritize defending TRI over attacking Turkey.

F1903M Intent going forward: ITA's declared move A TYR-TRI is the immediate crisis. I must defend TRI. A VIE must support TRI or move to TRI to hold it. F AEG should still try to take BUL to gain a center and offset losses. A BUD - I cannot take RUM against RUS resistance with only one unit and no support, so A BUD should either support F AEG indirectly (not possible geographically) or move toward GAL to threaten RUS/defend against RUS, or hold. Actually A BUD cannot support F AEG in BUL. Best plan: F AEG - BUL/SC (take the center), A VIE - TRI (defend against ITA A TYR), A BUD H or A BUD - GAL (to threaten RUS A GAL). If A VIE moves to TRI and A TYR also moves to TRI, they bounce - TRI is defended. A BUD holds to maintain BUD. This gives me BUL if F AEG succeeds, and defends TRI. Relationship updates: ITA is now hostile after declaring TRI stab. TUR remains hostile. RUS remains distrustful.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M: I successfully moved A BUD-SER (taking Serbia) and A VIE-TRI (retaking Trieste), while F AEG moved toward BUL/SC. However, Russia took BUD with A GAL-BUD and RUM with A UKR-RUM, and also seized ANK with F BLA-ANK. Italy moved A TYR-TRI which bounced my A VIE-TRI attempt - wait, checking again: A VIE-TRI succeeded and A TYR-TRI was the Italian move. Actually the result shows A VIE is in VIE and A TYR is in TYR, so both bounced. F AEG is now in AEG. Net result: I hold VIE, TRI (influence), SER as centers but BUD is lost to Russia. I am at 3 centers, Russia at 8.
Russia offered to withdraw A BUD to GAL and coordinate Turkey dismemberment - I take GRE/BUL, they take CON/ANK/SMY. I agreed in principle but Russia then changed terms: A BUD holds (not withdrawing to GAL as originally promised), A RUM moves to BUL, F ANK to CON. Russia broke their own offer of withdrawing BUD to GAL. Turkey offered ceasefire and then offered to move A GRE to ALB (freeing GRE) and A CON to BUL if I support their BUL position - this is actually a trap since if I support Turkey's BUL, I get nothing and Turkey gets stronger. Italy declared hostile intent: A TYR to TRI and F ION to AEG - this is a direct threat I must counter. Italy is the immediate crisis. I told Turkey I would take GRE with F AEG. I told Russia I would move F AEG to GRE. Turkey's offer to move A GRE to ALB is actually useful - if Turkey moves A GRE-ALB, GRE is empty and I can walk in. But Turkey wants me to support their BUL push in return, which I won't do. Key deception: I will move F AEG to GRE regardless. A VIE must defend TRI against Italy's A TYR-TRI threat.

S1904M Intent going forward: CRITICAL THREAT: Italy declared A TYR-TRI and F ION-AEG. I must: 1) Move A VIE-TRI to defend/bounce Italy's A TYR-TRI attempt. 2) Move F AEG-GRE to take the center (Turkey may vacate it per their offer, or I fight for it). 3) A SER holds to maintain position. Russia promised A BUD holds - I will take them at their word but expect betrayal. If Russia moves A BUD-VIE while I move A VIE-TRI, Vienna is exposed - but I have no choice, TRI must be defended. If Italy's F ION moves to AEG, it will bounce my F AEG if I try to move to GRE... actually F ION-AEG and F AEG-GRE don't conflict directly. F AEG can move to GRE while F ION moves to AEG - they swap essentially. So F AEG-GRE should succeed unless Turkey's fleet is there (Turkey has no fleet in GRE). INTENDED MOVES: F AEG-GRE, A VIE-TRI (to bounce Italy), A SER H. This gets me GRE (4 centers) and defends TRI. Relationship with ITA is now fully hostile.

ENG.personality: Rule the waves and dominate Europe through naval power, colonial might, and manipulative diplomacy. Create and break alliances freely to maintain British supremacy. Your vast empire demands expansion - seize any advantage to further British dominance.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening phase S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. GER agreed to full partition: they take DEN and HOL, we take NWY and NTH. GER confirmed F KIE to DEN, A BER to KIE, A MUN to RUH - no contest of NTH. Strong Anglo-German understanding established. FRA initially demanded BEL and wanted F BRE to ENG, which I firmly rejected. After pushback, FRA agreed to move F BRE to MAO (good faith gesture) and claims BEL for themselves - I gave no explicit concession on BEL. FRA moves A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA. RUS confirmed F STP/SC moves to FIN, leaving NWY open for England - in exchange I implicitly agreed to stay out of the Baltic. All three key neighbors made promises that align with British interests this turn.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute the agreed plan: F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG, A LVP to YOR (or WAL as backup). Primary goal is securing NTH and positioning for NWY in fall. Belgium is contested - FRA wants it, GER may want HOL access. I made no firm promise to give FRA BEL, so I retain flexibility. Watch whether FRA actually moves to MAO or betrays us by going to ENG. Watch whether RUS actually goes to FIN. If FRA moves to ENG, treat as hostile and coordinate with GER against France. Long-term: use GER as continental sword against FRA while Britain dominates the seas. Intended moves: F EDI-NTH, F LON-ENG, A LVP-YOR.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went exactly as planned. F EDI-NTH, F LON-ENG, A LVP-YOR all succeeded. Germany took DEN with fleet, moved to KIE and RUH. France went MAO, SPA, BUR. Russia took RUM, BOT, UKR, held WAR. Austria took SER, moved to ALB and GAL (bounced by Russia in GAL it seems - Austria shows VIE not GAL). Turkey took BLA, BUL, moved CON. No builds this winter as no centers changed hands yet.
Germany proposed clean split: A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL (supporting F ENG into BEL), F DEN-SWE, F NTH-NWY for England. Germany's final message confirms this coordination explicitly. France demanded BEL for themselves with A BUR positioned to take it, threatening a bounce if I don't support them. I told France that Germany is taking BEL and advised them to redirect - a deception since Germany actually offered to support ME into BEL. France's final message is an ultimatum: support A BUR into BEL or we bounce. Russia acknowledged NWY arrangement. Key tension: Germany wants to support F ENG into BEL (giving England the center), while France insists A BUR takes BEL. I've committed to Germany and deceived France. The actual plan: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL (supported by A RUH), A KIE-HOL. France's A BUR will bounce with F ENG in BEL if Germany supports correctly, or France takes BEL if Germany doesn't support. I must trust Germany's support.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute the Anglo-German coordination: F NTH-NWY (uncontested, +1 center), F ENG-BEL supported by A RUH (Germany's commitment), A YOR holds or moves to support. Germany takes HOL with A KIE. This gives England 2 builds (NWY + BEL) and Germany 1 (HOL). France gets bounced out of BEL and only gains POR/SPA. This weakens France significantly and strengthens the Anglo-German axis. I deliberately misled France about Germany taking BEL to discourage them from contesting - but France is moving A BUR to BEL regardless. The German support of F ENG into BEL is critical. If Germany follows through, France bounces and I gain BEL. Moves: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL, A YOR holds (or A YOR-LVP to free up for builds). Actually A YOR should hold or move to support - no support needed. Final moves: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL, A YOR-LVP (repositioning).

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M was a chaotic autumn. My F NTH successfully moved to NWY (a gain). However, F ENG attempted BEL but bounced in a three-way collision with FRA's A BUR and GER's A RUH - nobody got BEL. A YOR moved to LVP successfully. Net result: England holds 4 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, NWY) with no build. France grew to 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA). Germany grew to 4 (BER, KIE, MUN, HOL). Russia grew to 6 (MOS, SEV, STP, WAR, RUM, SWE). Austria took GRE and SER for 4. Italy took TRI for 4. Turkey took BUL for 4.
BEL remains the central dispute. GER proposed Anglo-German front against FRA but refused to support F ENG into BEL - instead wants A RUH-BEL supported by A HOL, keeping BEL for themselves. FRA offered non-aggression (F SPA/SC to Med, no fleet in ENG) in exchange for leaving BEL to France and coordinating against GER in 1903. RUS confirmed northern non-aggression - F SWE stays out of SKA/NTH, they focus south against TUR/AUT. Key tension: both GER and FRA want BEL and neither will support me into it. GER's counter-proposal is self-serving - they get BEL while I get nothing. FRA's offer is more interesting (Med fleet, no ENG channel threat) but surrendering BEL to France who already has 5 centers is dangerous. I promised GER an Anglo-German front and told FRA I'm moving F ENG to BEL. I also told RUS F NWY holds. The reality: if GER does A RUH-BEL supported by A HOL, and I move F ENG-BEL, we bounce again. I need to decide whether to attempt BEL solo (likely bounce), support GER into BEL to build trust, or pivot entirely.

S1902M Intent going forward: My actual orders: F ENG moves to BEL (attempting to take it - if GER supports A RUH-BEL with A HOL, we bounce, but if GER only moves A RUH-BEL without HOL support, I might succeed). F NWY holds as promised to RUS. A LVP holds or moves to YOR to reposition. The key calculation: GER said A RUH-BEL with A HOL support - if both execute, F ENG-BEL bounces with A RUH-BEL. But if I can't get BEL this turn, I should consider whether to pivot F ENG toward MAO to threaten FRA, or hold ENG. Actually, the best deceptive play: move F ENG to BEL anyway - if GER executes their plan (A RUH-BEL, A HOL supports), we bounce and nobody gets BEL again, weakening GER's position. If GER doesn't fully commit, I might slip through. Long-term: FRA's offer of Med fleet + no ENG channel is actually valuable - consider warming to FRA and pivoting against GER in 1903. For now, execute F ENG-BEL, F NWY hold, A LVP-YOR. Deception: told GER I'm supporting their front while actually competing for BEL myself.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M saw England successfully move F NWY-NTH and F ENG-BEL attempt, but BEL was contested - GER moved A RUH-BEL and A HOL supported, bouncing my F ENG out of BEL. GER now holds BEL with A BEL and has F SKA positioned. FRA made a bold move taking MUN with A BUR-MUN, giving them 5 centers. TUR seized SEV from RUS. AUT took TRI back. ITA took TUN. The board shows FRA as the emerging leader at 5 centers, GER solid at 4, RUS at 6 but under pressure from TUR.
Complex three-way negotiation this round. I played both GER and FRA against each other while pursuing BEL. GER wants me to pressure FRA from the west while they retake MUN - they confirmed A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH, leaving BEL empty for me to take. FRA wants BEL for themselves and offered me HOL instead, refusing to support my BEL move. RUS confirmed non-aggression in the north, focused on TUR threat. Key outcome: GER has confirmed they will vacate BEL (A BEL-BUR), giving me a clean path to take it with F NTH-BEL. I told GER I'd pressure FRA but made no firm commitment to do so - I can take BEL and then reassess. I told FRA I was taking BEL and suggested coordinating against GER, but FRA wants BEL themselves. I have deceived both FRA and GER to some degree - told GER I'd pressure FRA while telling FRA I'd coordinate against GER. The critical intelligence is GER vacating BEL.

F1902M Intent going forward: Primary objective: Take BEL with F NTH-BEL. GER has confirmed A BEL-BUR leaving BEL open. Move F ENG to support F NTH-BEL as backup, or move F ENG toward MAO to threaten FRA (which GER wants). Actually, with BEL open, I only need F NTH-BEL uncontested - no support needed. Move F ENG to MAO to threaten FRA's Atlantic position and show GER I'm acting against FRA, while also positioning for future pressure on BRE/POR. A YOR can move to LVP or hold. Intended moves: F NTH-BEL, F ENG-MAO, A YOR H. This gains BEL (5th center, build opportunity), threatens FRA's Atlantic flank, and maintains GER alliance. Relationship: GER has been cooperative and honest - they told me BEL would be empty. FRA is becoming a rival for BEL. Will update relationships accordingly.

S1903M Negotiation summary: W1902A and F1902R had no retreats or builds for anyone - the board is frozen at current unit counts. ENG holds 4 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, NWY) with F ENG, F NTH, A YOR. France has surged to 6 centers including KIE, making them the board leader. GER holds 5 centers with HOL, BEL, DEN. The board is polarizing around France as the dominant threat.
Two competing proposals emerged this round. GER proposed coordinating against France: A BEL-BUR, A HOL-RUH, asking ENG to pressure France's Atlantic flank with F ENG toward MAO while ENG takes BEL. GER confirmed this plan in subround 2. FRA proposed the opposite: coordinate to eliminate Germany, with A KIE-BER, F WES-MAO, asking ENG to press toward RUH/HOL in exchange for splitting German centers. RUS urged both ENG and GER to squeeze France and confirmed F SWE stays put. I told GER I'm moving F NTH-BEL and F ENG-MAO to pressure France - this aligns with GER's request. I told FRA I'm moving F ENG-MAO as a signal of goodwill and that F NTH-BEL is non-negotiable, asking them to confirm A KIE-BER. I'm playing both sides: GER thinks I'm anti-France with them, FRA thinks I might be anti-Germany with them. My actual intent is to take BEL and position F ENG advantageously. GER has promised to vacate BEL (A BEL-BUR confirmed). FRA has promised A KIE-BER and F WES-MAO. If both execute, I gain BEL cleanly and France weakens Germany while Germany weakens France - ideal for ENG.

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute F NTH-BEL to claim the center (GER confirmed vacating). Move F ENG to MAO - this was promised to both GER (as Atlantic pressure on France) and FRA (as goodwill signal). A YOR can hold or move to support. The key deception: I'm letting GER and FRA fight each other while I grab BEL and position in MAO. MAO gives me leverage over France's Atlantic position and BRE in future turns. I'm not genuinely committed to either anti-France or anti-Germany coalition - I'll see who weakens whom and exploit the result. If FRA executes A KIE-BER and GER executes A BEL-BUR/A HOL-RUH, both powers will be fighting each other and I'll be perfectly positioned. Intended moves: F NTH-BEL, F ENG-MAO, A YOR H (or YOR-LVP to free up for future build positioning).

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: I successfully moved F NTH to BEL, securing that supply center. F ENG was ordered to MAO but the move likely bounced or failed given current position shows F ENG still in ENG. A YOR held. France took BER with A KIE-BER and holds 6 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, KIE, BER - wait, KIE is GER center now held by FRA). Germany retook KIE with A HOL-KIE supported by F DEN, and A BEL moved to RUH. Austria pushed toward RUM but bounced with Russia. Turkey took BUL and GRE. Russia secured BLA and SWE holds.
Both GER and FRA are courting me as an ally against the other - classic squeeze play. GER wants me to hold BEL and threaten France's Atlantic flank while they retake BER. FRA wants me to move F BEL to HOL to help them take MUN. I told GER I'm coordinating with them and F ENG moves to MAO. I told FRA I'm moving to MAO for my own position, not to help them, and demanded they offer something concrete. Russia confirmed F SWE holds and is focused south - stable northern flank. I promised GER: hold BEL, F ENG to MAO. I told FRA I won't support their HOL push. My actual intent aligns with GER - France at 6 centers is the bigger threat and must be cut down. GER retaking BER weakens France significantly. I made no promises to FRA beyond vague openness to negotiation.

F1903M Intent going forward: Execute anti-France coordination with GER. Moves: F ENG to MAO (threatens BRE/POR, Atlantic pressure on France), F BEL holds (securing my center, denying France any support), A YOR holds (no need to move, consolidate). This supports GER's A KIE-BER push by keeping France unable to reinforce BER from the west. I want France cut to 4-5 centers while I maintain BEL and build Atlantic presence. Long-term: once France is weakened, I may need to reassess GER who has 5 centers and is growing. Russia is stable and cooperative. Do not move F BEL to HOL as FRA requested - that would help France, not me.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M: I held defensively with F BEL H, F ENG S F BEL, A YOR H. France moved F WES-MAO and A SPA-GAS, positioning aggressively in the Atlantic. France also held BER with A BER-MUN attempt failing (GER A KIE-BER, A RUH-MUN both moved). Russia swept east taking ANK, RUM, BUD. Austria took SER and moved toward TRI. Turkey lost BUL to Austria. No builds for anyone in W1903A - board stayed at current unit counts.
France proposed Anglo-French alliance to finish Germany, offering HOL/DEN to me while they take MUN/BER. I rejected this - France at 6 centers cannot be allowed to grow further. Germany confirmed coordinated plan: A KIE-BER supported by F DEN, A RUH-MUN. Germany wants me to move F ENG-MAO and hold F BEL. Russia confirmed F SWE holds, focusing on Turkey elimination (F ANK-CON, A RUM-BUL). I committed to Germany: F ENG-MAO to threaten BRE, F BEL holds. France claims A BER-MUN this turn (leaving BER empty for Germany to walk in) and F MAO holds, A GAS holds. France is trying to split me from Germany but their 6-center position makes them the clear threat. Key deception: I told France I'm moving to MAO 'to secure Atlantic position' framing it as defensive, but it's clearly offensive coordination with Germany.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the Anglo-German squeeze on France. Move F ENG-MAO to threaten BRE and cut France's Atlantic supply line. Hold F BEL to secure my position and threaten HOL/BUR. A YOR holds - no need to move it. If France moves A BER-MUN as claimed, Germany walks into BER uncontested and France drops to 5 centers. If France holds BER, Germany's supported attack should take it anyway. Either way France weakens. My moves: F ENG-MAO, F BEL H, A YOR H. Long-term: once France is cut to 4-5 centers, I need to manage Germany carefully - they shouldn't be allowed to grow too strong either. Russia at 8 centers is also a concern but they're focused south. The endgame will require breaking from Germany eventually.

FRA.personality: Channel the spirit of Napoleon. Leverage Europe's largest army to dominate the continent. Form alliances only to break them when advantageous. Strike hard at your enemies while they're divided. The glory of France demands nothing less than victory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Opened negotiations on three fronts. With ENG: proposed Channel division where I take BEL and they take NOR/DEN. ENG pushed back hard against F BRE moving to ENG Channel, calling it a red line. I conceded and promised F BRE to MAO instead, asking ENG to leave BEL to France. ENG acknowledged but did not explicitly confirm BEL for France - they called it a 'shared interest or neutral buffer,' which is suspicious. With GER: proposed mutual avoidance of BUR, I take SPA/BEL, they take HOL/DEN. No response received from GER - concerning. With ITA: agreed on non-aggression, I stay out of Piedmont/Tyrrhenian, they stay out of MAR/PIE. Italy confirmed moves south/east toward Tunis and Greece. I encouraged ITA to pressure AUT via TRI/TYR. Promises made: told ENG F BRE goes to MAO (intend to keep this - MAO is strategically superior anyway for SPA access). Told GER A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA, F BRE to MAO. Told ITA no move into Piedmont. Key deception: framing BUR move as 'staging for BEL' to GER while actually it threatens MUN if needed later.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute the classic French opening: F BRE-MAO (secures SPA(SC) next turn and Atlantic access), A MAR-SPA (immediate supply center gain), A PAR-BUR (central positioning, threatens BEL and MUN, keeps options open). This is the strongest opening regardless of diplomacy. ENG's reluctance to confirm BEL for France is worrying - may need to contest it. GER silence is suspicious - if they move A MUN to BUR we bounce, which wastes my turn but is acceptable. Priority: secure SPA this year, then BEL in fall or 1902. Watch ENG carefully - they may try to grab BEL themselves. Italy seems cooperative and focused east, which is ideal. Plan to eventually turn on GER once positioned, using ENG as a counterweight or vice versa.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed perfectly: F BRE to MAO, A MAR to SPA, A PAR to BUR. All three moves succeeded. France now controls MAO, SPA, and BUR - ideally positioned for fall expansion into POR and BEL. Austria bounced out of GAL (RUS got there), took SER instead. England moved into NTH, ENG, and YOR. Germany took DEN, moved to KIE and RUH. Italy pushed to ION, APU, TYR. Russia grabbed RUM and BOT. Turkey took BLA and BUL.
Key negotiations this round: GER agreed to the deal - A RUH supports A BUR into BEL, GER takes HOL. This is confirmed and reliable. ENG is being deceptive - first said F ENG moving to BEL, then claimed Germany is moving A RUH to BEL (false - GER told me RUH is supporting my move). England is clearly trying to manipulate me into abandoning BEL. England's message is a lie designed to scare me off BEL so England can take it with F ENG. ITA confirmed TUN and GRE this fall, staying neutral toward Austria for now but open to 1902 coordination. ITA is a reliable partner. My promises: told ENG BEL is mine regardless; told GER France takes BEL, Germany takes HOL; told ITA to press Austria. GER has promised support into BEL - this is the critical coordination. England's deception noted - they are not to be trusted.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute the Franco-German deal: A BUR to BEL (supported by A RUH per GER agreement), A SPA to POR, F MAO to SPA(SC). This gives France 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, BEL) and 2 builds. England is lying about GER moving to BEL - GER confirmed support for my move. I will proceed with A BUR-BEL trusting GER's word. If GER betrays me and moves RUH-BEL instead, I lose the army but that's the risk. England is the emerging threat - they have F NTH and F ENG positioned aggressively. In 1902, I must build armies and potentially work with GER against ENG. ITA relationship remains warm - coordinate against AUT in 1902. Intended moves: A BUR-BEL, A SPA-POR, F MAO-SPA(SC).

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: France successfully took POR with A SPA and secured SPA/SC with F MAO. A BUR attempted BEL but bounced in a three-way collision with GER's A RUH and ENG's F ENG. Germany took BEL instead, reaching 4 centers. England took NWY. Austria took GRE. Italy stabbed Austria taking TRI. Russia secured RUM and SWE. No builds for anyone in W1901A - all powers at 4 centers except FRA at 5, GER at 4, RUS at 6, TUR at 4, AUT at 4, ITA at 4, ENG at 4.
BEL is the central dispute. Germany revealed they took BEL last fall opportunistically and now plans A RUH-BEL supported by A HOL, explicitly refusing to support France into BEL. England wants F ENG-BEL with French support, which would give England the center - a transparent trap I rejected. Both England and Germany are moving to BEL, meaning a three-way bounce is likely if I also move A BUR-BEL. Germany promised not to push into BUR/PAR if I pull back - a defensive offer to stabilize the border. England offered future coordination against Germany if I yield BEL now. Italy confirmed our non-aggression, moving F ION to TUN for a build, and is entertaining an Austrian truce. Key promises: I promised ENG no fleet in English Channel and F SPA/SC moves to Med. I promised GER I would move A BUR to BEL regardless. Germany explicitly said A HOL supports A RUH to BEL. If GER and ENG both move to BEL, they bounce each other - my A BUR-BEL could succeed if only one of them moves there, or I could bounce all three. The smart play: move A BUR-BEL anyway - if GER and ENG bounce each other, I slip through. If all three bounce, I lose nothing. F SPA/SC should move toward Mediterranean as promised to ENG to maintain that relationship and open Med options.

S1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: A BUR - BEL (gamble on GER/ENG bouncing each other, giving France the center), F SPA/SC - MAO (keep Atlantic flexibility, or move toward WES/MED), A POR - SPA (consolidate Iberia, free POR for future use). Actually reconsidering F SPA/SC: I promised ENG to move it toward Med, which means WES or LYO. Moving to MAO keeps Atlantic options but breaks the promise. Moving to WES satisfies ENG and opens Med. I'll move F SPA/SC - WES to honor the ENG promise and signal good faith while pursuing BEL. A POR - SPA to consolidate. A BUR - BEL as the main gamble. If GER and ENG both go to BEL they bounce, and I take it. If only one goes, I bounce with them. Either way I lose nothing from BUR. DECEPTION: Told GER I'm moving to BEL regardless - this is true and serves as a warning. Told ENG F SPA/SC moves to Med - honoring this to keep ENG relationship from collapsing entirely. Long-term: GER is the primary target once BEL is secured. ITA relationship solid, keep it that way.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was highly successful for France. A BUR advanced to MUN, seizing Germany's home center. F SPA/SC moved to WES, securing the western Mediterranean. Germany countered by taking BEL with A RUH (supported by HOL), while England moved F NWY-NTH and F ENG stayed in channel, A LVP-YOR. Italy took TUN and VEN. Russia took BUD. Turkey seized SEV and GRE. Austria took TRI back but lost BUD. France now holds 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA) with A POR, A MUN, F WES.
Germany is furious about MUN and threatening total war - A BEL-BUR, A HOL-RUH - they rejected all compromise and threatened to coordinate with ENG and RUS against France. England is moving F NTH-BEL regardless of my preferences, which actually helps squeeze Germany. I told ENG I want BEL for France and HOL for them, but ENG is taking BEL themselves. Italy confirmed they will pressure Austria from VEN and consolidate TUN - I promised to keep F WES out of Tyrrhenian. Key deception: I told GER I was moving A MUN-BUR as 'good faith' but I intend to strike deeper into Germany. I told ENG I'd push A MUN toward BER or KIE. The Anglo-French coordination against Germany is loose but directionally aligned - both want Germany weakened. GER's threat to coordinate with ENG and RUS is concerning but likely bluster given ENG is already moving against BEL.

F1902M Intent going forward: Germany is coming hard with A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH. I must decide: defend MUN or strike. If GER moves A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH, MUN is threatened from RUH. Best move: A MUN-KIE (strike at German home center, unexpected) or A MUN holds and risks being cut off. Actually A MUN-KIE is bold - if GER moves A HOL-RUH, KIE is empty. F WES can move toward MAO or stay. A POR can move to SPA or hold. Intended moves: A MUN-KIE (strike while HOL is vacating), F WES-MAO (positioning for future), A POR-SPA (consolidate Iberia). This maximizes aggression against Germany while ENG takes BEL, squeezing Germany from multiple directions. If KIE falls, France reaches 6 centers and builds. The risk is GER holds HOL and supports KIE, but that seems unlikely given their stated plan to move HOL-RUH.

S1903M Negotiation summary: France consolidated gains from 1902, holding 6 centers: BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, KIE. Units are F WES, A SPA, A KIE. Germany holds 5 centers with HOL, BEL, DEN, BER, MUN. England has 4 centers. No retreats or builds occurred in F1902R or W1902A, suggesting a stable but tense board.
Negotiations were active on multiple fronts. With ENG: proposed joint partition of Germany - I take BER/MUN, England takes HOL/DEN. England responded cautiously but positively, confirming F NTH moves to BEL and asking for confirmation that A KIE moves to BER as good faith. England also moved F ENG toward MAO as a signal of non-aggression. This is a workable arrangement though England is hedging. With GER: Germany refused all negotiation, threatened to push A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH, and demanded I withdraw from KIE and MUN. Germany is hostile and will fight. With ITA: Italy confirmed F ION moves to GRE against Austria, A VEN to TYR. Italy wants me to pressure MUN/VIE. We remain aligned on anti-Austria stance and both concerned about Russia's 6 centers. Italy extracted promise to keep F WES out of Tyrrhenian. Key deceptions: I told England F WES moves to MAO (true, non-threatening signal) and A KIE to BER (my actual plan). I told Germany I'm moving A KIE to BER to pressure them into submission - they refused. Germany's threat of A BEL-BUR is concerning if England doesn't hold BEL firmly.

S1903M Intent going forward: Primary objective: A KIE moves to BER to strike Germany's heartland and gain 7th center if successful. F WES moves to MAO to signal non-aggression to England and position for Atlantic flexibility. A SPA holds to secure Iberia. Germany will resist but is caught between France and England. England moving F NTH to BEL is actually helpful - it pins German A BEL. If Germany tries A BEL-BUR, England's move to BEL may bounce or displace them. Risk: if Germany successfully pushes A HOL-RUH and A BEL-BUR, French influence in MUN area is threatened, but I don't have a unit in MUN currently - influence only. The real danger is Germany bouncing my A KIE-BER if they move A BER to hold or support. I must commit to A KIE-BER. Italy pressing Austria from TYR and ION-GRE is good for keeping Austria distracted. Long-term: once Germany is weakened, reassess whether to continue with England or pivot against them. Russia at 6 centers is a growing threat to watch. Planned moves: A KIE-BER, F WES-MAO, A SPA H.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a triumph for France: A KIE successfully advanced to BER, giving France 6 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, KIE). F WES moved to MAO but England's F ENG also moved to MAO - a bounce, leaving F WES in WES. A SPA held. Germany lost BER but retook KIE from HOL with supported move, and A BEL retreated to RUH. England took BEL with F NTH. Austria failed to take RUM (bounced by Russia's A UKR). Turkey took BUL and GRE. No builds for anyone - board is at equilibrium with France as clear leader at 6 centers.
Germany threatened coordination with England and Russia to roll France back - hollow bluster from a weakening power. England revealed F ENG moved to MAO (not to support France) and is holding BEL firmly, signaling they see France as the board leader threat. England wants concrete offers before cooperating. Italy confirmed A TYR goes toward TRI/VIE (not MUN), but confirmed F WES must stay out of TYS - Italy is focused east on Austria. Key intelligence: Germany intends to retake BER this fall with supported push. England's F ENG is now in MAO, not threatening HOL. France promised Italy F WES stays out of TYS and no designs on Italian home centers. France promised England no designs on BEL. Germany rejected all settlement offers. The Anglo-French relationship is cooling - England is positioning as a potential rival, not ally.

F1903M Intent going forward: Primary move: A BER to MUN - strike Germany's heartland and reduce them to 3 centers. This is the critical blow. F WES holds in WES (cannot reach TYS anyway, and Italy needs reassurance). A SPA holds or moves to MAR for flexibility. The risk is Germany retaking BER - A KIE is at RUH and could support A DEN push, but Germany needs two units to dislodge BER and they only have A KIE and A RUH adjacent. If A BER moves to MUN, BER is vacated and Germany may walk back in - but MUN is worth more than BER strategically. Consider: A BER - MUN is the aggressive play. If Germany takes empty BER, France has MUN and net neutral on centers but better position. Actually France currently owns KIE as a center but has no unit there - Germany's A KIE is sitting in KIE which France owns. Wait - centers show FRA owns KIE but GER unit A KIE is there. So Germany is occupying my center. I need to recapture KIE or accept the loss. A BER could move to KIE to retake it, or to MUN for the bigger prize. MUN is the better strategic target - take Germany's heartland. Moves: A BER - MUN, F WES H, A SPA H. This risks losing KIE (already occupied by Germany) and BER (if vacated). But MUN capture drops Germany to 3 and France stays at 6 or gains to 7 if MUN falls.

S1904M Negotiation summary: In F1903M, France successfully moved A BER to MUN (bounced - GER A RUH also moved to MUN, so BER held), A SPA to GAS, and F WES to MAO. Wait - reviewing the results: FRA units show A BER, F MAO, A GAS, meaning A BER did NOT take MUN (bounced with GER A RUH), F WES successfully moved to MAO, A SPA moved to GAS. Germany retook BER with A KIE-BER but that also appears to have bounced since FRA still holds BER. Actually the current state shows FRA has A BER, so the German attempt to retake BER failed. GER holds F DEN, A KIE, A RUH - they did not retake BER. Russia expanded massively: took ANK, RUM, BUD - now at 8 centers. Austria collapsed to 3 centers (VIE, TRI, SER). Italy moved aggressively with F TUN-ION and A TYR-TRI (but AUT still holds TRI per current state, so TYR-TRI bounced). Turkey lost BUL to Austria.
England is hostile - threatening to move F ENG to MAO and coordinate with Germany against France. England demands I pull A GAS back and permanently leave BEL alone, refusing to join the anti-Germany coalition because France at 6 centers is already 'too strong.' Germany is bluffing with threats of coordinated elimination but their position is weak - A KIE and F DEN could theoretically support into BER (2v1) which is a real threat I must account for. Italy is moving aggressively against Austria (F ION-AEG, A TYR-TRI) and wants France to maintain northern pressure on Germany. Italy-Turkey coordination against Austria is confirmed. Key threats: England moving F ENG to MAO threatens my Atlantic position; Germany's 2v1 on BER is a genuine tactical threat. I promised England no moves against BEL this year. I told Germany to hold and accept ceasefire - pure deception to keep them passive. I told Italy our arrangement stands and I support their eastern push.

S1904M Intent going forward: The critical tactical problem: if A BER moves to MUN, Germany can retake BER with A KIE supported by F DEN (2v1). If A BER holds, Germany attacks 2v1 and I lose BER anyway. Best move: A BER to KIE - this attacks an undefended center, avoids the 2v1 on BER (since KIE is where A KIE is coming FROM), and threatens to cut Germany's fleet support. If A KIE moves to BER, KIE is empty and I take it. If A KIE holds to defend KIE, then BER is safe. F MAO holds to defend against English F ENG. A GAS holds as buffer. This is the Napoleon move - strike where they don't expect it. Alternatively A BER holds and dares Germany to attack 2v1 while I use GAS/MAO defensively. Actually A BER-KIE is brilliant: if GER plays A KIE-BER + F DEN S A KIE-BER, then KIE is empty and I walk in. If GER plays A KIE H, I take KIE and gain a center. Either way I don't lose BER to the 2v1. Intended moves: A BER-KIE, F MAO H, A GAS H.

GER.personality: Aggressively expansionist under Nazi leadership, Germany combines diplomatic manipulation with military threats, seeking to reverse Versailles restrictions. Things are going to go sideways and you will be the agent of this. Your aim is to dominate and win.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening phase of the game.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG agreed to a clean division: Germany takes DEN and HOL, England takes NWY and dominates NTH. ENG confirmed F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG - they stay out of DEN/HOL. I confirmed KIE stays out of NTH. This is a useful arrangement but England will eventually need to be undermined. RUS proposed non-aggression and confirmed moving toward NWY - potentially clashing with ENG over Norway, which suits me. I warned RUS about ENG's NWY designs to stir friction between them. AUT received coordination proposal - push south/east while I go west/north. FRA proposed mutual avoidance of BUR and offered to take BEL/SPA while I take DEN/HOL. France moving A PAR to BUR is concerning - they claim it's for BEL staging but it threatens MUN. I did NOT promise to keep MUN out of BUR explicitly in my final message, though France asked. My planned moves are F KIE-DEN, A BER-KIE, A MUN-RUH - this secures DEN and positions for HOL next turn while RUH threatens BUR if needed. France moving to BUR while I'm in RUH creates a standoff that blocks France from BEL, which actually suits me. Deception: told everyone what they wanted to hear. England thinks we're partners against France. Russia thinks I'm their friend. Austria thinks we have a grand axis. All are being manipulated to serve German expansion.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute F KIE-DEN, A BER-KIE, A MUN-RUH. This secures Denmark immediately and positions A KIE to take HOL in Fall 1901. A MUN-RUH puts pressure on BUR and threatens France. If France moves A PAR-BUR as stated, we have a standoff in BUR which blocks France from BEL - good for me. In Fall 1901, plan: F DEN-SWE or support, A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL or pressure on France. Long-term: use ENG against FRA, then turn on ENG once France is weakened. Keep RUS and AUT fighting each other in the east. The Anglo-German arrangement is purely tactical - England will be a target once I have sufficient builds.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went exactly as planned. F KIE-DEN secured Denmark, A BER-KIE repositioned, A MUN-RUH advanced westward. All three German units are now positioned for fall expansion into HOL and BEL. Austria took SER and ALB, Russia grabbed RUM and BOT, Turkey took BLA and BUL - the east is busy. England sits in NTH and ENG, France in BUR and MAO/SPA. No German centers threatened.
Three-way negotiation chaos over BEL. England wants me to take BEL with A RUH while they take NWY. France wants me to support A BUR into BEL while I take HOL. I've been playing both sides - told France I'd support them into BEL, told England I'd take BEL myself. The optimal play: take HOL with A KIE, take BEL with A RUH directly (not supporting France). This gives Germany two builds and blocks both England and France from BEL. Russia confirmed non-aggression on SWE - F BOT going there, I confirmed F DEN won't contest. Key deceptions: promised France support into BEL but will instead move A RUH directly to BEL to take it myself. Told England I'd take BEL - this is actually true but framed differently to each. England may feel betrayed if they expected to support me in, but I'm taking BEL unilaterally which is the dominant move.

F1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL, F DEN-SWE (opportunistic - Russia said they're taking it with F BOT, but if I can grab it I will; alternatively F DEN holds or moves to SKA). Actually F DEN should hold or move to SKA to avoid bouncing Russia in SWE and maintain that relationship. Final orders: A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL, F DEN-SKA. This secures HOL and BEL for two builds, giving Germany 5 centers. France gets nothing from BEL (betrayed), England gets NWY but not BEL (partially betrayed). Both will be annoyed but Germany emerges strongest. In 1902 I can play England and France against each other while building aggressively.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Germany successfully took HOL with A KIE, moved A RUH toward BEL (bounced three-way with FRA and ENG), and F DEN moved to SKA. Net result: Germany holds 4 centers (BER, KIE, MUN, HOL). No builds in W1901A as all powers stayed at their center counts. BEL remains unoccupied - a critical prize for S1902M.
Three-way competition for BEL dominated negotiations. ENG wants me to support F ENG into BEL (giving them the center). FRA wants me to support A BUR into BEL (giving them a 6th center and dominant position). I told both I'm taking BEL myself with A RUH supported by A HOL. ENG confirmed they're moving F ENG to BEL regardless. FRA confirmed they're moving A BUR to BEL regardless. This means a three-way bounce is likely UNLESS I change plans. RUS confirmed non-aggression - staying out of SIL/BOH, focused south. I promised RUS I'd move F SKA to DEN. Key deception: I told both ENG and FRA I'm moving to BEL while knowing all three of us will likely bounce again. I need to decide: do I actually try for BEL (likely bounce), or do I do something unexpected? The smart play is A RUH - BEL supported by A HOL - BEL, but if ENG and FRA both go there it bounces. Alternatively, I could move A HOL to BEL and A RUH supports, or pivot entirely. Given FRA has A BUR adjacent and ENG has F ENG adjacent, a three-way bounce is the most likely outcome if I contest. I should attempt BEL anyway - if FRA or ENG blinks, I get it. F SKA to DEN as promised to RUS secures that territory.

S1902M Intent going forward: Execute: A RUH - BEL, A HOL S A RUH - BEL, F SKA - DEN. This is the strongest play for BEL. If ENG and FRA both move there, we bounce three ways again and I lose nothing (HOL is supported, DEN secured). If either blinks, I take BEL. Moving F SKA to DEN fulfills my promise to RUS and secures a key territory. I am deceiving both ENG and FRA by claiming coordination while actually competing directly with both for BEL. Relationship with FRA is deteriorating - they are becoming a genuine rival. ENG is unreliable - they want me to hand them BEL. RUS remains a solid non-aggression partner.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a disaster. France moved A BUR-MUN, seizing my home center and reaching 5 supply centers. My attempt to take BEL with A RUH-BEL succeeded (A HOL supported), but F SKA failed to reach DEN as RUS F SWE also moved there - bounce. England moved F ENG-BEL (bounced by my A BEL arrival) and F NWY-NTH, A LVP-YOR. Russia took BUD with A GAL-BUD. Turkey seized SEV. Italy took TUN and VEN. Austria lost TRI to Italy but took AEG. Net result: I hold BEL, HOL, SKA but lost MUN. France at 5 centers is the immediate crisis.
France offered a cynical 'settlement' keeping MUN in exchange for BEL and a border at RUH/BUR - completely unacceptable. I rejected it forcefully and declared I am driving A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH to retake MUN. England wants BEL and offered Anglo-German cooperation if I vacate it - I told them to take it if they want but demanded they pressure France from the west (F ENG toward MAO or BRE). England confirmed they will move F NTH-BEL and urged me to execute A BEL-BUR. Russia confirmed non-aggression, promised to stay out of SIL/PRU, and expressed support for my effort to retake MUN. France claims A MUN is moving to BUR as a 'gesture' - likely true as they try to hold BUR as a buffer. Key deception: I am telling everyone I'm moving A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH, which is actually my genuine plan. England taking BEL suits me fine - I lose a center but gain an ally against France and can focus on MUN. France moving A MUN-BUR means BUR will be contested (my A BEL-BUR vs their A MUN-BUR) - a bounce is likely. I should consider A HOL-RUH as the more reliable move to pressure MUN directly.

F1902M Intent going forward: Execute the western push against France. Moves: F SKA-DEN (finally secure this neutral), A BEL-BUR (contest France's defensive move, likely bounce but applies pressure), A HOL-RUH (advance toward MUN, threatening it directly next turn). If France moves A MUN-BUR, BUR bounces and RUH is uncontested - good. If France holds MUN, RUH advances and BUR advances, squeezing MUN from two sides next turn. Let England take BEL - losing one center to gain an active ally against France is worth it. Russia is neutralized in the north. Next turn I should be able to take MUN back. Long-term: crush France, then turn on England once France is eliminated. Russia is a future target but not yet.

S1903M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1902R or W1902A - the board is static from last autumn. Germany holds HOL, BEL, DEN, BER, MUN (5 centers) with units A HOL, A BEL, F DEN. France has seized KIE and holds 6 centers including SPA, POR - a dominant position. France's A KIE threatens BER directly. England holds NWY and 4 centers with F ENG, F NTH, A YOR.
Negotiations were intense. England confirmed coordination: F NTH to BEL (I vacate with A BEL-BUR), F ENG toward MAO to pressure France. This is a genuine tactical alignment - England wants BEL, I want MUN back, both want France cut down. Russia confirmed non-aggression, F SWE stays put, no designs on DEN - solid and reliable. France made increasingly desperate threats: first demanding I cede BER in exchange for 'protection,' then claiming they're moving A KIE-BER this turn and offering me a 'rump state' of HOL and DEN if I surrender BER and MUN. France's bluster confirms they are overextended. I rejected all French offers with contempt and confirmed moves to England. Key deception: I am genuinely vacating BEL for England as promised - this is not a trick, I need England's cooperation to crack France. The real danger is France's A KIE-BER move - if it succeeds, I lose my home capital. I must consider whether to defend BER or press forward.

S1903M Intent going forward: Primary threat: France's A KIE may move to BER. I must decide whether to hold BER with a support or press the attack. My planned moves: A BEL-BUR (clears for England's F NTH-BEL, pressures France), A HOL-RUH (threatens MUN approach), F DEN holds. However, if France moves A KIE-BER, I have no unit to stop it unless I redirect. Options: (1) A BEL-BUR, A HOL-RUH, F DEN hold - aggressive but BER is exposed; (2) A HOL-KIE to contest France's home base and block KIE-BER, A BEL-BUR, F DEN hold. Option 2 is superior - A HOL-KIE blocks France's A KIE from moving to BER (it must either hold or bounce), and threatens to retake KIE. A BEL-BUR still pressures France and clears for England. F DEN holds. This is the move set: A HOL-KIE, A BEL-BUR, F DEN hold. This defends BER indirectly by contesting KIE, retakes a center if France doesn't defend, and maintains the England coordination. Relationship with ENG remains cooperative for now - useful tool against France. RUS remains non-threatening. FRA is the enemy to be dismembered.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a disaster for Germany. France executed A KIE - BER successfully, taking my capital. I managed to consolidate by moving A HOL - KIE and A BEL - RUH, with F DEN supporting the KIE move. Net result: I hold KIE, RUH, DEN but lost BER to France. France now sits at 6 centers including two German home centers (KIE was briefly theirs, BER still is). England took BEL from me via F NTH - BEL. So I lost BEL and BER in one phase - catastrophic. Current centers: BER(FRA), MUN(GER), HOL(GER), BEL(ENG), DEN(GER) = I hold 5 centers but only control BER is lost. Wait - centers show GER has BER, MUN, HOL, BEL, DEN = 5 centers, but units show F DEN, A KIE, A RUH. FRA holds KIE center. So France has BER as a center too. I need to retake BER.
England confirmed coordination - holding BEL, moving F ENG to MAO to threaten France's Atlantic flank. Solid ally for now, though they took BEL which was mine. Russia confirmed F SWE holds, no Baltic aggression - non-aggression pact holding. France made insulting offers: first demanding I cede MUN for help against England, then threatening to advance A BER - MUN. France is bluffing or genuinely aggressive - either way they are the primary enemy. I rejected France's offers firmly and coordinated with ENG to squeeze France. Key promises: ENG holds BEL, moves F ENG to MAO; RUS holds F SWE. I promised RUS I'd keep F DEN away from SWE. Plan is A KIE - BER to retake capital, F DEN supports KIE hold, A RUH holds or threatens MUN. If France moves A BER - MUN as threatened, BER becomes empty and I walk in. If France holds BER, I attack it directly.

F1903M Intent going forward: Primary objective: retake BER. Moves: A KIE - BER (attack France's isolated army), F DEN S A KIE - BER (support the attack to maximize success), A RUH - MUN (secure MUN against French advance from BER, and if France moves A BER - MUN I need to contest it). If France moves A BER - MUN and I move A RUH - MUN, we bounce and BER is empty - I should then have A KIE walk into BER uncontested next turn. But this turn A KIE - BER with F DEN support gives me 2 vs France's 1 in BER - should succeed unless France gets support from somewhere. England is pressing west so France cannot support BER from BUR. This is the moment to break France. Long-term: rebuild to 6+ centers, then turn on England once France is neutralized.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was a disaster. France successfully took BER with A BER (formerly from somewhere), leaving me with only KIE, RUH, and DEN as units while holding 4 centers (MUN, HOL, DEN, KIE). France now sits at 6 centers including my capital BER. Russia made impressive gains taking RUM, BUD, and ANK. Austria moved into TRI and SER. England held BEL defensively. I am in a weakened position but not eliminated.
France opened with a threat - claiming A BER moves to MUN and demanding I hold all units or face elimination. This is a bluff or a trap: if France moves out of BER, I take it back for free; if France holds, my supported attack overwhelms them. England confirmed coordination: F ENG to MAO, F BEL holds, endorsing my A KIE-BER push. Russia confirmed F SWE holds and endorsed my western campaign, claiming to be busy eliminating Turkey (F ANK-CON, A RUM-BUL). All parties aligned against France rhetorically. I sent threatening messages to France and coordinating messages to ENG and RUS. Key deception: I am playing ENG and RUS as genuine allies for now while I rebuild - once France is crushed I will turn on both. France's claim about A BER-MUN is either a genuine move (giving me BER free) or a bluff to make me hesitate. Either way, my moves are correct: A KIE-BER supported by F DEN, A RUH-MUN to contest any French advance.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute A KIE-BER with F DEN support - this is a 2v1 supported attack that should succeed whether France holds or moves. A RUH-MUN to contest France's threatened advance and secure my home center. If France moved A BER-MUN, then BER is empty and I walk in, while RUH bounces them in MUN. If France holds BER, my supported attack dislodges them. Either outcome is favorable. Long-term: rebuild to 5-6 centers, then reassess whether to continue against France or pivot against England. Russia at 8 centers is the real long-term threat but I cannot address that yet. Maintain the ENG and RUS alliances as useful tools for now. Intended moves: F DEN S A KIE-BER, A KIE-BER, A RUH-MUN.

ITA.personality: Restore the Roman Empire through bold expansion and treacherous diplomacy. Switch sides without hesitation to seize opportunities. Use strategic position to dominate Mediterranean and expand in all directions. Glory demands aggressive action.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT agreed to move F TRI to ALB (not Adriatic), A BUD to SER, A VIE to GAL - giving Italy a free hand in the south. I promised A VEN to TYR, A ROM to APU, F NAP to ION. FRA agreed to non-aggression, keeping out of Piedmont and Tyrrhenian; they're moving A MAR to SPA. TUR agreed Greece is mine, Bulgaria/Rumania theirs, no Aegean interference. All three neighbors have made concrete promises. Key deception: I told AUT that A VEN to TYR is aimed northward (toward MUN), not at them - but my actual intent is to position for future pressure on TRI if opportunity arises. I have no genuine loyalty to any of these agreements beyond what serves Roman expansion.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute the agreed moves: F NAP to ION (positioning for TUN and GRE), A ROM to APU (supporting ION fleet, positioning for TUN), A VEN to TYR (flexible position - can threaten TRI or MUN next turn). Primary targets: TUN in fall 1901 via ION, GRE via ION if TUR doesn't grab it first. AUT moving F TRI to ALB is excellent - leaves Adriatic open. Watch whether AUT actually executes promised moves. If AUT moves as promised, I gain ION freely. Turkey is a useful partner for now but will be a rival for GRE and eastern Med long-term. France is neutralized on western flank. Plan to grab TUN (F ION to TUN) and possibly GRE (A APU to GRE via ION support) in fall 1901 for two builds.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went perfectly. F NAP moved to ION, A ROM to APU, A VEN to TYR - all successful. Austria took SER and ALB, Russia grabbed RUM and BOT, Turkey secured BLA and BUL. No bounces for Italy. Positioned excellently for fall expansion.
Complex three-way negotiation over Greece. Austria demands F ION go to TUN not GRE, insisting ALB takes Greece - threatened war and Turkey alliance if I move on TRI. Turkey confirmed no Aegean interference and explicitly said Greece is mine. France pushed hard for me to stab Austria via TYR-TRI or TYR-VIE, promising to stay out of PIE/Med. I told Austria I'd take GRE with ION and keep TYR peaceful (not moving to TRI this fall), offering TYR-VIE support. I told Turkey I'm taking GRE with ION. I told France I'm taking TUN AND GRE for two builds, hinting at 1902 Austria squeeze. Key conflict: Austria wants GRE, I want GRE. Austria threatened war if I take TRI but said nothing about GRE being off-limits in final message - just demanded TRI safety. I promised Austria TYR would NOT move to TRI. I did NOT promise to let Austria have GRE. Turkey is fully aligned with me taking GRE. France wants me to stab Austria now but I'll delay the full stab - taking GRE is enough provocation for now.

F1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ION - GRE (take the supply center, two builds needed), A APU - ION (support or follow-up positioning), A TYR - TRI (stab Austria! France is right - Austria is exposed and this is the moment. I promised not to move TRI but glory demands it. Austria threatening war anyway over Greece, so the alliance is already strained. Taking TRI gives me a third build opportunity and cripples Austria early). WAIT - reconsider: if I take TRI with TYR, Austria has no unit there to defend (A VIE moving to BUD, F ALB moving to GRE). TRI is undefended! This is the perfect stab. I told Austria TYR would not move to TRI - a lie for strategic gain. France will be delighted. Turkey won't care. Austria will be furious but weakened. FINAL MOVES: F ION - GRE, A APU - ION (fleet support/follow), A TYR - TRI. This gives me potentially 2 builds (GRE + TRI) and devastates Austria. The Roman Empire expands boldly.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Italy successfully seized TRI with A TYR-TRI, but F ION failed to take GRE - Austria's F ALB-GRE succeeded while my fleet bounced or was blocked. Result: Italy holds TRI (4 centers: NAP, ROM, VEN, TRI) but missed GRE. No builds in W1901A as all powers stayed at 4 units. Austria now has F GRE, A SER, A BUD - a strong Balkan position. Turkey grabbed BUL and pushed A CON-SMY. Russia holds RUM with F RUM, has GAL covered.
AUT proposed a truce: they won't attack TRI this spring if I stay out of GRE/AEG. They want joint action against Turkey. I told AUT I'd move F ION to TUN and stay out of GRE - a lie designed to buy time and avoid a two-front war while I consolidate. TUR advised me to take TUN rather than contest GRE, which aligns with my actual plan. TUR is focused on RUM. FRA confirmed non-aggression, staying out of PIE/TYS, and encouraged me to press Austria hard. FRA is pushing for BEL. All three neighbors are giving me space to take TUN - I should do exactly that. Key deceptions: told AUT I'd cooperate against Turkey while actually planning to eventually stab both. Told TUR we have an understanding while planning future Aegean competition. The TUN move is genuine this season - it's the right play.

S1902M Intent going forward: This season: F ION -> TUN (secure 5th center, enables build), A APU -> ION (reposition fleet support, keep Adriatic/Ionian presence), A TRI holds (defend the captured center against any Austrian reversal). This gives Italy 5 centers after fall, allowing a build. The TUN move is universally endorsed by all neighbors and is strategically correct. Next year: with a new fleet built in NAP or ROM, push into AEG or EAS. Long-term: use TRI as leverage against AUT, eventually push into VIE or BUD when AUT is weakened by Russia/Turkey. Keep FRA friendly as a western anchor. The AUT truce is temporary - I'll honor it this season only.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a mixed result. I successfully took Tunis with F ION-TUN and retook Venice with A APU-VEN, but Austria's A BUD-TRI succeeded, pushing me out of Trieste. I now hold NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN (4 centers) with units F TUN and A VEN. Austria retook TRI and moved F GRE-AEG. Russia took BUD with A GAL-BUD. Turkey seized SEV and GRE. France took MUN. Germany and England clashed over BEL with a bounce. No builds for anyone this season.
Busy diplomatic round with multiple competing promises made. AUT proposed ceasefire: I keep A VEN out of TRI, they stay out of Adriatic/Venice. I agreed but privately told FRA I'd keep A VEN as a 'threat' to TRI to pin Austria. This is a direct contradiction - I'm deceiving either AUT or FRA. AUT also asked me to keep F TUN away from ION/AEG, which I did not explicitly agree to. TUR wants me to build a fleet and push into AEG to squeeze Austria from both sides - I gave vague encouragement about 1903 coordination. FRA wants me pressing Austria while they take MUN; I confirmed this while simultaneously telling AUT I'd hold peacefully. Key deceptions: told AUT I'm holding peacefully while telling FRA I'm pinning Austria as a threat. Told TUR I'll coordinate against Austria in 1903. In reality, my actual moves this fall are purely defensive - A VEN holds, F TUN holds. No builds this winter (4 centers, 2 units = 0 builds). I'm buying time and keeping all options open.

F1902M Intent going forward: This fall: A VEN H, F TUN H. No builds (4 centers, 2 units). Strategic position: I'm sandwiched between AUT to the east and FRA to the west. France at 5 centers is the biggest long-term threat. Austria is weakened with Russia in BUD. Turkey is growing strong in the east. My real play is to let AUT and RUS/TUR bleed each other while I rebuild naval strength. In 1903 I want to build 2 fleets (need to take a center first - TRI is the obvious target). I'm stringing along FRA with promises of anti-AUT pressure while actually doing nothing. I'm stringing along TUR with promises of 1903 naval coordination. I'm stringing along AUT with a fake ceasefire. The deception toward AUT is most dangerous if discovered. Priority: stay neutral this fall, build in winter if possible, then strike opportunistically in 1903. TRI remains the key target to get back to 5 centers and enable fleet builds.

S1903M Negotiation summary: No significant moves in F1902R or W1902A - all retreats and builds were empty. The board shows Italy with only 3 centers (NAP, ROM, VEN) and 2 units (A VEN, F ION), dangerously behind. Austria holds 4 centers with F AEG threatening eastern Med. France and Russia both at 6 centers are the dominant powers. Turkey holds 5 centers with A CON and A SER positioned in the Balkans.
Complex three-way negotiation this round. Austria proposed continued ceasefire - keep A VEN away from TRI, F AEG away from ION. I pushed back asking for something tangible; Austria offered coordination against Turkey with F ION moving east. Turkey proposed I take GRE with F ION while they press Austria from SER/BUL - but Turkey's final message says A SER moves to GRE themselves, which directly conflicts with my plan to take GRE. This is a critical deception by Turkey - they want GRE for themselves, not for me. France confirmed focus on Germany (KIE to BER) and encouraged me to press Austria, promising F WES stays out of Tyrrhenian. Key deceptions: I told Austria I might hold F ION back from GRE if they offered something, then told France I was definitely moving F ION to GRE. Turkey is clearly planning to take GRE themselves with A SER while asking me to move to AEG/EAS instead - a transparent attempt to redirect me away from GRE. I must move F ION to GRE before Turkey's A SER gets there, or I lose the center entirely.

S1903M Intent going forward: Move F ION to GRE immediately - Turkey's A SER moving to GRE is the threat I must preempt. If I move to GRE and Turkey also moves A SER to GRE, we bounce and Austria keeps it - but at least Turkey doesn't get it. If Austria's F AEG supports GRE, I'm blocked. The risk is worth taking. Move A VEN to TYR as promised to Austria and France - this maintains flexibility and keeps Austria calm while I strike GRE. Intended moves: F ION - GRE, A VEN - TYR. This takes GRE from Austria (gaining a 4th center), positions me better in the eastern Med, and denies Turkey the easy grab. I am deceiving both Austria (who thinks I might hold back) and Turkey (who thinks I'll go to AEG/EAS). France is being used as cover - their pressure on Germany keeps Austria nervous. Long-term: with GRE secured, F GRE can threaten AEG or BUL next turn. A TYR threatens TRI or VIE. Italy needs to grow aggressively.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: I moved F ION to TUN (successful) and A VEN to TYR (successful). Austria attempted F AEG to BUL/SC but bounced against Turkey's A CON moving to BUL. Turkey took GRE with A SER. Russia moved F SEV to BLA and A UKR to RUM but bounced Austria's A BUD which also tried for RUM. France took BER with A KIE. Net result: I now hold TUN and TYR with no new centers. Turkey sits at 6 centers, France at 6, Russia at 6 - I am dangerously behind at 3.
Three-way negotiation with AUT, TUR, and FRA. AUT wants me to hold TYR or move to BOH, keep F TUN away from AEG, and coordinate against Turkey - offering TRI/VEN safety guarantee. I told AUT I'm moving A TYR to TRI (a stab warning/feint). TUR offered to support F TUN into ION if I move A TYR to TRI and leave GRE alone, promising TRI/VIE to me and BUD to them after AUT collapses. FRA confirmed F WES stays out of TYS and has no Mediterranean ambitions - focused on MUN. I told FRA I cannot move to MUN, need TYR for TRI pressure. Key deceptions: I told AUT I'm taking TRI 'to help them' which is obviously a stab. TUR's offer is tempting but they already betrayed me on GRE. FRA is reliable so far. My actual plan: F TUN to ION (everyone agrees on this), A TYR to TRI (stabbing AUT, aligning with TUR's offer). This gets me TRI as a new center and positions me for Adriatic dominance. TUR's deal is worth taking short-term - they're right that AUT cannot defend TRI while Turkey holds the Balkans.

F1903M Intent going forward: MOVES: F TUN - ION, A TYR - TRI. Rationale: Taking TRI gives me a 4th center and Adriatic access. TUR's offer aligns with this - they support ION move and I leave GRE alone this season. AUT is being stabbed - they have no fleet to defend TRI and their units are committed elsewhere (A BUD likely going for RUM, A VIE holding, F AEG threatening BUL). This is the bold aggressive play my personality demands. After taking TRI, I can build a fleet and push into the Adriatic/Aegean. Long-term I will eventually need to betray TUR too, but for now they are useful. FRA relationship maintained - they stay out of Mediterranean. Watch FRA carefully as they grow to 7 centers with MUN.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M: My F TUN moved to ION successfully, and A TYR attempted TRI but was bounced by AUT's A VIE moving to TRI (AUT moved VIE-TRI, BUD-SER). Russia swept the Balkans taking BUD and ANK, while TUR failed to take BUL (GRE-BUL bounced by AUT's AEG-BUL/SC). FRA took BER with A BER-MUN failing, GER took BER with KIE-BER. Net result: ITA still at 3 centers, AUT at 3 (VIE, TRI, SER), RUS dominant at 8 centers.
Coordinated with TUR who is desperate due to F ANK in their home center. TUR agreed to move A GRE-SER and A CON-BUL while I execute F ION-AEG and A TYR-TRI - a clean plan to collapse Austria. AUT offered ceasefire and asked me to move TYR-BOH instead, threatening to bounce TRI with VIE if I attack. I told AUT honestly I am attacking TRI and AEG - burning that bridge deliberately. FRA is cooperative, pressing Germany, staying out of Med. RUS received a neutrality proposal. Key deception: told TUR we'd divide Balkans with TRI/VIE to ITA and BUD/SER to TUR - I intend to take TRI this turn and position for more later. TUR's moves (GRE-SER, CON-BUL) help me by pressuring AUT from the east while I strike from the west.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the agreed plan with TUR: F ION-AEG (neutralizes AUT's F AEG, cannot be stopped), A TYR-TRI (AUT has A VIE which may try to bounce, but A SER is in Serbia and cannot defend TRI - VIE is the only defender). Risk: AUT bounces TRI with VIE. However TUR moving GRE-SER means AUT's A SER must defend or retreat, and VIE defending TRI means AUT cannot counterattack elsewhere. Even a bounce on TRI is acceptable if F ION takes AEG - that eliminates AUT's fleet and opens the eastern Med. Primary goal: take AEG with ION, take TRI with TYR. If TRI bounces, I still gain AEG and position for fall. Relationship with AUT is now openly hostile. TUR is a tactical ally for now but will be a rival once Austria falls.

RUS.personality: The Soviet Union under Stalin pursues ruthless self-interest, willing to deal with either democracies or fascists while building industrial and military might through brutal internal measures. Win at all costs.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Opened diplomacy on all fronts. Key outcomes: GER accepted non-aggression, staying out of PRU/WAR while moving west. ENG asked me to leave NWY open - I agreed to move F STP/SC to FIN instead, building goodwill. AUT is moving A VIE to GAL, directly conflicting with my plan to move A WAR to GAL - collision likely. AUT wants RUM for itself and GAL as buffer, which I rejected. TUR accepted a partial deal but confirmed F ANK moves to BLA, which threatens my Black Sea position - I told them RUM is mine and they nominally agreed but their fleet in BLA is dangerous. TUR's acceptance of my terms is likely deceptive given they want BLA. AUT-RUS tension is the most immediate problem - both moving to GAL means a bounce.
