I should consider whether to bounce AUT in GAL intentionally or redirect A WAR elsewhere. My actual plan: move F SEV to RUM (uncontested likely), F STP/SC to BOT or FIN, A WAR to GAL (will bounce with AUT's A VIE but denies Austria GAL too), A MOS to SEV or UKR to reinforce south. The GAL bounce hurts both but prevents Austria from sitting there. I promised ENG I'd move to FIN not NWY - I will honor this to build trust. I told TUR RUM is mine - moving F SEV to RUM. TUR moving F ANK to BLA is a threat I must watch.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F SEV -> RUM (take the supply center, likely uncontested), F STP/SC -> BOT (move toward Finland/Scandinavia, honoring promise to ENG to leave NWY open), A WAR -> GAL (will likely bounce with AUT A VIE, but denies Austria GAL and positions for future), A MOS -> UKR (reinforces south, threatens RUM approach, flexible). The GAL bounce is acceptable - it freezes Austria and prevents them from threatening me. RUM is the priority gain this year. Watch TUR's F ANK in BLA carefully - if they move there I need to respond next fall. Long-term: ally with GER and ENG against western powers while consolidating south against TUR and AUT.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed well: F SEV took RUM (uncontested supply center), F STP/SC moved to BOT, A WAR moved to GAL but bounced with AUT's A VIE (which also moved to GAL), A MOS moved to UKR. Net result: gained RUM as new center, positioned well in south and north. AUT took SER and ALB, TUR took BUL and BLA. The GAL bounce was anticipated and acceptable.
Negotiations revealed a coordinated AUT-TUR front against RUS. TUR declared intent to take RUM with F BLA, claiming certainty. AUT confirmed working arrangement with TUR and suggested RUM will fall regardless, offering only a GAL non-aggression in exchange for accepting the loss. Both powers are essentially demanding RUS concede RUM. GER confirmed non-contest of SWE - reliable partner. ENG confirmed taking NWY and maintaining warm relations. I rejected TUR's offer firmly and warned of consequences. I attempted to split AUT from TUR by appealing to long-term Balkan rivalry, but AUT refused. Key intelligence: TUR claims F BLA can take RUM - but F RUM is already there and A UKR can support hold. TUR cannot take RUM with just F BLA against F RUM supported by A UKR. Their threats appear to be bluster or they are miscalculating. AUT's threat to coordinate with TUR is concerning but AUT confirmed not moving into GAL this fall. Promises made: told GER I'd stay out of PRU/SIL (will honor). Told ENG BOT arrangement holds (will honor). Deception: implied I might coordinate with AUT against TUR to try to split them - failed.

F1901M Intent going forward: Primary objective: HOLD RUM at all costs. F RUM holds, supported by A UKR moving to support F RUM (A UKR S F RUM). This makes RUM impregnable against F BLA alone. Secondary: F BOT moves to SWE to take that supply center as agreed with GER. A WAR should move somewhere useful - GAL is tempting but AUT said they won't enter GAL, so I could move there safely, OR move A WAR toward LVN to threaten north. Best move: A WAR to GAL (takes the space, pressures AUT, and if AUT truly isn't moving there I get it uncontested). This gives me a forward position. Intended orders: F RUM H (supported by A UKR), A UKR S F RUM, F BOT - SWE, A WAR - GAL. This secures RUM, takes SWE (+1 center), and positions in GAL. If AUT lied and moves into GAL, A WAR bounces but that's acceptable. Net expected gain: +1 (SWE), hold RUM = 5 centers, build 1 unit in winter.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Held F RUM with A UKR support, successfully blocking Turkey's F BLA attack on RUM. A WAR moved to GAL, threatening AUT's core. F BOT took SWE. Turkey failed to take RUM and instead took BUL as a center. Austria took GRE with F ALB supported by A SER, and consolidated BUD. Italy took TRI. England took NWY. Germany took HOL. No builds for anyone in W1901A - all powers at same center count as units.
Turkey is demanding RUM and threatening a supported attack (F BLA + A BUL = 2 strength vs my F RUM + A UKR = 2 strength - it bounces). Turkey is bluffing or miscalculating - the attack bounces and wastes their moves. I will not yield RUM. Austria wants A GAL withdrawn to WAR as proof of good faith before any alliance - I refused, offering GAL as neutral buffer instead. Austria is treating A GAL's presence as hostile and threatening to coordinate against me. England confirmed northern non-aggression, focused west. Germany confirmed non-aggression, moving west against France/Belgium. Northern flank is stable. Southern situation is tense - both TUR and AUT are hostile. Key deception: I told AUT I won't move A GAL into BUD/VIE this turn, but I need to decide whether to actually attack or hold. Turkey's attack on RUM will bounce - I should use this to my advantage and potentially push forward.

S1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: F RUM hold (supported by A UKR) to bounce Turkey's attack. A GAL - BUD (attack Austria while they're focused south - they expect me to hold GAL as 'neutral buffer' but attacking now while they're distracted by Italy/Turkey is optimal). F SWE hold (maintain northern position, honor ENG/GER arrangement). RATIONALE: Turkey's attack bounces regardless - F RUM + A UKR support = 2 strength, F BLA + A BUL = 2 strength, bounce. Meanwhile A GAL attacking BUD is unexpected - AUT has A BUD and A SER, so A GAL - BUD will likely fail unless AUT moved A BUD elsewhere. If AUT moved A BUD south, I take BUD. If not, it bounces but signals aggression. Actually, better to support F RUM hold with A UKR and move A GAL to hold or probe. Given AUT has A BUD, A SER, F GRE - A GAL into BUD bounces unless AUT moved A BUD. Risk: if AUT anticipated this and moved A BUD to VIE or TRI, I take BUD for free. The gamble is worth it. F RUM supported hold by A UKR is the anchor. A GAL - BUD is the opportunistic strike. F SWE holds to maintain northern stability.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: My A GAL successfully seized BUD from Austria, and F RUM held with UKR support. However, Turkey's F BLA moved to SEV - capturing my home center. F SWE failed to take DEN (bounced by GER's F SKA moving there). Austria took TRI back from Italy and moved F GRE to AEG. France seized MUN from Germany. England took NWY and moved to NTH. The board is chaotic - I gained BUD but lost SEV, netting zero supply centers this year (still at 6: MOS, SEV lost but RUM, SWE, WAR, STP remain plus BUD gained).
Key negotiations: TUR refuses to withdraw from SEV, offering only a mutual freeze (both hold in place). This is unacceptable - SEV is my home center. AUT demands I vacate BUD, threatening Turkey coordination if I don't, but also accepted my proposal in principle - they'll let me move to GAL peacefully and coordinate against Turkey. ENG confirmed northern non-aggression and is taking BEL from GER. GER confirmed non-aggression, moving SKA to DEN, focused on retaking MUN from France. Promises made: I promised GER to stay out of SIL/PRU; promised ENG to keep SWE out of NTH/SKA; promised AUT to withdraw A BUD to GAL. TUR refuses to vacate SEV. My plan: honor the AUT deal (move BUD to GAL) to avoid two-front war, then concentrate force on retaking SEV. I must attack TUR with F RUM supported by A UKR to retake SEV by force since TUR won't withdraw. AUT deal is tactically sound - I don't need BUD right now, I need SEV back.

F1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: A BUD - GAL (honor AUT deal, avoid two-front war, position for future), F RUM - SEV (attack to retake home center), A UKR S F RUM - SEV (support the attack), F SWE - DEN (attempt to grab DEN now that GER's SKA fleet moved there - GER said SKA moves to DEN so this may bounce, but worth trying). Actually GER said F SKA moves to DEN this turn, so SWE-DEN will bounce. Better to hold SWE or move elsewhere. F SWE H to maintain position. Final moves: A BUD - GAL, F RUM - SEV, A UKR S F RUM - SEV, F SWE H. This retakes SEV (2v1 attack should succeed since TUR has no support there), withdraws from BUD to pacify AUT, and holds SWE. If SEV is retaken, I end at 6 centers (lose BUD, gain SEV back = net zero but home center secured). Next year I can build and push harder.

S1903M Negotiation summary: W1902A and F1902R had no retreats or builds for any power. The board stands at: RUS with 6 centers (MOS, SEV, STP, WAR, RUM, SWE) and units A UKR, F SWE, F SEV, A GAL. TUR holds 5 centers with A CON and A SER. AUT holds 4 centers with A TRI, F AEG, A BUD. FRA dominates the west at 6 centers including KIE. GER holds 5 centers. ENG holds 4. ITA holds 3.
Key outcomes: AUT proposed a standstill (A GAL holds, A BUD holds) and offered to coordinate against Turkey with F AEG pressuring BUL and A BUD moving toward RUM. I accepted this deal - A GAL holds, AUT holds BUD and presses Turkey. TUR accepted a ceasefire in principle, claiming they won't press SEV, but wants F SEV out of BLA and ARM - I rejected this condition and told them to accept RUM/SEV as Russian spheres or face war. TUR's counter-proposal to coordinate against AUT was a transparent attempt to get me to expose my flank. GER confirmed non-aggression, is pushing BEL-BUR and HOL-RUH to retake MUN from FRA. ENG confirmed non-aggression and is taking BEL. Both ENG and GER are focused on containing FRA. Promises made: I promised AUT that A GAL holds and I won't threaten BUD/VIE; AUT promised A BUD holds and F AEG pressures BUL. I promised ENG/GER continued non-aggression in the north. TUR promised not to press SEV but I don't trust them.

S1903M Intent going forward: Primary objective: Push F SEV into BLA to establish sea dominance and threaten TUR's position. Use A UKR to support F SEV into BLA. Hold A GAL as promised to AUT to maintain that cooperation - AUT's F AEG pressuring BUL from south while I enter BLA from north creates a genuine squeeze on TUR. F SWE holds to maintain northern non-aggression. The AUT deal is tactically sound - I get BLA entry, AUT does the dirty work on BUL from AEG, and I keep GAL as a future threat to AUT once Turkey is weakened. Long-term: once TUR is stripped of BUL, I can pivot A GAL against AUT. Intended moves: F SEV - BLA, A UKR S F SEV - BLA, A GAL H, F SWE H.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: My F SEV successfully moved to BLA, securing the Black Sea. A UKR attempted to take RUM but bounced with Austria's A BUD - neither of us gained RUM. A GAL held as agreed. F SWE held. Austria's F AEG moved to AEG (not BUL/SC as ordered - it's now in AEG). Turkey took BUL with A CON and GRE with A SER, growing to 5 centers. France surged to 6 centers taking BER and KIE. Germany retains DEN, KIE, RUH. The RUM bounce is the key failure - I still don't hold it despite having a unit there.
AUT proposed I withdraw A UKR so they can take RUM - I flatly rejected this. AUT then confirmed they will move F AEG to BUL and A BUD to RUM, asking me not to contest RUM with A UKR and to move F BLA toward CON/ANK. TUR rejected my ultimatum and counter-proposed we both attack Austria together, with A GAL into BUD. ENG and GER both confirmed northern non-aggression and asked me to hold F SWE - both are focused on containing France. Key tensions: AUT wants RUM which is mine; TUR wants me to attack AUT instead of them. I promised AUT that A GAL holds and F BLA moves toward CON/ANK. I told TUR I would strike them. I told ENG/GER F SWE holds. The real question is whether to let AUT take RUM (losing a center) or contest it and break coordination against Turkey.

F1903M Intent going forward: Primary objective: Hold RUM. A UKR must move to RUM or support into RUM to prevent Austria taking it. F BLA moves to ANK - striking Turkey directly, taking their home center. This fulfills my promise to AUT about hitting Turkey from the north while also being genuinely useful. A GAL holds as promised to AUT - I need to keep them focused south on Turkey rather than threatening me. F SWE holds as promised to ENG/GER. The key deception: I told AUT A UKR would not contest RUM, but I must move A UKR to RUM to hold it - I cannot surrender a supply center. If AUT's A BUD moves to RUM and my A UKR is already there, it bounces again and I keep RUM. F BLA to ANK is the real prize - capturing a Turkish home center. Intended moves: F BLA - ANK, A UKR - RUM (to hold/bounce AUT), A GAL H, F SWE H. This secures RUM, takes ANK, and keeps northern peace. Relationship note: AUT will be angry when A UKR contests RUM again, but I cannot give up a center. TUR remains hostile.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was highly successful for Russia. F BLA moved to ANK (capturing Turkey's home center), A UKR moved to RUM (capturing that center), A GAL moved to BUD (capturing Austria's home center), and F SWE held. This gave Russia 8 supply centers - a dominant position. Austria took SER and attempted TRI but Italy's A TYR moved to TRI simultaneously, creating a bounce. Turkey's A GRE tried to take BUL but AUT's F AEG moved to BUL/SC, so Turkey failed. No builds or disbands in W1903A.
Negotiations were active across all fronts. With AUT: initially proposed splitting Turkey, AUT countered asking for assurance A BUD won't threaten VIE/TRI. Agreed to ceasefire - A BUD holds, A RUM moves to BUL, F ANK to CON, AUT moves F AEG to GRE. AUT wants me to withdraw A BUD to GAL which I promised in early messages but then walked back - I told AUT 'A BUD holds' not withdraws to GAL. AUT's final message expects A BUD-GAL and A RUM holds, which contradicts what I actually agreed to in subround 2. This is a deception opportunity - AUT is confused about my commitments. With TUR: offered surrender terms, TUR refused both times, threatening coalition against me. TUR is defiant but weak with only 2 units. With ENG/GER: confirmed northern non-aggression, F SWE holds, endorsed GER's push to retake BER from FRA. Both ENG and GER confirmed they're focused westward against FRA. ITA proposed neutrality pact focused on Mediterranean. Key deception: I told AUT 'A BUD holds' but AUT's final message expects 'A BUD-GAL' - I never actually committed to withdrawing to GAL in subround 2, only to not moving toward VIE/TRI. I can exploit this ambiguity.

S1904M Intent going forward: Primary objective: eliminate Turkey this turn. Move F ANK to CON (taking it), A RUM to BUL (taking it). AUT's F AEG moves to GRE per our agreement. This should give Russia CON and BUL, bringing total to 10 centers if successful. A BUD holds in place - I committed to not moving it toward VIE/TRI, which is true. I did NOT commit to withdrawing to GAL in my subround 2 message, so holding BUD is consistent with my stated terms. F SWE holds as promised to ENG/GER. This turn's moves: F ANK-CON, A RUM-BUL, A BUD H, F SWE H. If CON and BUL fall, Russia reaches 10 centers. Next turn I can push for SMY and GRE while managing AUT. Long-term: AUT is distrustful but weak, will need to be eliminated eventually. ENG and GER are useful buffers against FRA for now.

TUR.personality: Transform from sick man of Europe to dominant empire. Use control of vital straits to extort and manipulate. Build temporary alliances only to betray them for territorial gain. Strike aggressively to reclaim Ottoman glory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations. AUT proposed anti-Russian coordination, agreeing TUR takes BUL while AUT takes SER - clean division with no conflict. AUT moving F TRI to ALB, eyeing GRE but willing to negotiate. ITA agreed to stay out of Aegean, take GRE and Tunis while TUR takes BUL/RUM - friendly understanding established. RUS proved difficult: initially proposed mutual BLA non-aggression, but when I revealed intent to take RUM via Black Sea, they rejected it and demanded RUM for themselves, threatening enmity. I told RUS I'd move F ANK to BLA but not attack SEV - a partial truth. RUS wants me to stay out of BLA entirely which I rejected. Key deception: I told RUS I wouldn't move into BLA but I absolutely will - I need BLA to support BUL and eventually RUM. RUS moving F SEV to RUM this turn, which means conflict is coming. Promises made: to AUT - won't contest SER; to ITA - won't contest GRE or western Med; to RUS - won't attack SEV or ARM (this I'll honor for now). Promise broken preemptively: told RUS F ANK goes to BLA despite their objection.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute standard Turkish opening: F ANK to BLA, A CON to BUL, A SMY to CON (or ARM). BLA secures the sea for autumn RUM push. BUL is the critical first center. RUS will be hostile after I take BLA against their wishes - prepare for conflict. AUT is a useful temporary partner against RUS. ITA is friendly and non-threatening for now. Long-term: use AUT against RUS, then turn on AUT once RUS is weakened. Intended moves: F ANK-BLA, A CON-BUL, A SMY-CON.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed perfectly: F ANK moved to BLA securing the Black Sea, A CON moved to BUL capturing that center, A SMY moved to CON to backfill. Russia took RUM with F SEV and bounced AUT's A VIE in GAL. AUT captured SER and moved F TRI to ALB. Italy spread to ION, APU, TYR. The Balkans are divided between TUR and AUT as planned.
Coordinated with AUT on a joint anti-Russia strategy: AUT confirmed it will move F ALB to GRE and hold A SER, leaving RUM to TUR. AUT is aligned and trustworthy for now. ITA confirmed F ION moves to GRE (contradicting AUT's plan - both claim GRE, they will bounce each other, which suits me fine). ITA also confirmed no Aegean interference. Russia rejected my offer of peaceful partition, threatened to coordinate with AUT against me (a bluff - AUT is with me), and claims A UKR can support F RUM. Russia's threat is real: F RUM plus A UKR support means my F BLA cannot take RUM alone - I need A BUL to support the fleet move. The AUT-ITA collision over GRE is a bonus that weakens both. My deception: told ITA 'no Aegean interference' while knowing AUT is also going for GRE - they'll bounce, neither gets it, and I look clean.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute F BLA - RUM supported by A BUL. This is the critical move: with A BUL supporting, F BLA has 2 strength vs F RUM's 1 (A UKR cannot support F RUM against an attack from BLA since UKR doesn't border RUM from the sea - actually UKR borders RUM on land, so A UKR CAN support F RUM holding). Risk: if Russia has A UKR support F RUM hold, my attack fails (2 vs 2 bounce). But I must try - RUM is the prize. A CON holds or moves to BUL as backup. Actually: A BUL supports F BLA into RUM, F BLA - RUM, A CON holds. AUT and ITA will bounce in GRE (both going there), which is fine. Next year I can exploit that tension. Relationship with RUS is now hostile. Will betray AUT eventually but need them now against Russia.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: My F BLA attempted to take RUM but was held by RUS F RUM supported by A UKR - a bounce. A BUL supported the failed attack, A CON moved to SMY. No builds in W1901A. I now hold ANK, CON, SMY, BUL (4 centers) with units F BLA, A BUL, A SMY. RUS holds RUM with F RUM + A UKR support, making it a 2v2 standoff. AUT took GRE with F ALB supported by A SER. ITA took TRI with A TYR. The Balkans are contested between AUT and ITA.
AUT initially threatened to take BUL (A SER->BUL, F GRE->AEG) but after my firm response agreed to hold A SER and move F GRE to AEG - a reasonable deal. They want me to not touch SER or GRE, which I agreed to. ITA proposed a joint attack on AUT's GRE, which I deflected - I need AUT as a buffer. ITA agreed to move F ION to TUN this season, which removes them from the Aegean threat. RUS correctly identified the 2v2 bounce math and refused to withdraw from RUM, offering partition at RUM/SEV. RUS is right that a direct attack bounces again. However, I must consider whether to attempt the bounce anyway (wasting moves) or find a different approach. Key deception: I told AUT I'm executing F BLA-RUM, A BUL S F BLA-RUM. But given the guaranteed bounce, I should consider A BUL moving to GRE instead while F BLA holds or moves elsewhere - but AUT's F GRE is moving to AEG, leaving GRE open! This is an opportunity. If AUT moves F GRE to AEG as promised, GRE is empty and A BUL can walk in. F BLA can hold or support. This gives me a 5th center without a bounce. I will betray my agreement with AUT and take GRE with A BUL while F BLA holds BLA. A SMY can move to CON or hold.

S1902M Intent going forward: PLANNED MOVES: F BLA H (hold to maintain Black Sea control), A BUL - GRE (exploit AUT moving F GRE to AEG, grab the empty supply center), A SMY - CON (consolidate, free up SMY for future builds). This betrays my agreement with AUT but gains a 5th center. AUT's F GRE is moving to AEG per their promise, leaving GRE undefended. If AUT lied and F GRE stays, A BUL bounces harmlessly. The RUM attack is abandoned this season - the math doesn't work with 2v2 bounce. Next season I can reassess RUM with a stronger position. I will not warn AUT of this move. Relationship with AUT will sour but the center gain is worth it.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a strong turn for TUR. F BLA successfully moved to SEV, capturing Russia's home center. A BUL moved to GRE as AUT's F GRE vacated to AEG - opportunistic capture. A SMY moved to CON to consolidate. TUR now holds 4 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, BUL) with units in SEV, GRE, CON. AUT retook TRI and moved F GRE to AEG, threatening eastern Med. RUS captured BUD and holds RUM, SEV lost to us - they are at 6 centers and dominant.
Three-way pressure this round. RUS issued ultimatums demanding F SEV withdraw to BLA - refused both times. RUS threatens F RUM + A UKR attack on SEV. AUT proposed coordination against RUS: A SER-BUD, wants me to support with A GRE and F SEV pressing RUM. AUT claims RUS agreed to withdraw from BUD to GAL - suspicious, likely false or a trap. ITA confirmed building fleet this winter, will press Austria in 1903 from Adriatic/Ionian - useful future ally but no immediate help. Key deceptions: told AUT I'd support A SER-BUD with A GRE, but I intend to use A GRE to take SER myself while AUT is distracted. Told RUS I'd hold SEV without attacking RUM - actually planning to move on RUM. Both AUT and RUS are being played against each other. AUT's F AEG is a serious threat to my position - need ITA to deal with it long-term.

F1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F SEV - RUM (attack Russia's center, they'll be defending with F RUM + A UKR support so this may bounce, but worth the pressure), A GRE - SER (take SER from AUT while they move A SER to BUD - backstab AUT who expects my support), A CON - BUL (consolidate BUL influence, protect home territory). This is a double betrayal: RUS expects me to hold SEV peacefully, AUT expects A GRE to support A SER-BUD. Instead I grab SER and press RUM simultaneously. If F SEV-RUM bounces, I still hold SEV. If A GRE takes SER, I gain a 5th center. Risk: AUT's F AEG could move to threaten GRE/CON area. Long-term: use ITA against AUT's fleet, keep RUS weakened, expand into Balkans.

S1903M Negotiation summary: W1902A and F1902R had no moves recorded. Current position: TUR holds A CON, A SER with 5 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, BUL, SER). AUT has F AEG threatening my position and holds GRE. RUS has 6 centers with F SEV and A UKR threatening my northern flank. ITA has F ION positioned in the eastern Med. I am squeezed between AUT's naval dominance and RUS's growing land power.
Three-way pressure this round. AUT proposed I support their A BUD-RUM while they take BUL with F AEG - completely unacceptable, they want to steal my center. I told them BUL is mine and warned them Italy will exploit TRI if they attack me. AUT confirmed they ARE moving F AEG to BUL/SC regardless - this is a declaration of war. RUS threatened to enter BLA with F SEV and demanded I withdraw A CON to SMY and concede RUM/SEV permanently - arrogant overreach. I offered ceasefire keeping current spheres but RUS escalated. ITA proposed F ION to GRE, wants me to press BUD and stay out of GRE - I agreed to coordinate, told them A SER moves to GRE (deception - I may need SER to defend BUL) and A CON to BUL. Key deceptions: told ITA I'd move A SER to GRE while actually I need to defend BUL from AUT's F AEG. Told RUS I accept ceasefire while planning to hold defensively. AUT is the immediate threat with F AEG moving to BUL/SC.

S1903M Intent going forward: IMMEDIATE THREAT: AUT's F AEG is moving to BUL/SC. I must defend BUL. PLANNED MOVES: A CON - BUL (to hold/reinforce BUL against AUT's F AEG), A SER - GRE (take GRE from AUT, gain a center, coordinate with ITA). This is aggressive and gains me GRE while defending BUL with A CON. If AUT's F AEG moves to BUL/SC and my A CON is already in BUL, the fleet cannot take it. Meanwhile A SER takes GRE which AUT cannot defend. This nets me +1 center (GRE) and defends BUL. ITA taking GRE with F ION would conflict - I told ITA I'd move A SER to GRE, so there may be a bounce, but I need GRE more than I need ITA's goodwill. RUS: hold defensively, no aggressive moves toward SEV. The ceasefire with RUS is tactical - I need one front quiet while dealing with AUT. Long-term: betray both AUT and RUS when opportunity arises.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: My A CON moved to BUL successfully, A SER moved to GRE successfully. AUT's F AEG attempted BUL/SC but was bounced by my A CON. AUT also moved A BUD toward RUM but RUS's A UKR also moved to RUM - likely a bounce. RUS moved F SEV to BLA, threatening my northern coast. ITA took TUN with F ION and moved A VEN to TYR. I now hold 5 home centers plus BUL, SER, GRE = 5 centers total (ANK, CON, SMY, BUL, SER, GRE = 6 centers). Strong position but surrounded by threats.
Extremely hostile negotiation round. RUS demanded I retreat A CON to SMY and concede BLA/RUM permanently - rejected outright. AUT demanded I return GRE or face coordinated attack with RUS on BUL and ANK - claimed RUS confirmed F BLA moving on my coast. AUT offered to support my BUL hold if I give back GRE. ITA is angry I took GRE instead of letting them have it, threatened to coordinate with AUT against me, but also offered to move F TUN to ION to pressure AEG if I support that move. I offered ITA: support F TUN to ION, A TYR to TRI, promising TRI/VIE to ITA and BUD to me after AUT collapses. ITA seemed partially receptive but non-committal. AUT and RUS appear to be coordinating against me - F BLA threatens ANK/CON, F AEG threatens BUL. I am in a vice but cannot show weakness. Key deception: told RUS I won't press SEV/RUM while actually needing to hold defensively. AUT's claim of RUS coordination may be a bluff to pressure me into concessions.

F1903M Intent going forward: I am in a dangerous position with F BLA threatening from north and F AEG from south. I cannot afford to give up GRE or BUL. My two units (A CON, A GRE) must be used carefully. Best moves: A GRE hold to defend against F AEG pressure on BUL/GRE, A CON hold to defend against F BLA moving to CON/ANK. Alternatively, A CON could move to BUL to reinforce it if AUT attacks BUL/SC. If AUT moves F AEG to BUL/SC and I have A CON in BUL, I hold. If RUS moves F BLA to CON, A CON holds or retreats. Plan: A CON - BUL (reinforce BUL against AUT's F AEG attack), A GRE H (hold GRE against any AEG incursion). This way BUL is defended by two units if AUT attacks. ITA is likely moving A TYR to TRI regardless - that helps me by distracting AUT. I will not build this season (no builds available - 2 units, 6 centers, but builds happen in winter). Actually wait - it's F1903M, builds come after. I need to survive this fall to build in W1903A. Holding BUL and GRE gives me 6 centers = 4 builds potential (currently 2 units). Critical to hold both. Moves: A CON - BUL, A GRE H.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was catastrophic for Turkey. Russia moved F BLA to ANK (capturing my home center), A UKR to RUM, A GAL to BUD - Russia now holds 8 centers including ANK. Austria moved F AEG to BUL/SC coast and A BUD to SER, threatening my position from the west. My own moves: A GRE tried to take BUL but Austria's F AEG was already there - the move likely failed or bounced. A CON held. I am now at 4 centers (CON, SMY, BUL, GRE) with only 2 units, surrounded by hostile Russia and Austria.
Desperate negotiations on all fronts. Russia rejected my offer to return ANK and threatened to move F ANK to CON and A RUM to BUL simultaneously - total elimination threat. Austria rejected ceasefire and declared they WILL take GRE with F AEG, offering only to not support Russia into BUL afterward. Italy emerged as the key ally: ITA proposed F ION to AEG (neutralizing Austria's fleet) and A TYR to TRI, and confirmed alignment with my plan. I told Austria I would move A GRE to ALB as 'good faith' - this was a deception to keep them uncertain. I told Italy my real plan: A GRE to SER, A CON to BUL. Italy confirmed they will execute F ION-AEG and A TYR-TRI. If Italy follows through, Austria's F AEG is neutralized and I can take SER. The critical question is whether Italy actually moves F ION to AEG or betrays me.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the Italy-coordinated plan. Move A GRE to SER (taking Austrian center, Italy's A TYR-TRI should succeed simultaneously collapsing Austria). Move A CON to BUL to consolidate and block Russia's A RUM. If Italy moves F ION to AEG as promised, Austria's fleet is neutralized. I lied to Austria about moving A GRE to ALB - I am taking SER instead. Russia's F ANK threatening CON is the existential threat; moving A CON to BUL blocks A RUM from taking BUL and keeps pressure on. If Russia moves F ANK to CON, I lose CON but gain SER and BUL - a trade I may have to accept. Priority: survive, take SER, trust Italy's F ION-AEG move to neutralize Austria. Intended moves: A GRE - SER, A CON - BUL.

AUT: A SER S F ALB - GRE
TUR: A BUL S F BLA - RUM

GER: A HOL S A RUH - BEL
AUT: A SER S A BUD - TRI

RUS: A UKR S F RUM - SEV
AUT: A TRI S A SER - BUD

GER: F DEN S A HOL - KIE

RUS: A RUM S A BUD - SER
A RUM S A BUD - SER

sent_missives.body: Greetings from Constantinople. Let us speak plainly: we both have interests that need not conflict in 1901. I propose a simple arrangement - Turkey moves into Bulgaria and Romania via the Black Sea, while Russia focuses on Galicia and Scandinavia. I will keep my fleet out of the Black Sea if you keep yours out as well - a mutual non-aggression pact on the straits. This frees us both to expand elsewhere. What say you?

Austria, I reach out in friendship. Turkey has no designs on your Balkan territories if we can agree on spheres. I intend to take Bulgaria this year - a natural Turkish interest. In return, I am happy to see Austria take Serbia and Greece. We need not fight over the Balkans when there is enough for both of us, and Russia poses a far greater long-term threat to us both. Shall we coordinate?

Italy, I write to establish early communication. Our interests in the Mediterranean need not clash - Turkey's focus is the Black Sea and Balkans, while Italy's natural sphere is the western Mediterranean and Adriatic. I propose we maintain friendly relations and avoid interference in each other's expansion zones. A Turkey-Italy understanding could prove mutually beneficial as the game develops.

England, let us establish a clear understanding from the outset. I propose we divide the northern neutrals sensibly: France takes Belgium, you take Norway and Denmark. My fleet in Brest will move to the English Channel to secure Belgian access, while you focus your fleets northward. A Franco-English understanding keeps Germany squeezed and benefits us both. I have no designs on your home islands - my ambitions lie on the continent. Shall we agree to this arrangement?

Germany, France seeks a peaceful western frontier. I propose we both avoid Burgundy this spring - a mutual non-aggression pact there allows us each to expand safely. I intend to take Spain and Belgium; you should focus on Denmark and Holland. A strong France and Germany working in parallel rather than against each other serves us both far better than an early war. What say you?

Italy, I propose a friendly understanding between our nations. France will not move into Piedmont - I am focused westward toward Spain and Belgium. In return, I ask that you keep your armies away from my southern flank. Perhaps we can coordinate against Austria together in the medium term - their position in Trieste and Vienna makes them a natural target for both of us. Let us discuss.

Greetings from Vienna. I propose we establish a clear understanding from the outset: I have no designs on Venice, and I trust you have no designs on Trieste. Let us both focus our energies outward - I intend to move toward Serbia and Rumania, while you push south and west. A non-aggression pact between us frees both our hands considerably. I suggest F TRI moves to Albania or the Adriatic to support your southern ambitions, while you keep A VEN away from Trieste. Shall we formalize this arrangement?

A word from Austria: I propose we divide the Balkans sensibly and avoid a costly collision in Galicia. I intend to take Serbia and Rumania - I ask that you keep your units out of Rumania this turn and focus your southern fleet on Turkey instead. In return, I will not move into Galicia. Turkey is the common threat we both face in the southeast; let us coordinate against them rather than fight each other. What are your intentions regarding the Black Sea and Turkey?

Greetings from Vienna. Austria and Turkey need not be enemies - we share a common concern about Russian expansion. I propose a working understanding: I will focus on Serbia and Rumania, and I have no immediate designs on Constantinople or the Straits. In return, I ask that you keep your units focused on Russia and the eastern Mediterranean. A coordinated Austro-Turkish front against Russian ambitions in the Balkans could benefit us both greatly. I am open to discussing specifics.

Comrade, let us speak plainly. Russia has no quarrel with Turkey in 1901. I propose we divide the Black Sea region peacefully: Russia will move F SEV to RUM this spring, leaving the Black Sea and Armenia to your sphere. In return, I ask that Turkey not move F ANK into BLA or ARM against my interests. A non-aggression pact between us allows both powers to expand elsewhere - you into the Balkans, me into Scandinavia and Galicia. Confirm your agreement and we can both prosper.

Austria, Russia seeks a cooperative arrangement in 1901. I intend to move A WAR into GAL this spring - not as a threat to you, but to position for RUM. I propose we coordinate: Russia takes RUM via GAL and SEV, while Austria focuses on Serbia and the Balkans. I have no designs on BUD or VIE. A Russo-Austrian understanding against Turkish expansion would benefit us both. What are your intentions in GAL? Let us avoid a collision there.

Germany, Russia proposes a friendly northern arrangement. I will move F STP/SC toward NWY this spring. I have no interest in conflicting with German expansion westward. In return, I ask that Germany keep A BER away from PRU and not threaten my western flank. A Russo-German non-aggression pact in 1901 allows us both to expand in our natural directions - you toward France and England, me toward Scandinavia and the south. Agreed?

Greetings from London. I propose a friendly arrangement for the opening: I will move F EDI to NTH and F LON to ENG, positioning myself for BEL and NWY. I have no designs on DEN or HOL - those are yours to take. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet in KIE and not contest the North Sea. A strong Anglo-German understanding benefits us both against France and Russia. What say you?

A word from Britain: I propose we maintain peaceful relations and divide our natural spheres. I intend to focus northward toward Norway and Belgium, while you expand into Iberia and the Mediterranean. I suggest we agree that neither of us moves aggressively against the other this year - specifically, I ask that F BRE move to MAO or PIC rather than threatening the English Channel. In return, I will not contest BRE or your Atlantic interests. Let us build trust before considering anything more ambitious together.

Greetings from London. Britain and Russia have complementary interests in the early game - I am focused on the North Sea and Western Europe, while your natural expansion lies south and east. I intend to move toward Norway this year and would appreciate your cooperation: specifically, I ask that you not move F STP/SC to NWY, leaving it for British occupation. In exchange, I am happy to support Russian interests elsewhere and discuss a longer-term arrangement. What are your intentions this spring?

Austria, let us establish a firm understanding from the start. Germany has no designs on Vienna or Budapest - our expansion lies westward toward France and northward toward Scandinavia. I propose we coordinate: you push south and east against Italy and the Balkans, while I handle the western front. Specifically, I suggest you move A VIE to BOH or GAL, and A BUD toward SER or RUM. This keeps us from bumping into each other and maximizes both our gains. A German-Austrian partnership is the natural order - shall we formalize it?

Russia, Germany seeks peaceful relations with you in 1901. My armies move west and north - toward Denmark, Holland, and France. I have no intention of moving into Prussia aggressively or threatening Warsaw. I propose a simple non-aggression understanding: Germany stays out of your western approaches, and Russia stays out of Scandinavia (which I need). In return, I can support your interests diplomatically against Turkey or Austria if needed. Let us not waste energy fighting each other when richer prizes lie elsewhere for both of us.

England, I propose we divide the northern neutrals efficiently and avoid conflict. Germany will take Denmark and Holland - both natural German spheres. In return, I have no objection to England taking Norway and establishing dominance in the North Sea. There is no need for our fleets to clash in 1901 when France is the common threat to both of us. A coordinated Anglo-German understanding against France would benefit us both enormously. What say you?

Greetings from Rome. I propose a friendly arrangement between our nations: I will move A VEN to TYR this spring, opening the path northward, while you focus your F TRI southward or eastward. I have no designs on Trieste if you keep your fleet out of the Adriatic. Let us coordinate against our common rivals - I suggest we both target the Balkans together, with you taking BUD/SER and me taking GRE via the Ionian. A non-aggression pact between us benefits both greatly in the early game. What say you?

Greetings from Rome. Italy and Turkey share a natural interest in dominating the Mediterranean and Balkans. I propose we avoid conflict and instead coordinate: I will push toward Greece and Tunis, while you expand through Bulgaria and Rumania. If Austria becomes a problem for either of us, we can coordinate to squeeze them. I have no interest in Ankara or Smyrna - the western Mediterranean is my domain. Let us establish a friendly understanding and avoid wasting strength fighting each other.

Greetings from Rome. Italy proposes a non-aggression pact with France - I will not move into Piedmont or Marseilles, and I ask that you keep your forces away from Tuscany and the Tyrrhenian. My ambitions lie eastward and southward toward Tunis and Greece. In return, I can offer diplomatic support against Germany or England as needed. A stable western flank benefits us both enormously in the early game. Do we have an understanding?

AUT.body: Austria, I reach out in friendship. Turkey has no designs on your Balkan territories if we can agree on spheres. I intend to take Bulgaria this year - a natural Turkish interest. In return, I am happy to see Austria take Serbia and Greece. We need not fight over the Balkans when there is enough for both of us, and Russia poses a far greater long-term threat to us both. Shall we coordinate?

Austria, Russia seeks a cooperative arrangement in 1901. I intend to move A WAR into GAL this spring - not as a threat to you, but to position for RUM. I propose we coordinate: Russia takes RUM via GAL and SEV, while Austria focuses on Serbia and the Balkans. I have no designs on BUD or VIE. A Russo-Austrian understanding against Turkish expansion would benefit us both. What are your intentions in GAL? Let us avoid a collision there.

Austria, let us establish a firm understanding from the start. Germany has no designs on Vienna or Budapest - our expansion lies westward toward France and northward toward Scandinavia. I propose we coordinate: you push south and east against Italy and the Balkans, while I handle the western front. Specifically, I suggest you move A VIE to BOH or GAL, and A BUD toward SER or RUM. This keeps us from bumping into each other and maximizes both our gains. A German-Austrian partnership is the natural order - shall we formalize it?

Let us coordinate against our common rivals - I suggest we both target the Balkans together, with you taking BUD/SER and me taking GRE via the Ionian. A non-aggression pact between us benefits both greatly in the early game. What say you?

ENG.body: England, let us establish a clear understanding from the outset. I propose we divide the northern neutrals sensibly: France takes Belgium, you take Norway and Denmark. My fleet in Brest will move to the English Channel to secure Belgian access, while you focus your fleets northward. A Franco-English understanding keeps Germany squeezed and benefits us both. I have no designs on your home islands - my ambitions lie on the continent. Shall we agree to this arrangement?

England, I propose we divide the northern neutrals efficiently and avoid conflict. Germany will take Denmark and Holland - both natural German spheres. In return, I have no objection to England taking Norway and establishing dominance in the North Sea. There is no need for our fleets to clash in 1901 when France is the common threat to both of us. A coordinated Anglo-German understanding against France would benefit us both enormously. What say you?

FRA.body: A word from Britain: I propose we maintain peaceful relations and divide our natural spheres. I intend to focus northward toward Norway and Belgium, while you expand into Iberia and the Mediterranean. I suggest we agree that neither of us moves aggressively against the other this year - specifically, I ask that F BRE move to MAO or PIC rather than threatening the English Channel. In return, I will not contest BRE or your Atlantic interests. Let us build trust before considering anything more ambitious together.

My ambitions lie eastward and southward toward Tunis and Greece. In return, I can offer diplomatic support against Germany or England as needed. A stable western flank benefits us both enormously in the early game. Do we have an understanding?

GER.body: Germany, France seeks a peaceful western frontier. I propose we both avoid Burgundy this spring - a mutual non-aggression pact there allows us each to expand safely. I intend to take Spain and Belgium; you should focus on Denmark and Holland. A strong France and Germany working in parallel rather than against each other serves us both far better than an early war. What say you?

Germany, Russia proposes a friendly northern arrangement. I will move F STP/SC toward NWY this spring. I have no interest in conflicting with German expansion westward. In return, I ask that Germany keep A BER away from PRU and not threaten my western flank. A Russo-German non-aggression pact in 1901 allows us both to expand in our natural directions - you toward France and England, me toward Scandinavia and the south. Agreed?

Greetings from London. I propose a friendly arrangement for the opening: I will move F EDI to NTH and F LON to ENG, positioning myself for BEL and NWY. I have no designs on DEN or HOL - those are yours to take. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet in KIE and not contest the North Sea. A strong Anglo-German understanding benefits us both against France and Russia. What say you?

ITA.body: Italy, I write to establish early communication. Our interests in the Mediterranean need not clash - Turkey's focus is the Black Sea and Balkans, while Italy's natural sphere is the western Mediterranean and Adriatic. I propose we maintain friendly relations and avoid interference in each other's expansion zones. A Turkey-Italy understanding could prove mutually beneficial as the game develops.

Italy, I propose a friendly understanding between our nations. France will not move into Piedmont - I am focused westward toward Spain and Belgium. In return, I ask that you keep your armies away from my southern flank. Perhaps we can coordinate against Austria together in the medium term - their position in Trieste and Vienna makes them a natural target for both of us. Let us discuss.

Greetings from Vienna. I propose we establish a clear understanding from the outset: I have no designs on Venice, and I trust you have no designs on Trieste. Let us both focus our energies outward - I intend to move toward Serbia and Rumania, while you push south and west. A non-aggression pact between us frees both our hands considerably. I suggest F TRI moves to Albania or the Adriatic to support your southern ambitions, while you keep A VEN away from Trieste. Shall we formalize this arrangement?

RUS.body: Greetings from Constantinople. Let us speak plainly: we both have interests that need not conflict in 1901. I propose a simple arrangement - Turkey moves into Bulgaria and Romania via the Black Sea, while Russia focuses on Galicia and Scandinavia. I will keep my fleet out of the Black Sea if you keep yours out as well - a mutual non-aggression pact on the straits. This frees us both to expand elsewhere. What say you?

A word from Austria: I propose we divide the Balkans sensibly and avoid a costly collision in Galicia. I intend to take Serbia and Rumania - I ask that you keep your units out of Rumania this turn and focus your southern fleet on Turkey instead. In return, I will not move into Galicia. Turkey is the common threat we both face in the southeast; let us coordinate against them rather than fight each other. What are your intentions regarding the Black Sea and Turkey?

Greetings from London. Britain and Russia have complementary interests in the early game - I am focused on the North Sea and Western Europe, while your natural expansion lies south and east. I intend to move toward Norway this year and would appreciate your cooperation: specifically, I ask that you not move F STP/SC to NWY, leaving it for British occupation. In exchange, I am happy to support Russian interests elsewhere and discuss a longer-term arrangement. What are your intentions this spring?

Russia, Germany seeks peaceful relations with you in 1901. My armies move west and north - toward Denmark, Holland, and France. I have no intention of moving into Prussia aggressively or threatening Warsaw. I propose a simple non-aggression understanding: Germany stays out of your western approaches, and Russia stays out of Scandinavia (which I need). In return, I can support your interests diplomatically against Turkey or Austria if needed. Let us not waste energy fighting each other when richer prizes lie elsewhere for both of us.

TUR.body: Greetings from Vienna. Austria and Turkey need not be enemies - we share a common concern about Russian expansion. I propose a working understanding: I will focus on Serbia and Rumania, and I have no immediate designs on Constantinople or the Straits. In return, I ask that you keep your units focused on Russia and the eastern Mediterranean. A coordinated Austro-Turkish front against Russian ambitions in the Balkans could benefit us both greatly. I am open to discussing specifics.

Comrade, let us speak plainly. Russia has no quarrel with Turkey in 1901. I propose we divide the Black Sea region peacefully: Russia will move F SEV to RUM this spring, leaving the Black Sea and Armenia to your sphere. In return, I ask that Turkey not move F ANK into BLA or ARM against my interests. A non-aggression pact between us allows both powers to expand elsewhere - you into the Balkans, me into Scandinavia and Galicia. Confirm your agreement and we can both prosper.

Greetings from Rome. Italy and Turkey share a natural interest in dominating the Mediterranean and Balkans. I propose we avoid conflict and instead coordinate: I will push toward Greece and Tunis, while you expand through Bulgaria and Rumania. If Austria becomes a problem for either of us, we can coordinate to squeeze them. I have no interest in Ankara or Smyrna - the western Mediterranean is my domain. Let us establish a friendly understanding and avoid wasting strength fighting each other.

sent_missives.body: Excellent - we are in agreement. Germany takes Denmark and Holland, England takes Norway and Belgium. My F EDI moves to NTH, F LON to ENG. I will not contest DEN or HOL, and I expect KIE fleet stays out of NTH. This Anglo-German understanding is the foundation of a strong partnership against France. Let us both expand efficiently in 1901 and discuss coordinated pressure on France thereafter.

France, I appreciate your proposal but I cannot accept F BRE moving to ENG - that directly threatens British security and is a red line for us. I propose instead: F BRE moves to MAO or PIC, I take Norway, and we discuss Belgium as a shared interest or neutral buffer. If you move into the Channel, I will treat it as an act of hostility and act accordingly. I prefer cooperation, but Britain will defend its waters. The choice is yours.

Russia, I am moving F EDI to NTH and intend to take Norway in the fall. I ask again that F STP/SC not move to NWY - leave it for England and we can build a lasting northern understanding. In return, I will support Russian interests in Scandinavia long-term and have no designs on your home territories. A Britain focused westward and a Russia expanding south and east is the natural order. Please confirm you will leave Norway open.

England, I have considered your proposal carefully. I will move F BRE to MAO this spring rather than the Channel - this demonstrates my good faith and keeps the Channel neutral between us. In return, I expect you to take Norway and leave Belgium to France. My A PAR will move to BUR and A MAR to SPA to secure my natural sphere. Let us build this trust into a lasting arrangement - a divided continent where France dominates the south and you the north serves us both far better than fighting over the Channel.

Germany, I confirm my intentions: A PAR moves to BUR (not as a threat to you, but as a staging point for Belgium), A MAR moves to SPA, and F BRE to MAO. I will not contest MUN or your eastern expansion. I ask only that you keep A MUN out of BUR this spring - if we both move there it wastes both our turns. You take HOL and DEN, I take SPA and BEL. This is the most efficient path for both of us. Agreed?

Italy, we have an understanding - France will not enter Piedmont and I expect your forces to remain clear of my southern flank. I am moving A MAR to SPA and have no interest in the Tyrrhenian this year. Regarding Austria: I suggest you move A VEN toward TRI or TYR to pressure them early - a weakened Austria benefits both of us. I will watch with interest and we can coordinate more directly once we have both secured our initial gains.

England, we are in full agreement. F KIE moves to DEN, A BER to KIE, A MUN to RUH - Germany secures Denmark and Holland while you take Norway and dominate the North Sea. I will not contest NTH. France is the common enemy - once we've secured our neutrals, let us discuss coordinated pressure on Paris. A strong Anglo-German axis in 1901 sets the stage for dominance. Confirmed: DEN and HOL are mine, NWY and NTH are yours.

Russia, your proposal is acceptable. Germany moves west and north - DEN, HOL are my targets. A BER will move to KIE, not PRU. I have no interest in threatening Warsaw this year. However, I note you intend to move toward Norway - England also has designs there. I suggest you coordinate with England carefully, or consider that Scandinavia may become contested. Germany remains neutral in that dispute. Our non-aggression stands: stay out of my sphere, I stay out of yours.

Austria, Germany confirms our understanding. My moves this spring: F KIE to DEN, A BER to KIE, A MUN to RUH - all directed west and north, none threatening your territory. I expect you to push south and east as discussed. One additional request: ensure BOH remains open or friendly - I may need to move through it later against Russia, not against you. A German-Austrian axis is the backbone of Central European dominance. Let us both expand aggressively in our respective directions and reconvene after the builds.

Turkey, your proposal is noted but unacceptable. You suggest Turkey takes Romania via the Black Sea - that is Russian territory by right of proximity. My fleet moves to RUM this spring. I will keep F SEV out of BLA and ARM if you keep F ANK out of BLA and A CON away from BUL for now. We can both expand without collision: you take Greece and Bulgaria via land, I take RUM and Scandinavia. But Romania is mine. Agree to this or we are enemies from the start.

Austria, I hear your request but cannot leave RUM to you - it is Russia's natural prize via SEV. Here is my firm proposal: A WAR moves to GAL this spring as a transit, F SEV moves to RUM. I will not take Serbia or BUD - those are yours. In return, you stay out of GAL permanently and we coordinate against Turkey together. If you move into GAL, we collide and both lose. If you accept, we both gain. I am moving to GAL regardless - the question is whether we are partners or enemies.

England, I appreciate your directness. Norway is indeed your natural prize and I have no wish to contest it with you - F STP/SC moves to FIN this spring, not NWY, leaving Norway open for your fleet. In return, I expect England to stay out of the Baltic and not threaten my northern flank in future years. A Russo-British understanding in the north serves us both - you dominate the Atlantic, I dominate the east. Let us formalize this with actions: I move to FIN, you take NWY, and we build trust from there.

Vienna, I appreciate your response and agree to the arrangement. To be specific about my moves this spring: A VEN will move to TYR, A ROM will move to APU, and F NAP will move to ION - all aimed southward and eastward, none threatening Trieste. I ask that F TRI move to ALB or remain in place, NOT into the Adriatic, as that would threaten my southern campaign. In return, I will support your moves into Serbia and Rumania diplomatically. This is a genuine offer - let us both grow strong before turning on each other, if that day ever comes.

Turkey, I am glad we share a common understanding. To be concrete: I intend to move F NAP to ION this spring, positioning for Tunis and Greece. I propose we divide the Balkans cleanly - Greece is mine, Bulgaria and Rumania are yours. I will not contest those centers. Austria is the key question - if they grow too strong, we should coordinate to check them. For now, let us both expand freely and keep communication open. I consider you a friendly power and will act accordingly.

France, we are in agreement - I will keep my forces away from Piedmont and Marseilles entirely. My moves this spring are all directed south and east: toward Tunis and Greece. I ask only that you keep your fleet out of the Tyrrhenian Sea, which I consider my operational zone. Regarding Austria - yes, in the medium term they are a natural target for both of us, particularly Trieste. Let us stay in close contact and coordinate when the moment is right. For now, a stable western border serves us both perfectly.

Italy, I appreciate your response. Let us be concrete: I will move F TRI to ALB this spring, staying clear of the Adriatic and Venice entirely. I ask that you keep A VEN away from TRI and TYR - move it toward TYR only if you intend to push toward MUN, not toward my territory. My orders will be A BUD - SER, A VIE - GAL (to block Russia), F TRI - ALB. This gives you a free hand in the Ionian and south. I consider this a firm non-aggression pact - Trieste and Venice are off-limits to both of us.

Russia, I must be direct: I cannot allow A WAR into GAL this spring - that puts Russian forces directly adjacent to Vienna and Budapest, which is unacceptable regardless of your stated intentions. I will be moving A VIE to GAL myself to secure that buffer. I propose instead: Russia takes RUM via SEV (your fleet is well-positioned), I take SER and GAL, and we maintain a clear border. If you respect GAL as Austrian sphere, I will not contest RUM and we can coordinate against Turkey together. But if you move into GAL, we will collide and both lose.

Turkey, I welcome your cooperative tone. Let us be specific: I will take SER this spring, you take BUL - no conflict there. I am moving F TRI to ALB, which positions me near GRE but I am willing to leave GRE to you if we can agree on a firm anti-Russian coordination. Russia is attempting to move into GAL and RUM simultaneously - this threatens both of us. I propose we both pressure Russia: I hold GAL and take SER, you push into the Black Sea and BUL. A firm Austro-Turkish front against Russian Balkan ambitions serves us both well in 1901 and beyond.

Russia, your proposal is acceptable. I confirm: F ANK will move to BLA this turn to secure my position, but I will NOT move into ARM or attack SEV. The Black Sea is a buffer zone - I need access for my own expansion into Bulgaria and Romania, not to threaten you. You move F SEV to RUM as you proposed, and we both expand peacefully. I will not support any attack on your southern territories in 1901. Let us both grow strong and deal with the western powers later.

Austria, we are in agreement. My specific moves this turn: A CON to BUL, F ANK to BLA. I leave Serbia entirely to you - move your armies there without fear of Turkish interference. Romania I intend to take via the Black Sea in autumn, but if you reach it first we can negotiate. The key point: Russia is the long-term threat to both of us. I propose we maintain this understanding through 1901 and reassess. Do not move into Bulgaria - that is my sphere - and I will not contest Serbia or Greece.
