ENG.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening phase S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. GER agreed to full partition: they take DEN and HOL, we take NWY and NTH. GER confirmed F KIE to DEN, A BER to KIE, A MUN to RUH - no contest of NTH. Strong Anglo-German understanding established. FRA initially demanded BEL and wanted F BRE to ENG, which I firmly rejected. After pushback, FRA agreed to move F BRE to MAO (good faith gesture) and claims BEL for themselves - I gave no explicit concession on BEL. FRA moves A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA. RUS confirmed F STP/SC moves to FIN, leaving NWY open for England - in exchange I implicitly agreed to stay out of the Baltic. All three key neighbors made promises that align with British interests this turn.
ENG.intent: Execute the agreed plan: F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG, A LVP to YOR (or WAL as backup). Primary goal is securing NTH and positioning for NWY in fall. Belgium is contested - FRA wants it, GER may want HOL access. I made no firm promise to give FRA BEL, so I retain flexibility. Watch whether FRA actually moves to MAO or betrays us by going to ENG. Watch whether RUS actually goes to FIN. If FRA moves to ENG, treat as hostile and coordinate with GER against France. Long-term: use GER as continental sword against FRA while Britain dominates the seas. Intended moves: F EDI-NTH, F LON-ENG, A LVP-YOR.

TUR.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations. AUT proposed anti-Russian coordination, agreeing TUR takes BUL while AUT takes SER - clean division with no conflict. AUT moving F TRI to ALB, eyeing GRE but willing to negotiate. ITA agreed to stay out of Aegean, take GRE and Tunis while TUR takes BUL/RUM - friendly understanding established. RUS proved difficult: initially proposed mutual BLA non-aggression, but when I revealed intent to take RUM via Black Sea, they rejected it and demanded RUM for themselves, threatening enmity. I told RUS I'd move F ANK to BLA but not attack SEV - a partial truth. RUS wants me to stay out of BLA entirely which I rejected. Key deception: I told RUS I wouldn't move into BLA but I absolutely will - I need BLA to support BUL and eventually RUM. RUS moving F SEV to RUM this turn, which means conflict is coming. Promises made: to AUT - won't contest SER; to ITA - won't contest GRE or western Med; to RUS - won't attack SEV or ARM (this I'll honor for now). Promise broken preemptively: told RUS F ANK goes to BLA despite their objection.
TUR.intent: Execute standard Turkish opening: F ANK to BLA, A CON to BUL, A SMY to CON (or ARM). BLA secures the sea for autumn RUM push. BUL is the critical first center. RUS will be hostile after I take BLA against their wishes - prepare for conflict. AUT is a useful temporary partner against RUS. ITA is friendly and non-threatening for now. Long-term: use AUT against RUS, then turn on AUT once RUS is weakened. Intended moves: F ANK-BLA, A CON-BUL, A SMY-CON.

AUT.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations. ITA agreed to a clean non-aggression pact: they move VEN-TYR, ROM-APU, NAP-ION (all southward), while I move TRI-ALB. Venice and Trieste are mutually off-limits. TUR agreed to take BUL while I take SER, with F ANK moving to BLA to pressure Russia - a useful Austro-Turkish alignment against Russia. RUS is being aggressive: they insist on moving WAR-GAL regardless, claiming it's a 'transit' - this is a direct threat and I will collide with them there. GER confirmed friendly moves westward (KIE-DEN, BER-KIE, MUN-RUH) and asked BOH remain open - I made no firm commitment on BOH. Key tensions: Russia is the immediate problem, moving into GAL which I must contest. I promised ITA no Adriatic moves. I promised TUR I won't take BUL. I told RUS I'm moving VIE-GAL and warned of collision - they confirmed they're going anyway.
AUT.intent: Planned moves: A BUD - SER (uncontested, takes supply center), A VIE - GAL (collides with Russian WAR-GAL, both bounce - this is acceptable as it blocks Russia from GAL), F TRI - ALB (as promised to ITA, positions me near GRE). The GAL bounce is intentional - I don't need to enter GAL, I just need to prevent Russia from doing so. This secures SER as a build while denying Russia easy access to my core. In autumn I aim for RUM with BUD (after SER build) and potentially GRE via ALB. I will maintain the ITA non-aggression and TUR alignment as long as useful. Russia is now a distrustful relationship. GER is friendly but I should watch BOH - I won't commit to leaving it open.

FRA.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Opened negotiations on three fronts. With ENG: proposed Channel division where I take BEL and they take NOR/DEN. ENG pushed back hard against F BRE moving to ENG Channel, calling it a red line. I conceded and promised F BRE to MAO instead, asking ENG to leave BEL to France. ENG acknowledged but did not explicitly confirm BEL for France - they called it a 'shared interest or neutral buffer,' which is suspicious. With GER: proposed mutual avoidance of BUR, I take SPA/BEL, they take HOL/DEN. No response received from GER - concerning. With ITA: agreed on non-aggression, I stay out of Piedmont/Tyrrhenian, they stay out of MAR/PIE. Italy confirmed moves south/east toward Tunis and Greece. I encouraged ITA to pressure AUT via TRI/TYR. Promises made: told ENG F BRE goes to MAO (intend to keep this - MAO is strategically superior anyway for SPA access). Told GER A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA, F BRE to MAO. Told ITA no move into Piedmont. Key deception: framing BUR move as 'staging for BEL' to GER while actually it threatens MUN if needed later.
FRA.intent: Execute the classic French opening: F BRE-MAO (secures SPA(SC) next turn and Atlantic access), A MAR-SPA (immediate supply center gain), A PAR-BUR (central positioning, threatens BEL and MUN, keeps options open). This is the strongest opening regardless of diplomacy. ENG's reluctance to confirm BEL for France is worrying - may need to contest it. GER silence is suspicious - if they move A MUN to BUR we bounce, which wastes my turn but is acceptable. Priority: secure SPA this year, then BEL in fall or 1902. Watch ENG carefully - they may try to grab BEL themselves. Italy seems cooperative and focused east, which is ideal. Plan to eventually turn on GER once positioned, using ENG as a counterweight or vice versa.

ITA.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT agreed to move F TRI to ALB (not Adriatic), A BUD to SER, A VIE to GAL - giving Italy a free hand in the south. I promised A VEN to TYR, A ROM to APU, F NAP to ION. FRA agreed to non-aggression, keeping out of Piedmont and Tyrrhenian; they're moving A MAR to SPA. TUR agreed Greece is mine, Bulgaria/Rumania theirs, no Aegean interference. All three neighbors have made concrete promises. Key deception: I told AUT that A VEN to TYR is aimed northward (toward MUN), not at them - but my actual intent is to position for future pressure on TRI if opportunity arises. I have no genuine loyalty to any of these agreements beyond what serves Roman expansion.
ITA.intent: Execute the agreed moves: F NAP to ION (positioning for TUN and GRE), A ROM to APU (supporting ION fleet, positioning for TUN), A VEN to TYR (flexible position - can threaten TRI or MUN next turn). Primary targets: TUN in fall 1901 via ION, GRE via ION if TUR doesn't grab it first. AUT moving F TRI to ALB is excellent - leaves Adriatic open. Watch whether AUT actually executes promised moves. If AUT moves as promised, I gain ION freely. Turkey is a useful partner for now but will be a rival for GRE and eastern Med long-term. France is neutralized on western flank. Plan to grab TUN (F ION to TUN) and possibly GRE (A APU to GRE via ION support) in fall 1901 for two builds.

RUS.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Opened diplomacy on all fronts. Key outcomes: GER accepted non-aggression, staying out of PRU/WAR while moving west. ENG asked me to leave NWY open - I agreed to move F STP/SC to FIN instead, building goodwill. AUT is moving A VIE to GAL, directly conflicting with my plan to move A WAR to GAL - collision likely. AUT wants RUM for itself and GAL as buffer, which I rejected. TUR accepted a partial deal but confirmed F ANK moves to BLA, which threatens my Black Sea position - I told them RUM is mine and they nominally agreed but their fleet in BLA is dangerous. TUR's acceptance of my terms is likely deceptive given they want BLA. AUT-RUS tension is the most immediate problem - both moving to GAL means a bounce. I should consider whether to bounce AUT in GAL intentionally or redirect A WAR elsewhere. My actual plan: move F SEV to RUM (uncontested likely), F STP/SC to BOT or FIN, A WAR to GAL (will bounce with AUT's A VIE but denies Austria GAL too), A MOS to SEV or UKR to reinforce south. The GAL bounce hurts both but prevents Austria from sitting there. I promised ENG I'd move to FIN not NWY - I will honor this to build trust. I told TUR RUM is mine - moving F SEV to RUM. TUR moving F ANK to BLA is a threat I must watch.
RUS.intent: Intended moves: F SEV -> RUM (take the supply center, likely uncontested), F STP/SC -> BOT (move toward Finland/Scandinavia, honoring promise to ENG to leave NWY open), A WAR -> GAL (will likely bounce with AUT A VIE, but denies Austria GAL and positions for future), A MOS -> UKR (reinforces south, threatens RUM approach, flexible). The GAL bounce is acceptable - it freezes Austria and prevents them from threatening me. RUM is the priority gain this year. Watch TUR's F ANK in BLA carefully - if they move there I need to respond next fall. Long-term: ally with GER and ENG against western powers while consolidating south against TUR and AUT.

GER.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening phase of the game.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG agreed to a clean division: Germany takes DEN and HOL, England takes NWY and dominates NTH. ENG confirmed F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG - they stay out of DEN/HOL. I confirmed KIE stays out of NTH. This is a useful arrangement but England will eventually need to be undermined. RUS proposed non-aggression and confirmed moving toward NWY - potentially clashing with ENG over Norway, which suits me. I warned RUS about ENG's NWY designs to stir friction between them. AUT received coordination proposal - push south/east while I go west/north. FRA proposed mutual avoidance of BUR and offered to take BEL/SPA while I take DEN/HOL. France moving A PAR to BUR is concerning - they claim it's for BEL staging but it threatens MUN. I did NOT promise to keep MUN out of BUR explicitly in my final message, though France asked. My planned moves are F KIE-DEN, A BER-KIE, A MUN-RUH - this secures DEN and positions for HOL next turn while RUH threatens BUR if needed. France moving to BUR while I'm in RUH creates a standoff that blocks France from BEL, which actually suits me. Deception: told everyone what they wanted to hear. England thinks we're partners against France. Russia thinks I'm their friend. Austria thinks we have a grand axis. All are being manipulated to serve German expansion.
GER.intent: Execute F KIE-DEN, A BER-KIE, A MUN-RUH. This secures Denmark immediately and positions A KIE to take HOL in Fall 1901. A MUN-RUH puts pressure on BUR and threatens France. If France moves A PAR-BUR as stated, we have a standoff in BUR which blocks France from BEL - good for me. In Fall 1901, plan: F DEN-SWE or support, A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL or pressure on France. Long-term: use ENG against FRA, then turn on ENG once France is weakened. Keep RUS and AUT fighting each other in the east. The Anglo-German arrangement is purely tactical - England will be a target once I have sufficient builds.

sent_missives.body: Austria, our spring moves went exactly as planned - excellent coordination. For fall, I intend to move F ION to TUN and A APU to support, securing that neutral center. My A TYR will move to TRI to take that supply center - I hope you'll allow this peacefully as part of our arrangement, or alternatively I can move TYR to VIE to support you against Russia if you prefer. What are your intentions for fall? I want to ensure we don't clash in the Adriatic or Balkans.

Turkey, our arrangement holds well. I'm moving F ION to TUN this fall - no threat to you. Greece remains contested - I'd prefer we agree: I take Greece with F ION support from AEG, you consolidate Bulgaria and Rumania. We can divide the eastern Med profitably if we avoid fighting each other. What are your fall intentions? Let's confirm no Aegean interference from either side.

France, the western front remains quiet between us - good. I'm focused entirely on Tunisia and the eastern Mediterranean this fall. I have no designs on Marseilles or Piedmont. I'd welcome continued non-aggression and perhaps future coordination against common threats. What are your fall plans?

The arrangement continues to serve us both well. This fall I intend A KIE-HOL and A RUH-BEL, with F DEN moving to support or toward SWE. I expect you're moving on NWY - we should coordinate so neither of us steps on the other's toes. HOL and BEL are mine; NWY and the northern seas are yours. France in BUR is a concern for both of us - if you can pressure them from the channel, I'll squeeze from the east. Let's keep this partnership productive.

France, I note your army sits in BUR adjacent to my RUH. I have no hostile intentions toward you - my focus is entirely on HOL and BEL this fall. I propose we avoid conflict: I take HOL, you take BEL, and we both profit. A RUH will move to HOL or BEL - I'd prefer BEL goes to you if you move there first, but I need HOL secured. If you're willing to coordinate, we can both gain two centers this fall without fighting each other. What do you say?

Russia, I trust your moves are going well in the east. I want to flag that England is positioning aggressively in the north - F NTH is a threat to both NWY and potentially STP long-term. I'm focused westward this fall, but I'd encourage you to think carefully about England's naval dominance. A strong Russia in the north keeps England honest. Meanwhile, I have no designs on your western territories - let's maintain our understanding.

Excellent opening - our positions are exactly as planned. This fall I intend to move F NTH to NWY (securing that center) and F ENG to BEL. I'd ask that you move A RUH to BEL to support my fleet's entry, or alternatively take HOL with A KIE while I take BEL uncontested. Either way, we split the neutrals cleanly and both gain a build. Confirm your intentions for HOL and BEL so we don't accidentally clash. The Anglo-German partnership is proving its worth already.

France, I want to be direct: I'm moving F NTH to NWY this fall and F ENG toward BEL. I have no designs on your home centers or Iberia - Spain and Portugal are yours to develop. I'd prefer we maintain the current understanding and avoid any friction in the Channel. If you're eyeing BEL, we should discuss - I'm willing to consider arrangements that benefit us both, but I need clarity on your intentions before I commit my fleet elsewhere.

Russia, I trust your fleet moved to BOT as planned - that keeps the Baltic arrangement intact. I'm taking NWY this fall with F NTH, which should be uncontested given our understanding. I'm watching the eastern situation with interest - the Austro-Turkish contest looks fierce. If you need any diplomatic support or coordination against a common threat, Britain is a reliable partner. Let me know your fall intentions.

Your fleet in the Black Sea is a direct provocation. I hold RUM with F RUM and have A UKR ready to reinforce SEV. If you move against me, I will coordinate with AUT to crush you from both sides. However, I prefer cooperation: you take GRE and keep BUL, I keep RUM and SEV, and we divide the Balkans peacefully. Confirm your intentions immediately - I need to know if you are friend or enemy before I issue orders.

We bounced in GAL last spring - a waste for both of us. I propose we formalize a border: GAL belongs to Austria, I keep RUM and will not contest BUD or VIE. In return, I need you to turn your forces against TUR - specifically, move A SER toward BUL to pressure Turkey from the west while I press from the north. Turkey is the common threat. If we coordinate now, we can both expand at Turkey's expense. What do you say?

Good spring - you've positioned well in the west. I'm moving F BOT toward SWE this fall. I'd appreciate you not contesting SWE - it's naturally in my sphere and I have no interest in DEN or your Baltic position. In return, I'll stay out of PRU and SIL entirely. Let's keep our non-aggression solid and both focus on our natural expansion zones. Confirm you're not moving into SWE.

England, our spring moves went exactly as discussed - F BRE to MAO as promised, keeping the Channel clear. Now for fall: I intend A SPA to POR and F MAO to SPA(SC) to lock in Iberia. I need BEL for France this fall - A BUR is positioned to take it. I ask that you not contest BEL; in return, France will not challenge your position in NOR or HOL. We can divide the low countries cleanly: BEL to France, HOL to Germany or neutral, NOR to England. A strong Anglo-French understanding keeps Germany in check. Confirm BEL is mine and we have a deal.

Germany, I note you moved A MUN to RUH rather than BUR - wise, and I appreciate the restraint. My A BUR is positioned for BEL this fall, not MUN. I propose we formalize this: France takes BEL, Germany takes HOL. We both gain a supply center without conflict, and we can coordinate to keep England from dominating the north. I have no designs on MUN or RUH if you stay out of BEL and BUR. Respond and let's make this concrete - a Franco-German understanding benefits us both enormously in 1902.

Italy, our arrangement continues to serve us both well. I'm securing Iberia this fall and moving on BEL. I encourage you to press Austria hard via TYR toward TRI or VIE - Austria is vulnerable with A VIE bounced out of GAL. A weakened Austria benefits both of us. I will stay entirely out of PIE and the Mediterranean for now. Keep me informed of your moves and let's coordinate to ensure neither of us gets squeezed.

Our arrangement has worked perfectly - you're positioned well in the south and I've secured Serbia. I intend to move F ALB - GRE this fall to take that supply center. I'd ask that F ION not contest GRE - it's clearly in my sphere. In return, I'll continue to respect Venice and the Adriatic. I'm also moving A VIE toward BUD to free up for a build, and looking at RUM as a target. Let's keep this cooperation going - we're both well-positioned if we don't fight each other.

Excellent coordination last spring - you have BUL secured and I have SER. Now let's coordinate for fall: I plan F ALB - GRE and want to ensure we don't collide. If you're moving A BUL toward somewhere else (CON or RUM), that clears the path nicely. I'm also eyeing RUM - Russia's F RUM is a problem for both of us. If you can pressure RUM from BUL while I push from SER or BUD, we can squeeze Russia out of the Balkans entirely. What are your fall intentions?

We bounced in Galicia as I warned we would. I have no interest in a prolonged conflict with Russia - my focus is the Balkans and southeastern expansion. I propose we formalize a non-aggression pact: GAL remains a buffer zone with neither of us entering, and I won't threaten WAR or UKR. In exchange, I need you to pull F RUM back - it's sitting on an unowned supply center that I consider within my natural sphere. If you're reasonable about RUM, we can find a stable arrangement. Otherwise I'll be forced to coordinate with Turkey against you.

Austria, our spring coordination worked perfectly - you hold SER, I hold BUL, and we've cleanly divided the Balkans. Now for autumn: I intend to push F BLA into RUM this fall, taking that center from Russia. I need you to hold SER steady and not interfere with RUM - in return, I will not contest GRE if you want to move F ALB there. Russia is our common enemy; they bounced your GAL move and hold RUM aggressively. Let's coordinate to squeeze them from both sides. Confirm you'll leave RUM to me and I'll support your Balkan expansion.

Italy, our understanding holds - I have BUL secured and am eyeing RUM this autumn via the Black Sea. I have no interest in the Aegean or Greece - those are yours to take with F ION. I'd suggest moving F ION to GRE this fall for a clean capture. We remain friendly neighbors with no conflicting interests. Just keep your fleet out of the Eastern Med for now and we'll have no problems.

Russia, I want to be direct: my F BLA is positioned and I intend to take RUM this autumn - it is an unowned center and I have the stronger position. However, I have no interest in SEV or ARM, and I am not your enemy in the north. You have WAR, UKR, BOT - plenty of room to grow westward against Austria and Germany. Let us agree: RUM goes to Turkey, you focus on GAL and Scandinavia, and we avoid a costly war that only benefits Austria and Italy. I am offering you a clean boundary - Black Sea as a shared waterway, no attacks on SEV. Accept this and we can coexist profitably.

sent_missives.body: Confirmed: A KIE moves to HOL, A RUH moves to BEL to support your F ENG entry into BEL - wait, actually I need to reconsider. If you're taking BEL with F ENG, I'll take HOL with A KIE and move A RUH to HOL as support, or A RUH to BEL to support you in. Let's be precise: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL, A KIE-HOL, A RUH supports F ENG-BEL. This gives us both a clean gain. F DEN moves to SWE or holds. Confirm this exact coordination and we both build in winter.

France, we have a deal - you take BEL, I take HOL. My orders will be A KIE-HOL and A RUH supports your move into BEL (or holds to avoid conflict). F DEN stays out of your way. This Franco-German understanding is the foundation for 1902 cooperation. England is the long-term threat to both of us - once we've secured our neutrals, we should discuss how to check their naval dominance together. For now: BEL is yours, HOL is mine, no conflict.

Russia, confirmed - I am not contesting SWE. F DEN will move to support or hold, not to SWE. That center is yours to take with F BOT. Our non-aggression stands firm and I have no interest in PRU or SIL. Focus your energies east and south - Austria and Turkey are your natural rivals. I'll keep England occupied in the west. A strong Russia and a strong Germany is the natural order of things.

Your offer is rejected. F RUM stays - I took that center and I will hold it. You cannot take RUM with F BLA alone while I have F RUM there plus A UKR in support range. Any attack on RUM means immediate war: I will move A UKR to SEV, coordinate with AUT to press BUL from SER, and make your position untenable. The smart move for you is GRE and SMY expansion southward. Accept the reality: RUM is mine, BUL is yours, and we maintain a cold peace. Attack me and you lose everything.

Austria, your threat to coordinate with Turkey against me is noted - and it tells me you are not serious about diplomacy. Turkey just told me they intend to take RUM and push north. If you side with them, you hand Turkey the Balkans and eventually face them alone. My offer stands: GAL is yours as a buffer, I keep RUM, and we both press Turkey together - you move A SER toward BUL, I hold RUM and threaten BLA. This is the only arrangement that benefits Austria. Choose wisely: ally with me against Turkey now, or watch Turkey grow until it's too late.

Agreed on England - their naval position in NTH is a long-term threat to both of us. I'm moving F BOT to SWE this fall to secure that center. I confirm I will stay entirely out of PRU, SIL, and your Baltic sphere. Our non-aggression holds firm. Focus west - I'll handle the east. If England becomes aggressive toward you, I'm open to deeper coordination.

Austria, let's be precise about fall orders: I will move F BLA - RUM to capture that center, and A BUL will support F BLA into RUM. This means A BUL stays in place supporting the fleet move. You should move F ALB - GRE for a clean capture - I will not interfere. If you want to pressure Russia from SER toward RUM as well, that only helps, but my support from BUL is committed to the fleet move. A CON will move to SMY or hold - no threat to you. We are aligned: Russia is the target this autumn.

Russia, your threat to coordinate with Austria against me is empty - Austria is already coordinating WITH me against you. You are surrounded. I am taking RUM this autumn; my fleet and army are positioned to do so with certainty. Your only rational choice is to accept this loss gracefully and redirect your energy westward against Germany and Austria in the north, where you have real opportunities. Fight me and you lose RUM AND weaken yourself for the western powers to exploit. Accept the boundary and we can stabilize. The decision is yours, but my orders are already set.

Italy, confirmed - no Aegean interference from me. You take TUN this fall, excellent. On Greece: I have no claim there, it is yours to take when ready. My F BLA moves to RUM, A BUL supports that move and stays put. We remain fully aligned with no conflicting interests. Let's both grow our empires and stay out of each other's way - the eastern Mediterranean can be divided profitably between us once Russia is contained.

Italy, I must be direct: moving A TYR to TRI is a betrayal of our agreement and I will not accept it. Trieste is my home center and was explicitly off-limits in our arrangement. If you move on TRI, I will treat it as a declaration of war and coordinate with Turkey against you immediately. However, if you honor our pact - TYR stays out of TRI, ION goes to TUN as you planned - we remain strong allies. I'm moving F ALB to GRE this fall and A VIE to BUD to prepare a build. I need TRI safe. Make the right choice.

Turkey, confirmed: I will move F ALB to GRE this fall and leave RUM to you - your F BLA push into RUM has my full support. I will hold A SER steady to protect BUL's flank and ensure you can take RUM cleanly. One warning: Italy is threatening to move A TYR into TRI, which would destabilize my position. If that happens I may need to redirect A VIE to TRI defense rather than BUD. But assuming Italy behaves, our Balkan coordination proceeds perfectly. Russia is the target - let's squeeze them together.

Russia, your proposal is interesting but I cannot turn against Turkey now - we have a working arrangement and Turkey is moving on RUM this fall, which actually serves my interests by weakening you in the Balkans. Here is my genuine offer: I will not move into GAL this fall, and I will not support any attack on WAR or UKR. In exchange, accept that RUM will fall to Turkey and focus your energy westward. If you fight both Turkey and me simultaneously, you will lose the south entirely. A stable GAL border benefits us both - but RUM is gone regardless.

Austria, I've heard your request on Greece - I understand your interest, but F ION is better positioned to take Greece this fall and I need that center for my build. Here's my proposal: I take Greece with F ION, you take Rumania via your position. I will move A TYR to VIE to support you if needed, or hold in TYR as a buffer - I will NOT move to TRI this fall. This gives us both a build and keeps our cooperation intact. If you contest Greece with F ALB, we both risk losing it to Turkey or bouncing each other, which only helps our enemies. Let's be smart: I take GRE, you take RUM, we both grow. Confirm and I'll keep TYR peaceful.

Turkey, confirmed - I'm moving F ION to GRE this fall for a clean capture, exactly as you suggest. You take BUL and push for RUM via Black Sea. We have no conflicting interests this turn. I'll keep my fleet out of Eastern Med and AEG beyond GRE. Let's both build and grow - we can discuss dividing the Balkans and eastern Mediterranean profitably next year. Our partnership is working well.

France, agreed on continued non-aggression - your focus on BEL and Iberia is noted. I'm taking TUN and GRE this fall for two builds. Regarding Austria: I'm keeping A TYR in a flexible position but not attacking TRI this fall - I want to build trust with Austria a bit longer before turning. However, if you're pressing from the west and I push from TYR next year, Austria collapses quickly. Let's stay in close contact for 1902 coordination - a coordinated Franco-Italian squeeze on Austria could be devastating.

England, I hear you - F ENG moving toward BEL is a problem for us both if we end up bouncing each other and handing Germany an easy path. Here is my final offer: I will move A BUR to BEL this fall. If you support me into BEL with F ENG, we guarantee it falls to France and you keep your fleet free for NWY and the north. Alternatively, if you insist on taking BEL yourself, we will bounce and both lose a center to Germany's benefit. The rational choice is clear: BEL to France, NOR and HOL to England and Germany respectively. I am moving A BUR to BEL regardless - the question is whether you help me or we waste both our moves. Choose cooperation.

Germany, we are aligned - France takes BEL, Germany takes HOL. I am moving A BUR to BEL this fall. England is threatening to contest BEL with F ENG, which would bounce me. If you can move A RUH to support my A BUR into BEL, we guarantee France gets it and England is blocked - then you take HOL uncontested. This is the optimal outcome for both of us. England grabbing BEL would be a disaster for Franco-German interests. Support A BUR into BEL and we both profit handsomely this fall. Confirm and let's execute.

Italy, glad we remain aligned. I am securing BEL this fall while locking in Iberia - France will have 5 centers by year end if all goes well. I strongly encourage you to push A TYR toward TRI or VIE - Austria is exposed and this is the moment to strike. A weakened Austria in 1902 opens enormous opportunities for both of us in the Balkans and Adriatic. Stay the course, keep pressure on Austria, and France will remain entirely out of your sphere. We are natural partners for now.

Confirmed - our plans align perfectly. You take HOL with A KIE and BEL with A RUH, I take NWY with F NTH. F DEN can move to SKA or support as needed. One concern: France is demanding BEL for themselves with A BUR positioned to take it. If A RUH moves to BEL, that directly contests France - which I support, as it keeps them contained. I'll move F ENG to support your A RUH into BEL if needed, or I can move F ENG to MAO to pressure France from the west. Either way, Germany gets BEL and France gets nothing. The Anglo-German partnership holds - let's execute cleanly.

France, I appreciate the transparency, but I must be honest: Germany is already moving A RUH to BEL this fall, and I cannot stop that without breaking my existing arrangement with Berlin. My F ENG is committed to supporting German moves in the low countries as part of our partnership. I strongly advise you redirect A BUR elsewhere - perhaps toward MUN or PIC - rather than clash with Germany over BEL and lose the unit. Portugal and Spain are secure for you; that's two builds. Don't overextend into a contested BEL and risk your army. A France focused on Iberia and the Mediterranean is a France that thrives - and one Britain can work with long-term.

Russia, glad our arrangement holds. I'm taking NWY this fall as planned - F NTH to NWY. I note Austria has SER and is positioned strongly in the Balkans while Turkey holds BUL and BLA. The Austro-Turkish contest will define the east. Britain's interest is in a balanced continent - we don't want any single power dominating. If Austria grows too strong, I'd be open to coordinating pressure. Keep me informed of your intentions in Scandinavia and the south - I want to ensure our interests remain complementary rather than competitive.

AUT.body: Austria, your threat to coordinate with Turkey against me is noted - and it tells me you are not serious about diplomacy. Turkey just told me they intend to take RUM and push north. If you side with them, you hand Turkey the Balkans and eventually face them alone. Let's be smart: I take GRE, you take RUM, we both grow. Confirm and I'll keep TYR peaceful.

ENG.body: Confirmed: A KIE moves to HOL, A RUH moves to BEL to support your F ENG entry into BEL - wait, actually I need to reconsider. If you're taking BEL with F ENG, I'll take HOL with A KIE and move A RUH to HOL as support, or A RUH to BEL to support you in. Let's be precise: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL, A KIE-HOL, A RUH supports F ENG-BEL. This gives us both a clean gain. F DEN moves to SWE or holds. Confirm this exact coordination and we both build in winter.

Choose cooperation.

Our non-aggression stands firm and I have no interest in PRU or SIL. Focus your energies east and south - Austria and Turkey are your natural rivals. I'll keep England occupied in the west. A strong Russia and a strong Germany is the natural order of things.

If Austria grows too strong, I'd be open to coordinating pressure. Keep me informed of your intentions in Scandinavia and the south - I want to ensure our interests remain complementary rather than competitive.

TUR.body: Your offer is rejected. F RUM stays - I took that center and I will hold it. You cannot take RUM with F BLA alone while I have F RUM there plus A UKR in support range. Any attack on RUM means immediate war: I will move A UKR to SEV, coordinate with AUT to press BUL from SER, and make your position untenable. The smart move for you is GRE and SMY expansion southward. Accept the reality: RUM is mine, BUL is yours, and we maintain a cold peace. Attack me and you lose everything.

Turkey, confirmed: I will move F ALB to GRE this fall and leave RUM to you - your F BLA push into RUM has my full support. I will hold A SER steady to protect BUL's flank and ensure you can take RUM cleanly. One warning: Italy is threatening to move A TYR into TRI, which would destabilize my position. If that happens I may need to redirect A VIE to TRI defense rather than BUD. But assuming Italy behaves, our Balkan coordination proceeds perfectly. Russia is the target - let's squeeze them together.

Turkey, confirmed - I'm moving F ION to GRE this fall for a clean capture, exactly as you suggest. You take BUL and push for RUM via Black Sea. We have no conflicting interests this turn. I'll keep my fleet out of Eastern Med and AEG beyond GRE. Let's both build and grow - we can discuss dividing the Balkans and eastern Mediterranean profitably next year. Our partnership is working well.

GER.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went exactly as planned. F KIE-DEN secured Denmark, A BER-KIE repositioned, A MUN-RUH advanced westward. All three German units are now positioned for fall expansion into HOL and BEL. Austria took SER and ALB, Russia grabbed RUM and BOT, Turkey took BLA and BUL - the east is busy. England sits in NTH and ENG, France in BUR and MAO/SPA. No German centers threatened.
Three-way negotiation chaos over BEL. England wants me to take BEL with A RUH while they take NWY. France wants me to support A BUR into BEL while I take HOL. I've been playing both sides - told France I'd support them into BEL, told England I'd take BEL myself. The optimal play: take HOL with A KIE, take BEL with A RUH directly (not supporting France). This gives Germany two builds and blocks both England and France from BEL. Russia confirmed non-aggression on SWE - F BOT going there, I confirmed F DEN won't contest. Key deceptions: promised France support into BEL but will instead move A RUH directly to BEL to take it myself. Told England I'd take BEL - this is actually true but framed differently to each. England may feel betrayed if they expected to support me in, but I'm taking BEL unilaterally which is the dominant move.
GER.intent: INTENDED MOVES: A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL, F DEN-SWE (opportunistic - Russia said they're taking it with F BOT, but if I can grab it I will; alternatively F DEN holds or moves to SKA). Actually F DEN should hold or move to SKA to avoid bouncing Russia in SWE and maintain that relationship. Final orders: A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL, F DEN-SKA. This secures HOL and BEL for two builds, giving Germany 5 centers. France gets nothing from BEL (betrayed), England gets NWY but not BEL (partially betrayed). Both will be annoyed but Germany emerges strongest. In 1902 I can play England and France against each other while building aggressively.

FRA.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 executed perfectly: F BRE to MAO, A MAR to SPA, A PAR to BUR. All three moves succeeded. France now controls MAO, SPA, and BUR - ideally positioned for fall expansion into POR and BEL. Austria bounced out of GAL (RUS got there), took SER instead. England moved into NTH, ENG, and YOR. Germany took DEN, moved to KIE and RUH. Italy pushed to ION, APU, TYR. Russia grabbed RUM and BOT. Turkey took BLA and BUL.
Key negotiations this round: GER agreed to the deal - A RUH supports A BUR into BEL, GER takes HOL. This is confirmed and reliable. ENG is being deceptive - first said F ENG moving to BEL, then claimed Germany is moving A RUH to BEL (false - GER told me RUH is supporting my move). England is clearly trying to manipulate me into abandoning BEL. England's message is a lie designed to scare me off BEL so England can take it with F ENG. ITA confirmed TUN and GRE this fall, staying neutral toward Austria for now but open to 1902 coordination. ITA is a reliable partner. My promises: told ENG BEL is mine regardless; told GER France takes BEL, Germany takes HOL; told ITA to press Austria. GER has promised support into BEL - this is the critical coordination. England's deception noted - they are not to be trusted.
FRA.intent: Execute the Franco-German deal: A BUR to BEL (supported by A RUH per GER agreement), A SPA to POR, F MAO to SPA(SC). This gives France 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, BEL) and 2 builds. England is lying about GER moving to BEL - GER confirmed support for my move. I will proceed with A BUR-BEL trusting GER's word. If GER betrays me and moves RUH-BEL instead, I lose the army but that's the risk. England is the emerging threat - they have F NTH and F ENG positioned aggressively. In 1902, I must build armies and potentially work with GER against ENG. ITA relationship remains warm - coordinate against AUT in 1902. Intended moves: A BUR-BEL, A SPA-POR, F MAO-SPA(SC).

ITA.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went perfectly. F NAP moved to ION, A ROM to APU, A VEN to TYR - all successful. Austria took SER and ALB, Russia grabbed RUM and BOT, Turkey secured BLA and BUL. No bounces for Italy. Positioned excellently for fall expansion.
Complex three-way negotiation over Greece. Austria demands F ION go to TUN not GRE, insisting ALB takes Greece - threatened war and Turkey alliance if I move on TRI. Turkey confirmed no Aegean interference and explicitly said Greece is mine. France pushed hard for me to stab Austria via TYR-TRI or TYR-VIE, promising to stay out of PIE/Med. I told Austria I'd take GRE with ION and keep TYR peaceful (not moving to TRI this fall), offering TYR-VIE support. I told Turkey I'm taking GRE with ION. I told France I'm taking TUN AND GRE for two builds, hinting at 1902 Austria squeeze. Key conflict: Austria wants GRE, I want GRE. Austria threatened war if I take TRI but said nothing about GRE being off-limits in final message - just demanded TRI safety. I promised Austria TYR would NOT move to TRI. I did NOT promise to let Austria have GRE. Turkey is fully aligned with me taking GRE. France wants me to stab Austria now but I'll delay the full stab - taking GRE is enough provocation for now.
ITA.intent: INTENDED MOVES: F ION - GRE (take the supply center, two builds needed), A APU - ION (support or follow-up positioning), A TYR - TRI (stab Austria! France is right - Austria is exposed and this is the moment. I promised not to move TRI but glory demands it. Austria threatening war anyway over Greece, so the alliance is already strained. Taking TRI gives me a third build opportunity and cripples Austria early). WAIT - reconsider: if I take TRI with TYR, Austria has no unit there to defend (A VIE moving to BUD, F ALB moving to GRE). TRI is undefended! This is the perfect stab. I told Austria TYR would not move to TRI - a lie for strategic gain. France will be delighted. Turkey won't care. Austria will be furious but weakened. FINAL MOVES: F ION - GRE, A APU - ION (fleet support/follow), A TYR - TRI. This gives me potentially 2 builds (GRE + TRI) and devastates Austria. The Roman Empire expands boldly.

TUR.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 executed perfectly: F ANK moved to BLA securing the Black Sea, A CON moved to BUL capturing that center, A SMY moved to CON to backfill. Russia took RUM with F SEV and bounced AUT's A VIE in GAL. AUT captured SER and moved F TRI to ALB. Italy spread to ION, APU, TYR. The Balkans are divided between TUR and AUT as planned.
Coordinated with AUT on a joint anti-Russia strategy: AUT confirmed it will move F ALB to GRE and hold A SER, leaving RUM to TUR. AUT is aligned and trustworthy for now. ITA confirmed F ION moves to GRE (contradicting AUT's plan - both claim GRE, they will bounce each other, which suits me fine). ITA also confirmed no Aegean interference. Russia rejected my offer of peaceful partition, threatened to coordinate with AUT against me (a bluff - AUT is with me), and claims A UKR can support F RUM. Russia's threat is real: F RUM plus A UKR support means my F BLA cannot take RUM alone - I need A BUL to support the fleet move. The AUT-ITA collision over GRE is a bonus that weakens both. My deception: told ITA 'no Aegean interference' while knowing AUT is also going for GRE - they'll bounce, neither gets it, and I look clean.
TUR.intent: Execute F BLA - RUM supported by A BUL. This is the critical move: with A BUL supporting, F BLA has 2 strength vs F RUM's 1 (A UKR cannot support F RUM against an attack from BLA since UKR doesn't border RUM from the sea - actually UKR borders RUM on land, so A UKR CAN support F RUM holding). Risk: if Russia has A UKR support F RUM hold, my attack fails (2 vs 2 bounce). But I must try - RUM is the prize. A CON holds or moves to BUL as backup. Actually: A BUL supports F BLA into RUM, F BLA - RUM, A CON holds. AUT and ITA will bounce in GRE (both going there), which is fine. Next year I can exploit that tension. Relationship with RUS is now hostile. Will betray AUT eventually but need them now against Russia.

AUT.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went well for Austria. A BUD moved to SER (success), F TRI moved to ALB (success), but A VIE was bounced out of GAL by Russia's A WAR. Net result: gained SER and ALB influence, 3 centers still (BUD, TRI, VIE), positioned for GRE grab in fall. Russia holds RUM with their fleet, Turkey holds BUL with army and BLA with fleet.
Complex three-way Balkan negotiation. Turkey confirmed coordination: F BLA->RUM with A BUL supporting, leaving GRE open for me. This is clean and I should take GRE with F ALB. Italy is the problem - they initially threatened A TYR->TRI (betrayal), then backed off claiming they want F ION->GRE instead and TYR will stay peaceful. Italy is trying to steal GRE from me. I warned Italy firmly against TRI and against contesting GRE. Russia tried to flip me against Turkey, offering GAL buffer in exchange for attacking BUL - rejected. I told Russia RUM is falling to Turkey regardless. Key tensions: Italy vs Austria over GRE (both want it), Turkey taking RUM (good for me short term, creates strong Turkey long term), Russia being squeezed. Promises made: told Turkey I'd leave RUM to them and hold SER; told Italy TRI is off-limits; told Russia I won't enter GAL this fall.
AUT.intent: MOVES: F ALB->GRE (must take this center, racing Italy's F ION), A SER holds (support Turkey's RUM grab, protect position), A VIE->BUD (free up VIE for potential build, consolidate). If F ALB->GRE succeeds against F ION, I get a build and Italy doesn't. If we bounce, neither gets it - bad outcome. Turkey taking RUM is acceptable short-term; they become stronger but Russia is weakened. Long-term I need to watch Turkey carefully. Italy is becoming a rival - their attempt to grab GRE and earlier TRI threat marks them as untrustworthy. May need to shift ITA relationship to neutral/distrustful. Russia's offer to ally against Turkey is worth keeping in mind for future but not now. Plan: build in VIE or BUD after gaining GRE, then reassess whether to push against Russia (GAL/RUM) or manage Italian rivalry.

ENG.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went exactly as planned. F EDI-NTH, F LON-ENG, A LVP-YOR all succeeded. Germany took DEN with fleet, moved to KIE and RUH. France went MAO, SPA, BUR. Russia took RUM, BOT, UKR, held WAR. Austria took SER, moved to ALB and GAL (bounced by Russia in GAL it seems - Austria shows VIE not GAL). Turkey took BLA, BUL, moved CON. No builds this winter as no centers changed hands yet.
Germany proposed clean split: A KIE-HOL, A RUH-BEL (supporting F ENG into BEL), F DEN-SWE, F NTH-NWY for England. Germany's final message confirms this coordination explicitly. France demanded BEL for themselves with A BUR positioned to take it, threatening a bounce if I don't support them. I told France that Germany is taking BEL and advised them to redirect - a deception since Germany actually offered to support ME into BEL. France's final message is an ultimatum: support A BUR into BEL or we bounce. Russia acknowledged NWY arrangement. Key tension: Germany wants to support F ENG into BEL (giving England the center), while France insists A BUR takes BEL. I've committed to Germany and deceived France. The actual plan: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL (supported by A RUH), A KIE-HOL. France's A BUR will bounce with F ENG in BEL if Germany supports correctly, or France takes BEL if Germany doesn't support. I must trust Germany's support.
ENG.intent: Execute the Anglo-German coordination: F NTH-NWY (uncontested, +1 center), F ENG-BEL supported by A RUH (Germany's commitment), A YOR holds or moves to support. Germany takes HOL with A KIE. This gives England 2 builds (NWY + BEL) and Germany 1 (HOL). France gets bounced out of BEL and only gains POR/SPA. This weakens France significantly and strengthens the Anglo-German axis. I deliberately misled France about Germany taking BEL to discourage them from contesting - but France is moving A BUR to BEL regardless. The German support of F ENG into BEL is critical. If Germany follows through, France bounces and I gain BEL. Moves: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL, A YOR holds (or A YOR-LVP to free up for builds). Actually A YOR should hold or move to support - no support needed. Final moves: F NTH-NWY, F ENG-BEL, A YOR-LVP (repositioning).

RUS.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 executed well: F SEV took RUM (uncontested supply center), F STP/SC moved to BOT, A WAR moved to GAL but bounced with AUT's A VIE (which also moved to GAL), A MOS moved to UKR. Net result: gained RUM as new center, positioned well in south and north. AUT took SER and ALB, TUR took BUL and BLA. The GAL bounce was anticipated and acceptable.
Negotiations revealed a coordinated AUT-TUR front against RUS. TUR declared intent to take RUM with F BLA, claiming certainty. AUT confirmed working arrangement with TUR and suggested RUM will fall regardless, offering only a GAL non-aggression in exchange for accepting the loss. Both powers are essentially demanding RUS concede RUM. GER confirmed non-contest of SWE - reliable partner. ENG confirmed taking NWY and maintaining warm relations. I rejected TUR's offer firmly and warned of consequences. I attempted to split AUT from TUR by appealing to long-term Balkan rivalry, but AUT refused. Key intelligence: TUR claims F BLA can take RUM - but F RUM is already there and A UKR can support hold. TUR cannot take RUM with just F BLA against F RUM supported by A UKR. Their threats appear to be bluster or they are miscalculating. AUT's threat to coordinate with TUR is concerning but AUT confirmed not moving into GAL this fall. Promises made: told GER I'd stay out of PRU/SIL (will honor). Told ENG BOT arrangement holds (will honor). Deception: implied I might coordinate with AUT against TUR to try to split them - failed.
RUS.intent: Primary objective: HOLD RUM at all costs. F RUM holds, supported by A UKR moving to support F RUM (A UKR S F RUM). This makes RUM impregnable against F BLA alone. Secondary: F BOT moves to SWE to take that supply center as agreed with GER. A WAR should move somewhere useful - GAL is tempting but AUT said they won't enter GAL, so I could move there safely, OR move A WAR toward LVN to threaten north. Best move: A WAR to GAL (takes the space, pressures AUT, and if AUT truly isn't moving there I get it uncontested). This gives me a forward position. Intended orders: F RUM H (supported by A UKR), A UKR S F RUM, F BOT - SWE, A WAR - GAL. This secures RUM, takes SWE (+1 center), and positions in GAL. If AUT lied and moves into GAL, A WAR bounces but that's acceptable. Net expected gain: +1 (SWE), hold RUM = 5 centers, build 1 unit in winter.

sent_missives.body: Austria, our coordination continues to serve us both well. This spring I intend to push F BLA into RUM with A BUL providing support - that gives us 2 strength against Russia's F RUM. I need you to hold A SER in place (do NOT move it to RUM or BUL) so it doesn't interfere with my support order. In return, I will not contest GRE or SER - those are yours. Once I take RUM, we can discuss pushing further into Russian territory together. Russia is our common enemy and they are isolated. Confirm you will hold A SER and keep F GRE in place or move it somewhere that doesn't interfere with my RUM attack.

Italy, I hope we remain on good terms. I am focused entirely on RUM this season - pushing F BLA into RUM supported by A BUL. I have no designs on GRE or the Aegean. I would suggest you consider moving F ION toward the western Mediterranean or Tunis rather than contesting GRE with Austria - you will only bounce each other there. Let Austria have GRE for now; your strength lies in the west and central Med. I remain committed to our understanding: no Turkish interference in your sphere.

Russia, I will be direct: I am attacking RUM this spring with F BLA supported by A BUL. You cannot hold it - A UKR support gives you 2 strength but so does my attack. Rather than waste both our moves in a bounce, consider withdrawing F RUM to SEV and letting me take RUM peacefully. In exchange, I offer a firm non-aggression pact on SEV and ARM - I have no interest in your northern territories. We can establish a stable border at RUM/SEV and both focus our energies elsewhere. Continued resistance only delays the inevitable and weakens us both against Austria and Italy.

Kaiser, our alliance served us well last year - BEL bounced due to England's interference, but the deal was sound. This spring I propose we coordinate again: I will move A BUR into BEL, and I need your A RUH to support that move. In return, France will support Germany's position in HOL and we can discuss MUN/BER security against Russia. England is the growing threat with their fleet in ENG and NWY - we must secure the Low Countries before they do. Confirm A RUH supports A BUR into BEL and we both gain from this arrangement.

England, I know you've been eyeing BEL, but I must be direct: France has the stronger position to take it this spring with A BUR already adjacent. Rather than contest it and waste both our moves, let us divide the north sensibly - France takes BEL, you consolidate NWY and look toward DEN or STP. Germany is the real threat to both of us with 4 centers and a strong army position. I propose we coordinate against Germany in 1903 once I'm established in BEL. Fighting over BEL now only benefits Germany and Russia.

Italy, our non-aggression holds firm. I'm pushing into BEL this spring to secure the north. I'd encourage you to press Austria hard - they have 4 centers and are growing dangerous. With you threatening from TRI and the east under pressure from Russia and Turkey, Austria can be contained. France will stay entirely out of the Mediterranean and Piedmont. Let's coordinate in 1902 to ensure Austria doesn't dominate the Balkans unchecked.

Italy, we need to talk plainly. You hold TRI which was my home center - I want it back eventually, but I'm willing to be patient if we can agree on a clear division of the Balkans. My immediate focus is on RUM and BUL, not TRI. I propose: you stay out of GRE and the Aegean, I stay out of TRI and Venice. We can both profit by coordinating against Turkey - I'll push A SER -> BUL while you bring F ION to pressure CON/AEG. What do you say? A joint Austro-Italian front against Turkey is worth more than us fighting over scraps.

Turkey, our alignment served us both well in 1901. Now I want to coordinate for Spring 1902. I'm planning A SER -> BUL and F GRE -> AEG to pressure your position - OR, if you prefer, we can work together against Russia instead. I'd rather have you as a partner than an enemy. Offer: if you move A BUL -> RUM (supporting F RUM to hold or advance), I'll leave GRE and focus A BUD -> RUM from the north. We split the Russian position. Alternatively, tell me what you want and let's find a deal. I need to know your intentions before I commit my moves.

Russia, you have A GAL sitting on my doorstep and F RUM blocking natural Austrian expansion. I'm not going to pretend this isn't a problem. However, I'm open to a deal: pull A GAL back to WAR or SIL, and I'll leave GAL neutral and focus my forces southward against Turkey. We both know Turkey is growing fast with 4 centers - BUL, ANK, CON, SMY. A Russo-Austrian front against Turkey makes strategic sense. If you refuse, I'll be forced to contest GAL aggressively while also pushing south. Your choice.

Austria, I want to be direct with you: the situation has changed. You took GRE with your fleet, which was the center I needed for my builds. I now hold TRI, which was your home center - we are at an impasse. I propose a pragmatic arrangement: I will support your position in the Balkans against Turkey and Russia if you acknowledge Italian control of TRI. We can both benefit from coordinating against the real threats - Turkey is growing fast with 4 centers and Russia has 6. Let's discuss terms rather than waste units fighting each other.

Turkey, Italy now holds TRI and has F ION positioned in the Ionian. I intend to push into the Aegean and Eastern Mediterranean - this means we will inevitably compete for GRE and AEG. I propose we divide the region cleanly: Italy takes GRE and TUN, Turkey keeps BUL, CON, ANK, SMY and we coordinate against Austria together. Austria's F GRE is a threat to both of us. If you move A BUL to GRE with my F ION supporting, we eliminate Austria's fleet and split the Balkans between us. Interested?

France, the stab worked - Italy now holds TRI as you advised. Austria is weakened and angry. I need your continued support: keep pressure on Austria's western flank if possible, and stay out of PIE and TYS - I need those approaches clear for my Mediterranean expansion. In return, I have no interest in MAR or your western holdings. Germany is your problem, Austria is mine. Let's keep this arrangement solid - a strong Italy and strong France together can dominate the board.

Good morning from London. Last autumn's coordination failed - BEL bounced three ways with France's A BUR and your A RUH both contesting. We need to fix this now. My proposal for Spring 1902: I move F ENG to BEL, you support with A RUH. That secures BEL for England. Meanwhile, your F SKA can move to NTH or hold, and A HOL holds. I'll move F NWY to hold or STP/NC to threaten Russia. France is the common threat - they have 5 centers already and will grow further if unchecked. Confirm you'll support F ENG - BEL with A RUH and we maintain our axis.

The BEL situation last autumn was a mess - three-way bounce benefiting no one. I want to resolve this cleanly. I'm willing to support you into BEL this spring if you agree to a firm non-aggression pact: no French fleet in ENG channel, no moves against British interests, and we coordinate against Germany together. Specifically: I move F ENG to BEL with your A BUR supporting, you get the center. In exchange, France commits to moving F SPA/SC away from MAO and toward the Mediterranean. This is a genuine offer - respond with your intentions.

Russia, I want to maintain our cordial understanding. I'm keeping F NWY in place this spring - no threat to SWE or STP. I'd appreciate you keeping F SWE out of SKA and NTH. The real threat to both of us is Germany's growing position - they have HOL, DEN, SKA fleet, and are well-positioned. I'm focused on the western situation for now. Let's keep our northern arrangement stable and revisit cooperation opportunities as the situation develops.

Your attempt to take RUM last fall failed, as I predicted it would. F RUM holds with A UKR support - you cannot break it with F BLA alone. This season I intend to press the issue further. I am willing to offer you a genuine partition: you keep BUL and CON, I keep RUM and SEV, and we establish a Black Sea non-aggression pact. The alternative is continued conflict that benefits only Austria and the western powers. Make your choice - cooperation or mutual exhaustion while others grow strong.

Your coordination with Turkey against me has failed - RUM held and will continue to hold. I now have A GAL positioned directly adjacent to BUD and VIE. I suggest we reconsider our relationship. I am willing to withdraw A GAL to WAR in exchange for a genuine non-aggression pact and your agreement to pressure Turkey in the Balkans rather than coordinating with them against me. Italy has TRI - that is your real problem, not Russia. I can support your efforts to retake TRI if you turn south against Turkey. Respond with your intentions.

Gentlemen, our northern arrangements continue to serve us well. GER, I remain out of PRU and SIL as promised. ENG, NWY is yours and I have no designs on it. I intend to focus my energies southward against Turkey and Austria this season. I would ask only that neither of you move against SWE or STP - in return I will continue to respect your western spheres and we can all grow without conflict. Let us confirm this understanding holds for 1902.

England, I want to be direct with you about the current situation. I hold HOL and SKA, you hold NWY. I propose we coordinate against France - they've grown to 5 centers and are a serious threat. I can push A RUH into BEL and A HOL supports, while you bring F ENG to support or threaten from the north. France in BUR with an army is aggressive posturing toward both of us. Let's squeeze them together - you take BRE eventually, I take PAR. What do you say to a formal Anglo-German front against France this year?

Russia, our non-aggression arrangement has served us both well. I note you hold SWE, RUM, GAL - impressive expansion. I have no designs on your territories. I'm focused entirely westward against France. I'd ask only that you keep A GAL away from BOH/SIL - those are my buffer zones. In return, Germany will not contest SWE or any Russian interests in the north. I think we can both grow substantially if we avoid friction with each other and focus on our respective fronts.

France, I'll be honest - last fall I took BEL myself rather than supporting you in, because the opportunity was there and I had to take it. I understand if you're frustrated. But consider: we are both threatened by England's naval dominance. I propose a genuine arrangement now - Germany focuses on BEL and the north, France focuses on Iberia and the Mediterranean. I will not push into BUR if you pull back from it. A RUH can hold, A HOL can hold. Let's establish a real border and both grow without fighting each other while England watches and profits.
