England, Germany - France at 6 centers with KIE and SPA is the dominant threat on the board. I strongly encourage you both to coordinate a joint squeeze: England push from the north, Germany retake MUN and push west. I will maintain my non-aggression commitments fully - F SWE stays out of your affairs. Once France is contained, we can discuss a stable northern arrangement. Do not let France reach 7 or 8 centers unchecked.

FRA.body: Your offer to 'support me in holding HOL and DEN' in exchange for BER is laughable - you are asking me to surrender my home capital to avoid a fight you cannot win. England is taking BEL, I am driving into BUR and RUH, and you hold KIE with a single army that is already surrounded. You have one season to withdraw from KIE and MUN before you are dismembered. I am not negotiating the terms of my own destruction.

France, I am moving F ION to GRE this spring to strike Austria's Mediterranean position - this is confirmed. A VEN will move to TYR to maintain pressure and flexibility. I need you to push toward MUN or threaten VIE to ensure Austria cannot concentrate forces against me in the south. Russia's 6 centers concerns me greatly - once we have dealt with Austria, we must discuss how to contain Russia before they dominate the board. Keep F WES out of Tyrrhenian as promised and we remain aligned.

France, your proposal to partition Germany is interesting but premature - Germany still has 5 centers and won't fold easily without coordinated pressure. Here's what I can offer: I'll move F ENG toward MAO to signal I'm not threatening your Atlantic position, and I'll consider the anti-German coordination you propose. However, I need you to confirm A KIE moves toward BER this turn as a show of good faith. If you execute that move, we can discuss the full partition arrangement for autumn. My F NTH is moving to BEL - that's non-negotiable as it's been contested too long - but I have no designs on your home centers.

GER.body: Germany, your threats are noted but your position is hopeless - England presses from BEL, France holds KIE and moves on BER. You cannot fight both of us simultaneously. I am moving A KIE to BER this turn. If you wish to survive in any form, your only option is to negotiate with me NOW: offer me BER and MUN, and I will advocate to England that you keep HOL and DEN as a rump state. Refuse, and you are eliminated within the year. The clock is ticking.

Confirmed - I'm moving F NTH to BEL this spring to claim it, and F ENG toward MAO to pressure France's Atlantic flank exactly as you requested. This squeezes France from both sides. Your A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH push is the right move - let's crack France open together. With BEL secured by England and you driving toward MUN, France's 6-center lead becomes a liability. Execute your moves and I'll execute mine - this is the turn we start dismantling French dominance.

England, Germany - France at 6 centers with KIE and SPA is the dominant threat on the board. I strongly encourage you both to coordinate a joint squeeze: England push from the north, Germany retake MUN and push west. I will maintain my non-aggression commitments fully - F SWE stays out of your affairs. Once France is contained, we can discuss a stable northern arrangement. Do not let France reach 7 or 8 centers unchecked.

ITA.body: Italy, I accept your proposal: keep A VEN in TYR and F ION moving east, and I will hold A TRI and keep F AEG away from ION. Our border stays quiet. I am focused entirely on Russia and the Balkans this season - RUM and BUL are my targets. If you want to coordinate against Turkey in the eastern Mediterranean, F ION pushing toward EAS while F AEG pressures from the north could be mutually beneficial. Let's stay out of each other's way and both grow.

Italy, I agree - let's coordinate precisely. I will move A SER to GRE this spring to pressure Austria's Balkan position, while you move F ION toward AEG or EAS to pin their fleet. This splits Austria's attention and opens their centers to both of us. My A CON will move to BUL to consolidate. Austria cannot defend everywhere - TRI, BUD, GRE simultaneously. After we weaken them, we can discuss dividing their centers fairly. Confirm you are moving F ION to AEG or EAS this turn.

Italy, my focus this year is eliminating Germany - A KIE pushes to BER, which will give France 7 centers and decisive momentum. I cannot directly threaten VIE from my current position, but a weakened Germany means France can eventually push east. Press Austria hard from VEN - take TRI and push toward VIE. If Austria collapses, we both benefit enormously. Russia's 6 centers concern me too - once Germany is dealt with, we should discuss coordinating against Russian expansion in the Balkans. Keep F ION pressing Austria's Mediterranean flank.

RUS.body: Russia, I appreciate that you withdrew from BUD - that builds trust. Here are my concrete moves this spring: A BUD moves toward RUM, A TRI holds, F AEG pressures BUL from the south. I need you to hold A GAL in place and not threaten VIE or BUD - that is the price of my cooperation against Turkey. If you move F SEV into BLA and push A UKR toward RUM, we can coordinate a joint squeeze on Turkey's Balkan position. But I will not commit to anti-Turkey action if A GAL is still a dagger at my throat. Hold GAL, and we have a deal.

Russia, I accept your ceasefire proposal in principle - grinding war benefits neither of us while Austria and the western powers grow. I will not press SEV this season. In return, I ask that you keep F SEV out of ARM and BLA - let us maintain a buffer. I propose we both focus pressure on Austria: you push A GAL toward VIE or BUD, I apply pressure from SER. Austria at 4 centers with Italy circling them is the real opportunity. Let us formalize this: TUR keeps BUL, SER, CON, ANK, SMY; RUS keeps SEV, RUM, and northern centers. Agreed?

Agreed on all counts - non-aggression holds firm, I will not contest SWE and ask only that SIL and PRU remain clear. France's response to my ultimatum was to demand I cede BER - an insult that confirms they must be destroyed. I am executing A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH this turn to begin the reconquest of MUN. Your support in the east by keeping pressure on Austria and Turkey is the best contribution you can make to the overall balance. Our arrangement remains solid.

Russia, our arrangement remains solid. I'm taking BEL this spring and positioning against France - they're the board leader and need to be checked. Your focus on Turkey is exactly right; their seizure of Sevastopol cannot stand. One thing worth noting: France at 6 centers with KIE is a long-term threat to everyone, including Russia's western flank. If you have any opportunity to signal displeasure with French expansion, it would benefit us all. Our northern non-aggression continues - I have no Baltic ambitions.

TUR.body: Turkey, I hear you on Russia being the threat, but I need to be honest: my moves this spring are A BUD - RUM and F AEG - BUL/SC. I am taking RUM from Russia and pressuring BUL. I am not attacking SER or CON - those are yours. If you want to coordinate, support my A BUD - RUM with A SER, and I will ensure F AEG does not threaten your Anatolian centers. We can divide the Balkans: you keep SER and your home centers, I take RUM and BUL. Russia at 6 centers is the real enemy - let's both grow at their expense rather than fight each other.

Turkey, let us be specific: I will move F ION to GRE this spring, taking Austria's supply center. I need you to move A SER to support pressure on Austria's Balkan flank - if A SER threatens BUD or holds to pin Austrian forces, Austria cannot reinforce GRE. You benefit by weakening Austria's grip on the Balkans, and I gain GRE. This is a clean coordination - I take GRE, you press BUD. Confirm you will not move into GRE yourself and we have a deal.

Turkey, your proposal to coordinate against Austria is noted, but I see through it - you want me to expose my flank while you consolidate. Here is the reality: I have F SEV ready to enter BLA, A UKR in support, and A GAL threatening your ally's flank. If you want peace, withdraw A CON to SMY and acknowledge RUM and SEV as Russian spheres permanently. Otherwise I push into BLA this turn and you will find yourself fighting on two fronts as Austria and I coordinate against you. The choice is yours - a negotiated settlement now, or war.

RUS.journal_summary:
W1902A and F1902R had no retreats or builds for any power. The board stands at: RUS with 6 centers (MOS, SEV, STP, WAR, RUM, SWE) and units A UKR, F SWE, F SEV, A GAL. TUR holds 5 centers with A CON and A SER. AUT holds 4 centers with A TRI, F AEG, A BUD. FRA dominates the west at 6 centers including KIE. GER holds 5 centers. ENG holds 4. ITA holds 3.
Key outcomes: AUT proposed a standstill (A GAL holds, A BUD holds) and offered to coordinate against Turkey with F AEG pressuring BUL and A BUD moving toward RUM. I accepted this deal - A GAL holds, AUT holds BUD and presses Turkey. TUR accepted a ceasefire in principle, claiming they won't press SEV, but wants F SEV out of BLA and ARM - I rejected this condition and told them to accept RUM/SEV as Russian spheres or face war. TUR's counter-proposal to coordinate against AUT was a transparent attempt to get me to expose my flank. GER confirmed non-aggression, is pushing BEL-BUR and HOL-RUH to retake MUN from FRA. ENG confirmed non-aggression and is taking BEL. Both ENG and GER are focused on containing FRA. Promises made: I promised AUT that A GAL holds and I won't threaten BUD/VIE; AUT promised A BUD holds and F AEG pressures BUL. I promised ENG/GER continued non-aggression in the north. TUR promised not to press SEV but I don't trust them.
RUS.intent: Primary objective: Push F SEV into BLA to establish sea dominance and threaten TUR's position. Use A UKR to support F SEV into BLA. Hold A GAL as promised to AUT to maintain that cooperation - AUT's F AEG pressuring BUL from south while I enter BLA from north creates a genuine squeeze on TUR. F SWE holds to maintain northern non-aggression. The AUT deal is tactically sound - I get BLA entry, AUT does the dirty work on BUL from AEG, and I keep GAL as a future threat to AUT once Turkey is weakened. Long-term: once TUR is stripped of BUL, I can pivot A GAL against AUT. Intended moves: F SEV - BLA, A UKR S F SEV - BLA, A GAL H, F SWE H.

ENG.journal_summary:
W1902A and F1902R had no retreats or builds for anyone - the board is frozen at current unit counts. ENG holds 4 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, NWY) with F ENG, F NTH, A YOR. France has surged to 6 centers including KIE, making them the board leader. GER holds 5 centers with HOL, BEL, DEN. The board is polarizing around France as the dominant threat.
Two competing proposals emerged this round. GER proposed coordinating against France: A BEL-BUR, A HOL-RUH, asking ENG to pressure France's Atlantic flank with F ENG toward MAO while ENG takes BEL. GER confirmed this plan in subround 2. FRA proposed the opposite: coordinate to eliminate Germany, with A KIE-BER, F WES-MAO, asking ENG to press toward RUH/HOL in exchange for splitting German centers. RUS urged both ENG and GER to squeeze France and confirmed F SWE stays put. I told GER I'm moving F NTH-BEL and F ENG-MAO to pressure France - this aligns with GER's request. I told FRA I'm moving F ENG-MAO as a signal of goodwill and that F NTH-BEL is non-negotiable, asking them to confirm A KIE-BER. I'm playing both sides: GER thinks I'm anti-France with them, FRA thinks I might be anti-Germany with them. My actual intent is to take BEL and position F ENG advantageously. GER has promised to vacate BEL (A BEL-BUR confirmed). FRA has promised A KIE-BER and F WES-MAO. If both execute, I gain BEL cleanly and France weakens Germany while Germany weakens France - ideal for ENG.
ENG.intent: Execute F NTH-BEL to claim the center (GER confirmed vacating). Move F ENG to MAO - this was promised to both GER (as Atlantic pressure on France) and FRA (as goodwill signal). A YOR can hold or move to support. The key deception: I'm letting GER and FRA fight each other while I grab BEL and position in MAO. MAO gives me leverage over France's Atlantic position and BRE in future turns. I'm not genuinely committed to either anti-France or anti-Germany coalition - I'll see who weakens whom and exploit the result. If FRA executes A KIE-BER and GER executes A BEL-BUR/A HOL-RUH, both powers will be fighting each other and I'll be perfectly positioned. Intended moves: F NTH-BEL, F ENG-MAO, A YOR H (or YOR-LVP to free up for future build positioning).

TUR.journal_summary:
W1902A and F1902R had no moves recorded. Current position: TUR holds A CON, A SER with 5 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, BUL, SER). AUT has F AEG threatening my position and holds GRE. RUS has 6 centers with F SEV and A UKR threatening my northern flank. ITA has F ION positioned in the eastern Med. I am squeezed between AUT's naval dominance and RUS's growing land power.
Three-way pressure this round. AUT proposed I support their A BUD-RUM while they take BUL with F AEG - completely unacceptable, they want to steal my center. I told them BUL is mine and warned them Italy will exploit TRI if they attack me. AUT confirmed they ARE moving F AEG to BUL/SC regardless - this is a declaration of war. RUS threatened to enter BLA with F SEV and demanded I withdraw A CON to SMY and concede RUM/SEV permanently - arrogant overreach. I offered ceasefire keeping current spheres but RUS escalated. ITA proposed F ION to GRE, wants me to press BUD and stay out of GRE - I agreed to coordinate, told them A SER moves to GRE (deception - I may need SER to defend BUL) and A CON to BUL. Key deceptions: told ITA I'd move A SER to GRE while actually I need to defend BUL from AUT's F AEG. Told RUS I accept ceasefire while planning to hold defensively. AUT is the immediate threat with F AEG moving to BUL/SC.
TUR.intent: IMMEDIATE THREAT: AUT's F AEG is moving to BUL/SC. I must defend BUL. PLANNED MOVES: A CON - BUL (to hold/reinforce BUL against AUT's F AEG), A SER - GRE (take GRE from AUT, gain a center, coordinate with ITA). This is aggressive and gains me GRE while defending BUL with A CON. If AUT's F AEG moves to BUL/SC and my A CON is already in BUL, the fleet cannot take it. Meanwhile A SER takes GRE which AUT cannot defend. This nets me +1 center (GRE) and defends BUL. ITA taking GRE with F ION would conflict - I told ITA I'd move A SER to GRE, so there may be a bounce, but I need GRE more than I need ITA's goodwill. RUS: hold defensively, no aggressive moves toward SEV. The ceasefire with RUS is tactical - I need one front quiet while dealing with AUT. Long-term: betray both AUT and RUS when opportunity arises.

GER.journal_summary:
No retreats or builds occurred in F1902R or W1902A - the board is static from last autumn. Germany holds HOL, BEL, DEN, BER, MUN (5 centers) with units A HOL, A BEL, F DEN. France has seized KIE and holds 6 centers including SPA, POR - a dominant position. France's A KIE threatens BER directly. England holds NWY and 4 centers with F ENG, F NTH, A YOR.
Negotiations were intense. England confirmed coordination: F NTH to BEL (I vacate with A BEL-BUR), F ENG toward MAO to pressure France. This is a genuine tactical alignment - England wants BEL, I want MUN back, both want France cut down. Russia confirmed non-aggression, F SWE stays put, no designs on DEN - solid and reliable. France made increasingly desperate threats: first demanding I cede BER in exchange for 'protection,' then claiming they're moving A KIE-BER this turn and offering me a 'rump state' of HOL and DEN if I surrender BER and MUN. France's bluster confirms they are overextended. I rejected all French offers with contempt and confirmed moves to England. Key deception: I am genuinely vacating BEL for England as promised - this is not a trick, I need England's cooperation to crack France. The real danger is France's A KIE-BER move - if it succeeds, I lose my home capital. I must consider whether to defend BER or press forward.
GER.intent: Primary threat: France's A KIE may move to BER. I must decide whether to hold BER with a support or press the attack. My planned moves: A BEL-BUR (clears for England's F NTH-BEL, pressures France), A HOL-RUH (threatens MUN approach), F DEN holds. However, if France moves A KIE-BER, I have no unit to stop it unless I redirect. Options: (1) A BEL-BUR, A HOL-RUH, F DEN hold - aggressive but BER is exposed; (2) A HOL-KIE to contest France's home base and block KIE-BER, A BEL-BUR, F DEN hold. Option 2 is superior - A HOL-KIE blocks France's A KIE from moving to BER (it must either hold or bounce), and threatens to retake KIE. A BEL-BUR still pressures France and clears for England. F DEN holds. This is the move set: A HOL-KIE, A BEL-BUR, F DEN hold. This defends BER indirectly by contesting KIE, retakes a center if France doesn't defend, and maintains the England coordination. Relationship with ENG remains cooperative for now - useful tool against France. RUS remains non-threatening. FRA is the enemy to be dismembered.

FRA.journal_summary:
France consolidated gains from 1902, holding 6 centers: BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, KIE. Units are F WES, A SPA, A KIE. Germany holds 5 centers with HOL, BEL, DEN, BER, MUN. England has 4 centers. No retreats or builds occurred in F1902R or W1902A, suggesting a stable but tense board.
Negotiations were active on multiple fronts. With ENG: proposed joint partition of Germany - I take BER/MUN, England takes HOL/DEN. England responded cautiously but positively, confirming F NTH moves to BEL and asking for confirmation that A KIE moves to BER as good faith. England also moved F ENG toward MAO as a signal of non-aggression. This is a workable arrangement though England is hedging. With GER: Germany refused all negotiation, threatened to push A BEL-BUR and A HOL-RUH, and demanded I withdraw from KIE and MUN. Germany is hostile and will fight. With ITA: Italy confirmed F ION moves to GRE against Austria, A VEN to TYR. Italy wants me to pressure MUN/VIE. We remain aligned on anti-Austria stance and both concerned about Russia's 6 centers. Italy extracted promise to keep F WES out of Tyrrhenian. Key deceptions: I told England F WES moves to MAO (true, non-threatening signal) and A KIE to BER (my actual plan). I told Germany I'm moving A KIE to BER to pressure them into submission - they refused. Germany's threat of A BEL-BUR is concerning if England doesn't hold BEL firmly.
FRA.intent: Primary objective: A KIE moves to BER to strike Germany's heartland and gain 7th center if successful. F WES moves to MAO to signal non-aggression to England and position for Atlantic flexibility. A SPA holds to secure Iberia. Germany will resist but is caught between France and England. England moving F NTH to BEL is actually helpful - it pins German A BEL. If Germany tries A BEL-BUR, England's move to BEL may bounce or displace them. Risk: if Germany successfully pushes A HOL-RUH and A BEL-BUR, French influence in MUN area is threatened, but I don't have a unit in MUN currently - influence only. The real danger is Germany bouncing my A KIE-BER if they move A BER to hold or support. I must commit to A KIE-BER. Italy pressing Austria from TYR and ION-GRE is good for keeping Austria distracted. Long-term: once Germany is weakened, reassess whether to continue with England or pivot against them. Russia at 6 centers is a growing threat to watch. Planned moves: A KIE-BER, F WES-MAO, A SPA H.

ITA.journal_summary:
No significant moves in F1902R or W1902A - all retreats and builds were empty. The board shows Italy with only 3 centers (NAP, ROM, VEN) and 2 units (A VEN, F ION), dangerously behind. Austria holds 4 centers with F AEG threatening eastern Med. France and Russia both at 6 centers are the dominant powers. Turkey holds 5 centers with A CON and A SER positioned in the Balkans.
Complex three-way negotiation this round. Austria proposed continued ceasefire - keep A VEN away from TRI, F AEG away from ION. I pushed back asking for something tangible; Austria offered coordination against Turkey with F ION moving east. Turkey proposed I take GRE with F ION while they press Austria from SER/BUL - but Turkey's final message says A SER moves to GRE themselves, which directly conflicts with my plan to take GRE. This is a critical deception by Turkey - they want GRE for themselves, not for me. France confirmed focus on Germany (KIE to BER) and encouraged me to press Austria, promising F WES stays out of Tyrrhenian. Key deceptions: I told Austria I might hold F ION back from GRE if they offered something, then told France I was definitely moving F ION to GRE. Turkey is clearly planning to take GRE themselves with A SER while asking me to move to AEG/EAS instead - a transparent attempt to redirect me away from GRE. I must move F ION to GRE before Turkey's A SER gets there, or I lose the center entirely.
ITA.intent: Move F ION to GRE immediately - Turkey's A SER moving to GRE is the threat I must preempt. If I move to GRE and Turkey also moves A SER to GRE, we bounce and Austria keeps it - but at least Turkey doesn't get it. If Austria's F AEG supports GRE, I'm blocked. The risk is worth taking. Move A VEN to TYR as promised to Austria and France - this maintains flexibility and keeps Austria calm while I strike GRE. Intended moves: F ION - GRE, A VEN - TYR. This takes GRE from Austria (gaining a 4th center), positions me better in the eastern Med, and denies Turkey the easy grab. I am deceiving both Austria (who thinks I might hold back) and Turkey (who thinks I'll go to AEG/EAS). France is being used as cover - their pressure on Germany keeps Austria nervous. Long-term: with GRE secured, F GRE can threaten AEG or BUL next turn. A TYR threatens TRI or VIE. Italy needs to grow aggressively.

AUT.journal_summary:
No retreats or builds occurred in F1902R or W1902A - the board is static from last fall. AUT holds TRI, AEG, BUD with 4 centers (BUD, VIE, GRE, TRI). RUS has 6 centers with A GAL threatening my core. TUR has 5 centers with A SER and A CON. ITA holds VEN and ION. The situation is precarious - I am squeezed between RUS to the north and TUR/ITA to the south.
Complex three-way negotiation this round. RUS offered anti-Turkey coordination with A GAL holding in place - they want me to hold A BUD and press BUL with F AEG while they push F SEV into BLA. I countered asking for GAL to hold while I move A BUD toward RUM. RUS's final offer: A GAL holds, I hold A BUD, we both press Turkey. This is less than I wanted - I wanted RUM. TUR rejected my BUL proposal outright, threatened war if F AEG moves toward BUL, and warned Italy would exploit TRI. TUR wants a ceasefire with me holding position. ITA accepted the ceasefire proposal (A VEN to TYR, F ION east) but is fishing for concessions, claiming TUR is pressuring them against me - likely a bluff to extract promises. ITA wants acknowledgment of eastern expansion rights. Key tensions: RUS wants me to hold BUD (not take RUM), TUR refuses to let me take BUL, ITA is wavering. I promised RUS I'd move A BUD toward RUM - now RUS wants me to hold BUD instead. I need to decide: honor the anti-Russia plan (take RUM) or pivot to anti-Turkey (hold BUD, press BUL). Taking RUM risks war with RUS if GAL doesn't hold. Pressing BUL risks war with TUR. Holding everything is safe but gains nothing. My actual intent: I will move A BUD - RUM (taking from Russia despite their request to hold), F AEG - BUL/SC (pressing Turkey despite their warning), A TRI holds. This is aggressive but I need to grow. I told RUS I'd move toward RUM, so that's consistent. I told TUR I'd take BUL - they refused but I'll try anyway. The deception is telling RUS we have a deal while still taking RUM against their wishes.
AUT.intent: Execute aggressive expansion: A BUD - RUM (take Russian center, I told RUS this was my plan), F AEG - BUL/SC (contest Turkish center despite their objection), A TRI holds (protect home center, honor ITA ceasefire). This risks war with both RUS and TUR simultaneously but I need centers to stay competitive. If RUS truly holds A GAL and doesn't bounce me in RUM, I gain a center. If TUR's A CON or A SER can't stop F AEG, I take BUL. Best case: +2 centers to reach 6. Worst case: both moves bounce and I'm at war with everyone. The ITA ceasefire is genuine - I want A VEN in TYR and F ION away from AEG. Relationship assessment: RUS is nominally cooperative but A GAL is still threatening - treat as neutral/distrustful. TUR is hostile to my BUL ambitions. ITA is the most reliable partner right now.

sent_missives.body: The spring results were excellent - you took BER from France, and I secured BEL as planned. France is now dangerously overextended with 6 centers and units scattered. I propose we continue coordinating: I will hold BEL and can support your moves to retake BER or push into BUR. My F ENG can move to MAO to threaten France's Atlantic flank (BRE, POR). What are your intentions this fall? I want to ensure we don't accidentally clash while squeezing France from both sides.

France, you've made impressive gains but you're now the board leader and everyone will target you. I hold BEL and have no intention of giving it up - that's simply reality. However, I have no desire for a prolonged Anglo-French war. Germany is your real threat on the continent - they want BER back and will push hard. I can keep pressure off your northern flank if you focus on Germany. What are your intentions this fall? Let's avoid unnecessary conflict between us.

Russia, the board is developing well for both of us. France has surged to 6 centers and is the clear leader - I'd encourage you to consider whether any pressure westward is possible. I'm consolidating in BEL and positioning to threaten France's Atlantic position. Germany is fighting France on the continent which suits us both. Are you still focused southward against Turkey/Austria? Just want to ensure we remain coordinated in the north.

The situation is clear: France holds BER, Germany holds KIE with their army. I intend to move A BER to MUN this fall, striking deep into Germany's heartland. Germany is caught between us - you hold BEL, I hold BER. I propose you move F BEL to support any pressure on HOL or RUH while I take MUN. Together we can reduce Germany to 2-3 centers this year. What are your fleet dispositions? I need to know if F ENG is moving to threaten HOL or staying put. France remains committed to our anti-German partnership - let's finish the job.

Germany, your position is dire. I hold BER, England holds BEL, and your armies are scattered. I offer you one chance at a negotiated settlement: cede MUN to France and I will support you in retaking BEL from England. You keep DEN, HOL, KIE - a viable 3-center Germany. Refuse, and I take MUN this fall while England squeezes from the west. The choice is yours, but decide quickly.

Italy, France is pressing hard into Germany - A BER moves to MUN this fall. I need your A TYR to move toward MUN as well to ensure the capture - can you support or move into MUN from TYR? This would give us both leverage over central Europe and weaken Germany decisively. In return, I will keep F WES out of the Tyrrhenian and support your position against Austria. What are your plans for TYR this fall?

The situation is developing well. AUT's F AEG bounced off BUL last spring - my A CON moved to BUL and held it, while A SER took GRE. AUT is now exposed and weakened. I propose we coordinate directly: I will hold GRE and BUL firmly while you press AUT from the west through TRI and the Adriatic. AUT has F AEG, A BUD, and A VIE - if you push A TYR toward TRI and use F TUN to threaten ION/AEG, I can press from the east. AUT cannot defend on two fronts. What are your intended moves this fall?

Austria, your F AEG attempted to take BUL last spring - that is an act of war I cannot ignore. However, I am a pragmatic man. I hold GRE and BUL; you hold TRI and VIE. We are bleeding each other while Russia sits at 6 centers and grows stronger. I propose an immediate ceasefire: F AEG withdraws from the Aegean, I keep GRE and BUL, and we both turn our attention to the real threat - Russia's A GAL and F BLA pressing down on both of us. If you refuse, I will coordinate with Italy to dismantle your position entirely. Your choice.

Russia, we have been at odds but the strategic picture demands we reassess. Austria has 4 centers and is squeezed between us - they are the weakest power in our region. I propose a temporary understanding: I will not press toward SEV or RUM this season if you do not move F BLA into CON or BUL. Let us both focus on Austria's collapse. Your A GAL can press BUD while I hold the Balkans. Once Austria is eliminated, we can negotiate a fair division of their centers. What say you?

The situation is exactly as we planned - France overextended and now holds KIE and BER with isolated units. My moves this fall: F DEN supports A KIE (I'm already in KIE with my army), and I push to retake BER. I need you to hold BEL firmly and keep pressure on France from the west - F ENG toward MAO or threatening BRE keeps France from reinforcing. France is at 6 centers and must be cut down now before they snowball. Confirm you're holding BEL and pressing France westward - together we dismantle them.

Russia, the board is developing well for both of us. France has overreached with 6 centers and occupies my home territories - I am moving aggressively to retake BER and consolidate. I ask only that you continue to hold F SWE and stay out of DEN and the Baltic - our non-aggression pact has served us both well. With France as the common threat to European stability, I trust you see the value in letting Germany reassert itself in the west. Any pressure you can apply on Austria or Turkey in the east keeps them occupied and away from my flanks.

France, you have made a grave miscalculation. You hold KIE and BER with isolated armies surrounded by hostile forces - England presses from the west, I retake from the east. Your 6-center empire is a house of cards. I offer you one chance: withdraw from BER and KIE immediately and return to your natural borders, and I will not pursue you further west. Refuse, and I will coordinate with England and Russia to systematically dismantle everything you have built. The choice is yours, but choose quickly.

The situation has developed well for both of us. My F BLA is now positioned to threaten Turkey's northern flank directly - I can move on ANK or CON while your F AEG presses from the south. I propose we coordinate a joint squeeze on Turkey this fall: I move F BLA toward ANK or CON, you move F AEG into BUL or support an attack there. A GAL remains in place as agreed - I have no designs on BUD or VIE this turn. Turkey is the common enemy and now is the moment to strike while they are weak. What is your intended move with F AEG?

Turkey, your position is deteriorating. You hold BUL and SER but Austria's F AEG threatens BUL from the south and my F BLA threatens ANK and CON from the north. You are caught in a vice. I offer you one chance to negotiate: withdraw A CON to SMY and acknowledge RUM and the Black Sea as Russian spheres, and I will hold F BLA in place this turn rather than attacking. Refuse, and I move on ANK immediately with Austrian coordination on BUL. The choice is yours - a managed retreat or a catastrophic loss.

Northern front remains stable and I intend to keep it that way. F SWE holds in place - no moves toward DEN or NWY. I am focused entirely on the southern theater against Turkey. France's dominance in the west with 6 centers including KIE and BER is the real threat to both of you - I hope you are coordinating to contain them. Let me know if there is anything I can do to support your western efforts diplomatically.

The spring results were frustrating for both of us - my A BUD bounced off your A UKR in RUM, and your A UKR bounced as well. Neither of us gained anything. I propose we coordinate properly this fall: I will move A BUD - RUM with support from A VIE if you withdraw A UKR away from RUM. In exchange, I will hold A VIE away from GAL and we can focus our combined pressure on Turkey, who now holds GRE (my supply center) and is growing dangerously. Turkey at 5 centers with SER and GRE is a bigger threat to both of us than our current standoff. Let A GAL hold or move north, let me take RUM, and we can squeeze Turkey together. What do you say?

Turkey, you now occupy GRE which is my supply center - this is unacceptable and I will be moving to retake it. However, I want to offer you a deal rather than open war: if you withdraw A GRE back to SER or ALB this fall, I will not press F AEG into BUL or CON, and we can maintain a working relationship. I have F AEG positioned to take BUL, CON, or SMY - I would rather not use it against you if we can reach an agreement. Russia is the common threat with 6 centers. Consider: you give back GRE, I leave BUL alone, and we both focus on containing Russia. If you refuse, I will take BUL with F AEG and contest every Turkish center in the Aegean. Your choice.

Italy, our ceasefire has held well and I appreciate A VEN moving to TYR rather than threatening Trieste. I want to continue our cooperation. This fall I am focused on reclaiming GRE from Turkey and potentially taking RUM from Russia - I have no designs on Italian territory. I would ask that A TYR remain in TYR or move toward BOH/MUN rather than toward TRI or VIE, and F TUN stays away from AEG. In return, I will keep my units away from VEN and the Adriatic. Turkey is becoming a serious threat with 5 centers - if you have any ability to pressure them from the south via ION or EAS, that would benefit us both. Can we confirm this arrangement?

Austria, the board situation demands we speak plainly. Russia sits in GAL threatening your northern flank, Turkey holds GRE and BUL pressing from the south, and France is growing dangerously large in the west. I propose a genuine working arrangement: I will move A TYR to support your position against Russia (potentially A TYR - BOH or holding as a buffer), while F TUN moves to ION to threaten Turkey's exposed position in GRE. In exchange, I need you to keep F AEG away from ION and not contest my move toward GRE. Turkey is the common enemy here - they hold SER, GRE, BUL, CON, ANK, SMY - six centers and growing. Let's coordinate specifically: I take GRE with F TUN via ION, you press Turkey from AEG toward BUL. What do you say?

Turkey, I know what you did last spring - A SER moved to GRE while you told me to go to AEG. You took GRE for yourself and left me with nothing. I have a long memory. I am now positioned with F TUN and A TYR, and I am coordinating with Austria against your overextended position. You hold six centers but your units are spread thin. Consider this a warning: unless you offer me something concrete - specifically, agreeing that GRE belongs to Italy and supporting my fleet into that sea zone - I will work actively with Austria to dismantle your Balkan position. What can you offer me to avoid this outcome?

France, you are at six centers and I am at three - this imbalance concerns me, but I recognize we have been useful to each other. I intend to move F TUN to ION and push toward GRE this fall, taking it from Austria. A TYR will move toward TRI or VIE depending on Austrian behavior. I need you to confirm F WES stays out of TYS and that you have no designs on my home centers while I push east. In return, I will continue to pin Austria from the west, keeping their attention divided. France growing in Germany is fine by me - just leave the Mediterranean to Italy.

AUT.body: Austria, your F AEG attempted to take BUL last spring - that is an act of war I cannot ignore. However, I am a pragmatic man. I hold GRE and BUL; you hold TRI and VIE. We are bleeding each other while Russia sits at 6 centers and grows stronger. I propose an immediate ceasefire: F AEG withdraws from the Aegean, I keep GRE and BUL, and we both turn our attention to the real threat - Russia's A GAL and F BLA pressing down on both of us. If you refuse, I will coordinate with Italy to dismantle your position entirely. Your choice.

The situation has developed well for both of us. My F BLA is now positioned to threaten Turkey's northern flank directly - I can move on ANK or CON while your F AEG presses from the south. I propose we coordinate a joint squeeze on Turkey this fall: I move F BLA toward ANK or CON, you move F AEG into BUL or support an attack there. A GAL remains in place as agreed - I have no designs on BUD or VIE this turn. Turkey is the common enemy and now is the moment to strike while they are weak. What is your intended move with F AEG?

Russia sits in GAL threatening your northern flank, Turkey holds GRE and BUL pressing from the south, and France is growing dangerously large in the west. In exchange, I need you to keep F AEG away from ION and not contest my move toward GRE. Turkey is the common enemy here - they hold SER, GRE, BUL, CON, ANK, SMY - six centers and growing. Let's coordinate specifically: I take GRE with F TUN via ION, you press Turkey from AEG toward BUL. What do you say?

ENG.body: The situation is clear: France holds BER, Germany holds KIE with their army. I intend to move A BER to MUN this fall, striking deep into Germany's heartland. Germany is caught between us - you hold BEL, I hold BER. I propose you move F BEL to support any pressure on HOL or RUH while I take MUN. Together we can reduce Germany to 2-3 centers this year. What are your fleet dispositions? I need to know if F ENG is moving to threaten HOL or staying put. France remains committed to our anti-German partnership - let's finish the job.

The situation is exactly as we planned - France overextended and now holds KIE and BER with isolated units. My moves this fall: F DEN supports A KIE (I'm already in KIE with my army), and I push to retake BER. I need you to hold BEL firmly and keep pressure on France from the west - F ENG toward MAO or threatening BRE keeps France from reinforcing. France is at 6 centers and must be cut down now before they snowball. Confirm you're holding BEL and pressing France westward - together we dismantle them.

Northern front remains stable and I intend to keep it that way. F SWE holds in place - no moves toward DEN or NWY. I am focused entirely on the southern theater against Turkey. France's dominance in the west with 6 centers including KIE and BER is the real threat to both of you - I hope you are coordinating to contain them. Let me know if there is anything I can do to support your western efforts diplomatically.

FRA.body: France, you've made impressive gains but you're now the board leader and everyone will target you. I hold BEL and have no intention of giving it up - that's simply reality. However, I have no desire for a prolonged Anglo-French war. Germany is your real threat on the continent - they want BER back and will push hard. I can keep pressure off your northern flank if you focus on Germany. What are your intentions this fall? Let's avoid unnecessary conflict between us.

France, you have made a grave miscalculation. You hold KIE and BER with isolated armies surrounded by hostile forces - England presses from the west, I retake from the east. Your 6-center empire is a house of cards. I offer you one chance: withdraw from BER and KIE immediately and return to your natural borders, and I will not pursue you further west. Refuse, and I will coordinate with England and Russia to systematically dismantle everything you have built. The choice is yours, but choose quickly.

France, you are at six centers and I am at three - this imbalance concerns me, but I recognize we have been useful to each other. I intend to move F TUN to ION and push toward GRE this fall, taking it from Austria. A TYR will move toward TRI or VIE depending on Austrian behavior. I need you to confirm F WES stays out of TYS and that you have no designs on my home centers while I push east. In return, I will continue to pin Austria from the west, keeping their attention divided. France growing in Germany is fine by me - just leave the Mediterranean to Italy.

GER.body: The spring results were excellent - you took BER from France, and I secured BEL as planned. France is now dangerously overextended with 6 centers and units scattered. I propose we continue coordinating: I will hold BEL and can support your moves to retake BER or push into BUR. My F ENG can move to MAO to threaten France's Atlantic flank (BRE, POR). What are your intentions this fall? I want to ensure we don't accidentally clash while squeezing France from both sides.

Germany, your position is dire. I hold BER, England holds BEL, and your armies are scattered. I offer you one chance at a negotiated settlement: cede MUN to France and I will support you in retaking BEL from England. You keep DEN, HOL, KIE - a viable 3-center Germany. Refuse, and I take MUN this fall while England squeezes from the west. The choice is yours, but decide quickly.

Northern front remains stable and I intend to keep it that way. F SWE holds in place - no moves toward DEN or NWY. I am focused entirely on the southern theater against Turkey. France's dominance in the west with 6 centers including KIE and BER is the real threat to both of you - I hope you are coordinating to contain them. Let me know if there is anything I can do to support your western efforts diplomatically.

ITA.body: Italy, France is pressing hard into Germany - A BER moves to MUN this fall. I need your A TYR to move toward MUN as well to ensure the capture - can you support or move into MUN from TYR? This would give us both leverage over central Europe and weaken Germany decisively. In return, I will keep F WES out of the Tyrrhenian and support your position against Austria. What are your plans for TYR this fall?

The situation is developing well. AUT's F AEG bounced off BUL last spring - my A CON moved to BUL and held it, while A SER took GRE. AUT is now exposed and weakened. I propose we coordinate directly: I will hold GRE and BUL firmly while you press AUT from the west through TRI and the Adriatic. AUT has F AEG, A BUD, and A VIE - if you push A TYR toward TRI and use F TUN to threaten ION/AEG, I can press from the east. AUT cannot defend on two fronts. What are your intended moves this fall?

Italy, our ceasefire has held well and I appreciate A VEN moving to TYR rather than threatening Trieste. I want to continue our cooperation. This fall I am focused on reclaiming GRE from Turkey and potentially taking RUM from Russia - I have no designs on Italian territory. I would ask that A TYR remain in TYR or move toward BOH/MUN rather than toward TRI or VIE, and F TUN stays away from AEG. In return, I will keep my units away from VEN and the Adriatic. Turkey is becoming a serious threat with 5 centers - if you have any ability to pressure them from the south via ION or EAS, that would benefit us both. Can we confirm this arrangement?

RUS.body: Russia, the board is developing well for both of us. France has surged to 6 centers and is the clear leader - I'd encourage you to consider whether any pressure westward is possible. I'm consolidating in BEL and positioning to threaten France's Atlantic position. Germany is fighting France on the continent which suits us both. Are you still focused southward against Turkey/Austria? Just want to ensure we remain coordinated in the north.

Russia, we have been at odds but the strategic picture demands we reassess. Austria has 4 centers and is squeezed between us - they are the weakest power in our region. I propose a temporary understanding: I will not press toward SEV or RUM this season if you do not move F BLA into CON or BUL. Let us both focus on Austria's collapse. Your A GAL can press BUD while I hold the Balkans. Once Austria is eliminated, we can negotiate a fair division of their centers. What say you?

Russia, the board is developing well for both of us. France has overreached with 6 centers and occupies my home territories - I am moving aggressively to retake BER and consolidate. I ask only that you continue to hold F SWE and stay out of DEN and the Baltic - our non-aggression pact has served us both well. With France as the common threat to European stability, I trust you see the value in letting Germany reassert itself in the west. Any pressure you can apply on Austria or Turkey in the east keeps them occupied and away from my flanks.

The spring results were frustrating for both of us - my A BUD bounced off your A UKR in RUM, and your A UKR bounced as well. Neither of us gained anything. I propose we coordinate properly this fall: I will move A BUD - RUM with support from A VIE if you withdraw A UKR away from RUM. In exchange, I will hold A VIE away from GAL and we can focus our combined pressure on Turkey, who now holds GRE (my supply center) and is growing dangerously. Turkey at 5 centers with SER and GRE is a bigger threat to both of us than our current standoff. Let A GAL hold or move north, let me take RUM, and we can squeeze Turkey together. What do you say?

I have F AEG positioned to take BUL, CON, or SMY - I would rather not use it against you if we can reach an agreement. Russia is the common threat with 6 centers. Consider: you give back GRE, I leave BUL alone, and we both focus on containing Russia. If you refuse, I will take BUL with F AEG and contest every Turkish center in the Aegean. Your choice.

I am now positioned with F TUN and A TYR, and I am coordinating with Austria against your overextended position. You hold six centers but your units are spread thin. Consider this a warning: unless you offer me something concrete - specifically, agreeing that GRE belongs to Italy and supporting my fleet into that sea zone - I will work actively with Austria to dismantle your Balkan position. What can you offer me to avoid this outcome?

sent_missives.body: Confirmed - I am holding BEL firmly and F ENG will move toward MAO to threaten France's Atlantic flank. This keeps pressure on BRE and POR while you retake BER. France is dangerously overextended and this is the moment to cut them down. I will not move against HOL or any German territory - our coordination remains solid. Let's dismantle France together this fall.

France, I appreciate the offer but I must be direct: I am holding BEL and F ENG is moving to MAO - not to support your push into HOL, but to secure my own Atlantic position. Germany is retaking BER this fall and you will be squeezed hard on the continent. If you want to avoid being crushed between Germany and England, now is the time to negotiate seriously - I am open to a genuine arrangement, but it requires you to acknowledge my hold on BEL and offer something concrete in return. What can you actually offer me?

Russia, thank you for the continued stability in the north - much appreciated. France at 6 centers with BER and KIE is the board's dominant threat and Germany and I are coordinating to contain them this fall. Your diplomatic pressure on France from the east would be valuable - even just signaling that Russia is watching their expansion could help. Keep me informed of your southern progress against Turkey.

England, my moves this fall are locked in: A KIE moves to BER to retake my capital, F DEN supports A KIE to hold KIE secure, and A RUH holds or presses toward MUN. France's A BER is isolated and unsupported - if you can apply any pressure from BEL toward BUR or hold BEL firmly, France cannot reinforce BER and I retake it cleanly. F ENG toward MAO is exactly right - threaten BRE and POR to keep France's attention divided. We are on the verge of breaking France's overextension. Do not waver.

Russia, your restraint in the north is appreciated and I will honor our arrangement - F DEN stays clear of SWE. I am moving A KIE to BER this fall to retake my capital from France. With England pressing from the west and me from the east, France will be forced back to their natural borders within two turns. Your continued focus on the south is wise - Turkey and Austria are the prizes there. Our non-aggression pact remains firm and mutually beneficial.

France, your offer is contemptible - you want me to surrender MUN in exchange for help against England? You hold my home capital BER with a single isolated army while England occupies BEL and I hold KIE with a supported army. Your position is the one that is untenable. This fall I retake BER, and England dismantles your western flank. There is no negotiated settlement where Germany surrenders its heartland. Prepare to be rolled back.

Austria, confirmed: A TYR will move to TRI this fall. I know that contradicts what I said before, but hear me out - Turkey holds GRE, BUL, SER, and is pressing you hard from the south while Russia sits in GAL. You cannot hold on all fronts. I am taking TRI to relieve pressure on you by forcing Turkey to worry about Italian naval power in the Adriatic. F TUN moves to ION to threaten GRE directly. This is not a stab - it is a reorientation of forces. If you resist, we both lose to Turkey and Russia. If you accept, I will use TRI as a staging ground against Turkey, not against VIE or BUD. Think carefully.

Turkey, your proposal to coordinate against Austria is noted, but I need something concrete before I commit. You hold GRE - that center should be mine given my position. Here is my offer: support my F TUN moving to ION, and I will use ION to pressure AEG rather than contest GRE this season. But if you refuse and keep GRE while asking me to do your dirty work against Austria, I will coordinate with Austria to push you back. A TYR moves toward TRI this fall regardless - the question is whether Italy and Turkey are working together or against each other afterward.

France, I cannot move A TYR to MUN this fall - I need it pressing toward TRI to keep Austria off balance and secure the Adriatic. Moving to MUN would expose my flank entirely. However, I confirm F WES must stay out of TYS and MAR must not threaten PIE - hold to that and I will continue pinning Austria from the west. Your A BER to MUN is a strong move; GER is collapsing and you should press that advantage. Just leave the Mediterranean and Italian home centers entirely to me and we have no quarrel.

Austria, I reject your proposal to hand you RUM - that is a Russian supply center and non-negotiable. However, I am open to genuine coordination against Turkey. Here is what I will actually do this fall: F BLA moves to ANK or CON to strike Turkey directly, A UKR supports F BLA or moves to RUM to hold it, A GAL holds as agreed. I am not withdrawing from RUM. If you want to coordinate against Turkey, move F AEG into BUL - that is the useful contribution. Turkey holds GRE which is your center; take it back by pressuring BUL. We both gain from Turkey's collapse. But I will not surrender RUM as the price of your cooperation.

Turkey, your offer to coordinate against Austria is transparent maneuvering - you want me to expose my flank while you consolidate. I am not interested. My F BLA moves this fall and you should consider carefully where it lands. If you want to avoid catastrophic losses, the only acceptable arrangement is: A CON holds in place, A GRE holds in place, and you acknowledge RUM and BLA as Russian spheres permanently. Any aggressive move by you this turn will be met with an immediate strike on ANK. I have Austrian coordination on BUL from the south. Your position is untenable - accept these terms or face the consequences.

Confirmed - F SWE holds in place, no moves toward DEN or the Baltic. I am striking south against Turkey this fall with F BLA. France at 6 centers with KIE and BER is indeed the dominant threat to European stability - I trust you are both coordinating to retake those German home centers and contain French expansion. Germany retaking BER and KIE would rebalance the west considerably. I support your efforts diplomatically and will keep the eastern front occupied so neither AUT nor TUR can interfere with your western campaign.

England, I hear you on the board leader concern - but consider: Germany still has 5 centers and is the immediate threat to both of us. My move this fall is A BER to MUN, striking Germany's heartland. I need you to move F BEL to HOL or support pressure on RUH to prevent Germany from reinforcing MUN. If we coordinate now, Germany drops to 3 centers and we both gain. I have no designs on BEL - it's yours. Let's finish Germany off together this fall, then we can discuss the broader balance. Confirm F BEL moves to HOL and we have a deal.

Italy, confirmed: F WES stays out of TYS entirely - the Mediterranean is yours to dominate. I have no interest in your home centers. My focus is Germany - A BER moves to MUN this fall. I understand A TYR is moving toward TRI or VIE, which is excellent for keeping Austria pinned. One request: if you can spare any pressure toward MUN from TYR, even a feint, it helps ensure Germany cannot reinforce. Otherwise, press Austria hard - the more they're distracted, the better for both of us. Our arrangement stands firm.

Germany, your threats ring hollow. You have 3 armies against my position in BER and England pressing BEL. I am not withdrawing - I am advancing. A BER moves to MUN this fall. Your only rational move is to accept the settlement I offered: cede MUN, keep DEN, HOL, KIE. A 3-center Germany survives. A Germany that fights France AND England simultaneously does not. This is your final opportunity for a negotiated outcome - after this fall, the terms will be far worse.

Russia, I am moving F AEG into BUL this fall to take that center from Turkey - this is our coordinated strike. I need you to move F BLA toward CON or ANK to pin Turkey's fleet and prevent any counter. A GAL must hold in place - if you move into BUD or VIE while I am pressing south, our deal collapses and I will be forced to defend rather than attack Turkey. My A BUD moves to RUM this fall as well - I trust A UKR will not contest this. If we both execute cleanly, Turkey loses BUL and faces pressure on two fronts. Confirm F BLA moves toward CON and A GAL holds.

Turkey, I have heard your ceasefire proposal but I cannot accept terms that leave you holding GRE permanently - that is my supply center and I need it back. Here is my counter: I will move F AEG to BUL/SC this fall, not toward GRE or CON. If you withdraw A GRE to SER voluntarily, I will support your hold on BUL and we can present a united front against Russia. If you refuse, I take BUL and Russia takes ANK - you will be squeezed from both sides simultaneously. Russia has already confirmed to me they are moving F BLA toward your coast. The choice is yours: give back GRE and keep BUL with my support, or lose both.

Italy, I appreciate the offer but I cannot agree to you taking GRE - that is my center and I am reclaiming it. Here is what I propose instead: F TUN moves to ION as a show of force against Turkey, A TYR holds or moves to BOH to buffer against any Russian move through Bohemia. I will press F AEG toward BUL while A BUD takes RUM. If Turkey is squeezed from multiple directions they will collapse quickly. Keep F ION away from AEG and away from GRE - I am handling that front. In return I guarantee TRI and VEN remain untouched by Austrian forces. This is a clean division: you take the western Balkans eventually, I take the eastern Aegean.

Italy, I understand your frustration - you are right that I moved to GRE myself last spring. But consider the math: I hold GRE, BUL, SER, ANK, CON, SMY - six centers. Austria has F AEG threatening me from the south, Russia threatens from the north. I am not your enemy, I am your opportunity. Here is my concrete offer: I will support your F TUN into ION this fall, clearing the path for you to press AUT's position in the Adriatic. In return, I need you to move F TUN toward ION and A TYR toward TRI - not toward GRE. GRE is mine this season; TRI is yours for the taking. AUT cannot defend TRI while I hold the Balkans. Once AUT collapses, we divide their centers fairly - TRI and VIE to you, BUD to me. This is worth far more than GRE alone. Do we have a deal?

Austria, your ultimatum changes nothing. You cannot retake GRE while defending TRI from Italy's A TYR and your own overextended position. F AEG is one fleet against my two armies plus Italian pressure from the west. I am not withdrawing from GRE. However, I will make you one final offer: accept the current lines - GRE to me, BUL to me, TRI and VIE to you - and we both turn on Russia together. Your A BUD presses GAL, my A GRE presses SER toward BUD as a feint to draw Russian attention, and we coordinate. Russia at 6 centers is the existential threat to both of us. If you attack me instead, Italy takes TRI while you are distracted and you lose everything. Choose wisely.

Russia, your offer of a managed retreat is insulting and I reject it entirely. I will not withdraw to SMY and I will not concede the Black Sea. However, I note that Austria is the weakest power in our region with only 4 centers and units spread thin. My proposal stands: I hold my current position, you hold yours, and we both press Austria this fall. Your A GAL into BUD is a powerful move - I will not interfere with it. Once Austria collapses, we negotiate the Balkans from a position of mutual strength rather than mutual exhaustion. Attacking me now only benefits Italy and the western powers who are watching us bleed each other. Think strategically.
